## PAGE 00001: 2024x Refresh1 Version News

- page_id: `189146427`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189146427/2024x+Refresh1+Version+News
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-04-29T15:09:29.981+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### SysML Plugin

Released on: July 5, 2024

SysML Plugin 2024x Refresh1 release introduces several improvements to Item Flow management. You can now navigate from the selected Item Flow in the Containment tree to the diagrams where the Item Flow is realized; specify Conveyed Items from the Diagram Palette for Item Flows directly in the diagram;hide thearrow notation on the realizing elements when the Conveyed Item is hidden or unspecified; easily identifyConveyed Items in the 'Create / Edit Item Flow'dialog as they are now represented with icons. Additionally, Item Flow creation between Activity Parameter Nodes and Actions has been enhanced, as nowthe tool checks action allocations to typed parts for Activity Parameter Nodes,displaying the suggested sender and receiver elements inthe Direction field of the 'Create / Edit Item Flow'dialog based on the element typing the parts to which actions are allocated. Two newvalidation ruleshave been implemented to highlight Item Flows that do not have any Conveyed Items. The Item Flow management experience has been further enhanced with the introduction of the 'Preview Affected Diagrams'dialog, which presents all affected diagrams, marking the affected elements in the diagram preview pane to convey where the created Item Flows will be realized.

Furthermore, the 2024x Refresh1 release contains new validation solvers for the 'Invalid Connector' validation rule, allowing for an easy connector redefinition.Finally, the release introduces the MagicGrid perspective, simplifying the modeling tool by providing only the MagicGrid-required subset of SysML elements and diagrams, making it easier to understand and navigate for users new to MagicGrid.

To download the latest version of the modeling tool, see[Downloading installation files](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/IL2024xR2/Downloading+installation+files).

****

- 
- 

- 
- 

#### Affected Diagrams Preview Dialog

To enhance the Item Flow creation and management experience, we have introduced the**Preview Affected Diagrams**dialog. This dialog conveniently presents all affected diagrams, allowing you to preview them individually. Furthermore, the affected elements are prominently marked in the diagram preview pane conveying where the created Item Flows will be realized.

During the preview process, you can select the **Update Diagram** check box for diagrams in which you wish to display the Conveyed Items of the selected flows.

The new[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='']**Show Item Flows on Affected Diagrams**controls how the diagrams will be updated for newly created or modified flows. Set the option to *true*to update all affected diagrams and show the Conveyed Items of flows whenever a new flow is created or an existing one is modified.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Preview Affected Diagrams dialog' space='']>]]>

#### Item Flows Management

- Interactions can be modeled across various diagrams in both behavior and structure models, creating difficulties in locating diagrams representing Item Flow realizations and hindering transparency in their identification. Now you can easily navigate from the selected Item Flow in the Containment tree to the diagrams where the Item Flow is realized. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Item Flow' space='']>]]> 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Specify the Conveyed Item directly in the diagram, picking the needed Conveyed Item element from the diagram palette and dragging it on to the Item Flow. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Item Flow' space='']>]]> [IMAGE alt='' src='']
- You can now choose whether you want to hide the arrow notation on the realizing elements when the Conveyed Item is hidden or unspecified in case you would need to conceal such approximate interactions, and vice versa . The **Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information Is Missing** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space=''] controls the arrow visibility. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Item Flow' space='']>]]> 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Now the Conveyed Items in the Create / Edit Item Flow dialog are represented with icons to help you identify the Conveyed Items. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Item Flow creation between Activity Parameter Nodes and Actions has been enhanced to facilitate the creation of Item Flows when interactions are modeled between Part Properties. Typically, the 'Usage' allocation mode is used for this, which does not create an allocation relation on the end of the Activity Parameter Node, thereby not providing a possible sender or receiver on this end. Now, the tool checks action allocations to typed parts for Activity Parameter Nodes. Consequently, the Direction field in the **Create / Edit Item Flow** dialog displays suggested sender and receiver elements based on the element typing the parts to which actions are allocated. [ATTACHMENT filename='Item Flow between Activity Parameter Node and Action.png'] On the end of the*input : Audio data*Activity Parameter Node there are no Actions, only the*Read and transmit the sound*Activity. Thus, the tool checks the model to find Actions that invoke this Activity, locating the*: Read and transmit the sound* Action in the *Listen the audio* Activity diagram. 
Then the tool checks the allocation relationships for this action and an allocation to the *: Wireless Earbuds* Part Property is found. 
Using this information the **Create/Edit Item Flow** dialog identifies the *Wireless Earbuds* Block as a possible sender of the flow, displaying*Wireless Earbuds*and *Processing*Blocks as a suggestion for the sender/receiver in the **Direction**field of the dialog.
- New validation rules( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Item Flow has no Conveyed Items' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Item Flow has no Conveyed Items' space=''] ) have been implemented to highlight Item Flows that do not have any Conveyed Items. This helps to declutter the model and identify potentially unused flows. Learn more on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Item Flow has no Conveyed Items' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Realized Item Flow has no Conveyed Items' space=''] pages.

#### Connector Redefinition

New validation solvers are available for the **Invalid Connector** validation rule, allowing for an easy connector redefinition:

- Use the **Redefine and Replace Connector** solver to have a new redefining connector created if the selected connector is inherited and at least one of its ends is redefined.
- Use the **Replace with Redefining** solver to replace the failed connector with the redefining one if the failed connector is already redefined in the same context.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Invalid Connector (UML Correctness)' space='']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The image displays how to resolve the **Invalid Connector**validation error via the **Redefine and Replace Connector**solver.****

#### MagicGrid Perspective

The MagicGrid perspective has been introduced, supplementing existing perspectives, such as Full Featured or System Engineer. This perspective simplifies the modeling tool by providing only the MagicGrid-required subset of SysML elements and diagrams, making it easier to understand and navigate for users new to MagicGrid. You can manually switch perspectives via the **Select Perspective** dialog. Furthermore, a dialog prompting you to switch to this perspective appears whenever you create a MagicGrid project.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting perspective' space='']>]]>

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The MagicGrid perspective available via the **Select Perspective** dialog.

**Documentation**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Plugin Documentation' space='SYSMLP2024xR1']

**News of earlier versions**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2024x Version News' space='SYSMLP2024x'] [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2022x Version News' space='SYSMLP2022x']

## PAGE 00002: 2024x Refresh2 Version News

- page_id: `189146417`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`

- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### SysML Plugin

Released on: November 8, 2024

The SysML Plugin 2024x Refresh2 release introduces anew 'Properties to Ignore for Status Update'project option. This new project option allows you to specify the properties that, when modified, will not change the elements' status to 'updated' when importing requirements from ReqIF files.

To download the latest version of the modeling tool, see[Downloading installation files](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/IL2024xR2/Downloading+installation+files).

#### Improvements

- A new Properties to Ignore for Status Update project option has been introduced that allows you to specify those properties that, when modified, will not change the elements' status to 'updated' when importing requirements from ReqIF files. It enables more efficient requirement management by preventing unnecessary status updates when specified properties are modified during ReqIF imports. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space='']>]]>

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Plugin Documentation' space='']

## PAGE 00003: 2024x Version News

- page_id: `189146437`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189146437/2024x+Version+News
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-05-22T07:33:56.771+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

###### SysML Plugin

Released on: November 10, 2023

SysML Plugin 2024x introduces several improvements to Item Flow creation and editing. The **Create / Edit Item Flow**dialog now provides greater clarity and usability, providing a more focused approach to reviewing Activity Pairs connected with existing Activity Edges. In addition, **Autowire Parts** and **Delegate Port(s)** commands' availability is changed in the SysML Internal Block Diagram and the SysML Parametric Diagram. Also, the **Delegate Port(s)** command functionality regarding nested ports is now improved to treat each displayed nested port individually.

To download the latest version of the modeling tool, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Downloading installation files' space='IL2024x'].

#### Improved Item Flow Creation and EditingImproved Item Flow Creation and Editing

SysML Plugin 2024x brings a more focused approach to reviewing Activity Pairs connected with existing Activity Edges in the**Create / Edit Item Flow**dialog, improving overall usability and clarity.

- Now, the second step centers exclusively on reviewing existing Activity Pairs connected by existing Activity Edges, thus:
  - New Activity Pairs cannot be created, and existing Activity Pairs cannot be edited. Because of this, the tool will no longer initiate the automatic generation of new elements or diagrams.
  - The overall synchronization of the dialog's steps is now improved to ensure that all relevant Activity Pairs are systematically gathered based on specified preferences. This results in a well-organized diagram list in the third step, accurately representing selected options.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A comparison of some of the changes in the second step of the **Create / Edit Item Flow** dialog between versions 2024x and 2022x Refresh2.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Item Flow' space='']>]]>

#### Other ImprovementsOther Improvements

- The Tools option group containing Autowire Parts and Delegate Port(s) commands has been removed from the SysML Parametric Diagram toolbar. Both Delegate Port(s) and Autowire Parts commands are no longer available for Constraint Properties and Constraint Parameters in the SysML Internal Block Diagram and the SysML Parametric Diagram.
- Improved Delegate Port(s) command functionality regarding nested ports. Now if the command is called from a part or a port enclosing a nested ports structure, each displayed nested port is treated individually to create delegated connectors and ports. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Automatic delegation' space='']>]]>

**Documentation**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Plugin Documentation' space='SYSMLP2024x']

**News of earlier versions**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2022x Refresh2 Version News' space='SYSMLP2022xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2022x Refresh1 Version News' space='SYSMLP2022xR1']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='2022x Version News' space='SYSMLP2022x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h5 style="text-align: center;">SysML Plugin</h5><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(128,128,128);">Released on: November 10, 2023</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">SysML Plugin 2024x introduces several improvements to Item Flow creation and editing. The<span> </span> <strong>Create / Edit Item Flow<span> </span> </strong>dialog now provides greater clarity and usability, providing a more focused approach to reviewing Activity Pairs connected with existing Activity Edges. In addition, <strong style="text-align: left;">Autowire Parts</strong> <span> </span>and<span> </span> <strong style="text-align: left;">Delegate Port(s)</strong> <span> </span>commands' availability is changed in the SysML Internal Block Diagram and the SysML Parametric Diagram. Also, the<span> </span> <strong style="text-align: left;">Delegate Port(s)</strong> <span> </span>command functionality regarding nested ports is now improved to treat each displayed nested port individually.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">To download the latest version of the modeling tool, see<span> </span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL2024x" ri:content-title="Downloading installation files" /></ac:link>.</span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><hr /></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><ac:link ac:anchor="Improved Item Flow Creation and Editing" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><ac:link ac:anchor="Other Improvements" />  </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><hr /></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f513c0f4-8ec4-4734-9196-44187491e529"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Improved Item Flow Creation and Editing</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Improved Item Flow Creation and Editing</h2><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">SysML Plugin 2024x brings a more focused approach to reviewing Activity Pairs connected with existing Activity Edges in the </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Create / Edit Item Flow </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">dialog, improving overall usability and clarity.</span></p><ul><li><span>Now, the second step centers exclusively on reviewing existing Activity Pairs connected by existing Activity Edges, thus:</span><ul><li><span>New Activity Pairs cannot be created, and existing Activity Pairs cannot be edited. Because of this, the tool will no longer initiate the automatic generation of new elements or diagrams. </span></li><li>The overall synchronization of the dialog's steps is now improved to ensure that all relevant Activity Pairs are systematically gathered based on specified preferences. This results in <span>a well-organized diagram list in the third step, accurately representing selected options.</span></li></ul></li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Improved_Item_Flow.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A comparison of some of the changes in the second step of the <strong>Create / Edit Item Flow</strong> dialog between versions 2024x and 2022x Refresh2.</h6><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Item Flow" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about Item Flow creation and editing >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </p><h2><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9c001aa7-667d-4750-b4db-cc7e6ffc4392"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Other Improvements</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Other Improvements</h2><ul><li>The <strong>Tools</strong> option group containing <strong>Autowire Parts</strong> and <strong>Delegate Port(s)</strong> commands has been removed from the SysML Parametric Diagram toolbar. Both <strong>Delegate Port(s)</strong> and <strong>Autowire Parts</strong> commands are no longer available for Constraint Properties and Constraint Parameters in the SysML Internal Block Diagram and the SysML Parametric Diagram.</li><li>Improved <strong>Delegate Port(s)</strong> command functionality regarding nested ports. Now if the command is called from a part or a port enclosing a nested ports structure, each displayed nested port is treated individually to create delegated connectors and ports. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Automatic delegation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about automatic delegation >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><hr /></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Documentation</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024x" ri:content-title="SysML Plugin Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Plugin 2024x]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>News of earlier versions</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2022xR2" ri:content-title="2022x Refresh2 Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Plugin 2022x Refresh2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2022xR1" ri:content-title="2022x Refresh1 Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Plugin 2022x Refresh1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2022x" ri:content-title="2022x Version News" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Plugin 2022x ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189147615 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00008: Accept Change Structural Feature Event Action

- page_id: `189147615`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189147615/Accept+Change+Structural+Feature+Event+Action
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T14:46:14.727+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Glossary of SysML concepts > Element descriptions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Accept Change Structural Feature Event Action is an action that waits for the occurrence of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Change Structural Feature Event' space=''] (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Directed Feature' space='']). The actions have exactly two output pins. The first output pin holds the values of the structural feature just after the values changed, while the second pin holds the values just before the values changed. The action only accepts events for structural features on the blocks owning the behavior containing the Action, or on the behavior itself, if the behavior is not owned by a Block.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Accept Event Action' space='MD2024xR2']

#### PANEL: Related diagrams

Related diagrams

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Activity Diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>An Accept Change Structural Feature Event Action is an action that waits for the occurrence of a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Change Structural Feature Event" /></ac:link> (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Directed Feature" /></ac:link>). The actions have exactly two output pins. The first output pin holds the values of the structural feature just after the values changed, while the second pin holds the values just before the values changed. The action only accepts events for structural features on the blocks owning the behavior containing the Action, or on the behavior itself, if the behavior is not owned by a Block.</p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="173ae66c-4bd7-448c-acbb-6872cb097815"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Accept Event Action" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cce34faa-2f55-4678-b24f-7f27f024c038"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related diagrams</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Activity Diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189147422 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00009: Action Behavior is not allocated

- page_id: `189147422`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189147422/Action+Behavior+is+not+allocated
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-02-11T09:51:26.025+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Validation > SysML validation rules > Validation rules of Allocations
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

ActionBehavior

**Description**

This rule checks to see if the action or behavior is allocated to the Allocated Activity Partition when the swimlane's**Allocation Mode**is**Usage**.

**Severity**

warning****

**Constrained Element**

Action****

**Solvers**

**Allocate Action**- sets the same element in the **Allocated To** property value as defined in the Allocated Activity Partition.

**Example**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### *Receive the regulator request* Action is allocated to *tr* Part Property.****

****

****

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p>ActionBehavior</p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>This rule checks to see if the action or behavior is allocated to the Allocated Activity Partition when the <span>swimlane's </span><strong>Allocation Mode</strong><span> is </span><strong>Usage</strong><span>.</span></p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p>warning<strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p>Action<strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><p><strong>Allocate Action </strong>- sets the same element in the <strong>Allocated To</strong> property value as defined in the Allocated Activity Partition.</p><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p><strong><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="behavior_is_not_allocated.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Action Behavior is not allocated" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><em>Receive the regulator request</em> Action is allocated to <em>tr</em> Part Property.<strong><br /></strong></h6><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00012: Actuator

- page_id: `189147616`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189147616/Actuator
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2016-04-08T08:50:37.664+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Glossary of SysML concepts > Element descriptions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Actuator is a special external system that influences the environment of the system under development.

#### TIP: Example

Example

- Heater assembly.
- Central locking system of a car.

#### PANEL: Related diagrams

Related diagrams

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Use Case Diagram' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189147493 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00015: AdjunctProperty

- page_id: `189147493`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189147493/AdjunctProperty
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-01-13T12:13:13.474+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Validation > SysML validation rules > Validation rules of Non-normative Extensions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

ADJUNCT_SYNC

**Description**

This validation rule checks if property to which «adjunct»stereotype is applied meet the following constraints:

- Has the same name as the principal.
- Is owned by an element that owns the principal.
- If the principal is a Connector or CallAction, the Adjunct Property have to be composite.

**Severity**

warning****

**Constrained****element**

Property

**Solvers**

- Synchronize - synchronizes the Adjunct property to match its type, multiplicity, aggregation and NamdElement name.
- Set Principal - opens the Select Principal dialog to choose the principal for the Adjunct Property.

**Example**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### The Adjunct Property name is the same as principal's.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p>ADJUNCT_SYNC</p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>This validation rule checks if property to which <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);letter-spacing: 0.0px;">«</span>adjunct<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);letter-spacing: 0.0px;">» </span>stereotype is applied meet the following constraints:</p><ul><li>Has the same name as the principal.</li><li>Is owned by an element that owns the principal.</li><li>If the principal is a Connector or CallAction, the Adjunct Property have to be composite.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p>warning<strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Constrained</strong><strong> element</strong></p><p>Property</p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><ul><li><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Synchronize</strong> - synchronizes the Adjunct property to match its type, multiplicity, aggregation and NamdElement name. </li><li><strong>Set Principal</strong> - opens the <strong>Select Principal</strong> dialog to choose the principal for the Adjunct Property.</li></ul><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p><strong><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="adjunctproperty.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="AdjunctProperty" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Adjunct Property name is the same as principal's.</h6>
````

## PAGE 00017: Allocation Mode

- page_id: `189147050`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`

949204615

When creating a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Allocation Matrix' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] or new swimlane in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Activity Diagram' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'], you can select the kind**Definition**or**Usage** allocation mode. The definition elements areActivity and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']; usage elements are Action and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'].

When we talk about the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Allocation Matrix' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'], the rows in the matrix represent either the Activity or Action that are the clients of the Allocate relationship. Columnsin the matrixrepresent either the Block or Part Property that are the suppliers of the Allocate relationship.

Creating a SysML Allocation Matrix in the [SysML projects](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/SYSMLP2024xR2/Creating+SysML+projects#CreatingSysMLprojects-CreatingtheblankSysMLproject) for the first time, the**Create SysML Allocation Matrix**dialogappears, shown in the figure below. The usage allocation matrix is created by defaultwhen creating a new SysML Allocation Matrix in the [MagicGrid project](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/SYSMLP2024xR2/Creating+SysML+projects#CreatingSysMLprojects-CreatingMagicGridprojects).You can change that by specifying the **Allocation Mode** option value in the **Project Options**dialog.>]]>[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Allocation Mode' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']

The **Allocation Mode** project option is global and will be applied for the SysML Allocation matrix and SysML Activity diagram (swimlanes).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The****Create SysML Allocation Matrixdialog where you can select the Definition or Usage mode.

The choice you made will be remembered and applied for the following matrices you will create in the project. The same applies to the swimlanes.

When you create a swimlane for the first time, the **Create Swimlane** dialog opens. In the dialog, you can choose one of the allocation modes and the choice will be remembered. The dialog will not be displayed if the Allocation Mode is already chosen when creating a SysML Allocation matrix.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Create Swimlane dialog where you can select the Definition or Usage mode.

The examples of Usage and Definition modes are illustrated below. Figures are created by using the[distiller model.mdzip](https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/9919520/distiller%20model.mdzip?version=1&modificationDate=1505483436734&api=v2)sample model that comes only with the[SysML Plugin](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/SYSMLP2024xR2/SysML+Plugin+Documentation).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The definition and usage modes in the SysML Allocation matrices.

##### Changing the Allocation Mode

Changing the Allocation Mode in the Project Options dialog applies the change for the SysML Allocation matrix and swimlanes.

change_modeTo change the default Allocation Mode

1. From the main menu, select **Options** > **Project**.
2. In the**Project Options**dialog, expand the **General** node and select **SysML**.
3. Select the**Allocation Mode**option value: 
 - Definition (Activity allocated to Block) 
 - Usage (Action allocated to Part) 
 - Ask and Remember
4. Click **OK**. The defaultAllocation Mode is selected and will be applied each time you create a new SysML Allocation matrix or new swimlanes in the SysML Activity diagram.

949204612

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Allocation Matrix' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Activity Diagram' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189147732 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00019: Applying OMG SysML Style

- page_id: `189147732`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189147732/Applying+OMG+SysML+Style
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-02-01T10:19:20.715+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Customizations > Setting project options
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can apply the visual style of the OMG SysML Specifications (OMG SysML style) to your SysML model.

To apply the OMG SysML style to a SysML project

1. In the main menu, select Options > Project .
2. In the **Project Options** dialog, choose the **Symbol styles** on the left and select the **OMG SysML style** on the right. If you do not see the **OMG SysML style**a. Click the **Import** button. The **Open** dialog opens.b. Open the ***<md.install.dir>/templates/SysML directory*** and select **OMG SysML style.stl**.c. Click **Open**.
3. Click the Make Default button.
4. Click OK . Your SysML project uses OMG SysML style as a default style.

To apply OMG SysML style to a SysML diagram

1. In the main menu, select Options > Project .
2. In the **Project Options** dialog, choose the **Symbol styles** on the left and select the **OMG SysML style** on the right.
3. Click the **Apply** button.
4. In the **Select Diagrams** dialog, select a SysML diagram(s) and click **OK**.
5. Click **OK**in the **Project Options** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Applying OMG SysML style to existing SysML diagrams might distort the diagrams. Use the automated layout features that optimize diagram layout for viewing.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Click the <strong>Import</strong> button. The <strong>Open</strong> dialog opens.</p><p>b. Open the <strong><em>&lt;md.install.dir&gt;/templates/SysML directory</em></strong> and select <strong>OMG SysML style.stl</strong>.</p><p>c. Click <strong>Open</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click the <strong>Make Default</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />Your SysML project uses OMG SysML style as a default style.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To apply OMG SysML style to a SysML diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li><p>In the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, choose the <strong>Symbol styles</strong> on the left and select the <strong>OMG SysML style</strong> on the right.</p></li><li><p>Click the <strong>Apply</strong> button.</p></li><li><p>In the <strong>Select Diagrams</strong> dialog, select a SysML diagram(s) and click <strong>OK</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>OK </strong>in the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog.</p></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:title="Project Options dialog" ac:alt="Project Options dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Project Options_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying OMG SysML Style" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4e5ea953-dd75-4fc0-bf36-8b09cac3607e"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Applying OMG SysML style to existing SysML diagrams might distort the diagrams. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189146842 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00020: Applying Rollup Pattern Blocks

- page_id: `189146842`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189146842/Applying+Rollup+Pattern+Blocks
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T14:46:03.939+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Modeling structure with Blocks > Rollup Pattern Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Rollup pattern Blocks

1348753929

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Rollup pattern Blocks

1348753931

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Rollup pattern Blocks

1348753930

When trying to calculate total mass, cost, power, or another system dimension based on individual values of all the parts in the model,you need to apply a pattern Block to any number of[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='MD2024xR2']by using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Rollup Pattern Wizard' space=''].It automatically applies pattern Blocks (using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization' space='MD2024xR2']) recursively, and creates property values.The procedure below describes how to apply the rollup pattern Block.

To apply the rollup pattern Block

1. Right-click the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='MD2024xR2'] to which you want to apply the rollup pattern Block.
2. S elect **Tools** > **Apply Rollup Pattern**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Rollup Pattern Wizard' space='']**, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and select the pattern Block you want to apply. Rollup pattern BlocksYou can select: 
- The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Default Rollup Pattern Blocks' space='']: CostRollupPattern, MassRollupPattern, and PowerRollupPattern. They are created to calculate the total cost, mass, and power of the model elements. 
- Your own created rollup pattern Blocks. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new Rollup Pattern Block' space='']]]>
4. Specify options: 
- **Apply Recursively**to apply the pattern Block recursively. 
- **Set Role Name** to set the role names of Part Properties. 
- **Set Subsetted Properties** to set the Subsetted Properties of the Part Properties. 
- **Create Value Properties and Redefine** to create and redefine Value Properties.
5. Click **OK**. 
The pattern Block is applied to the model elements according to selected options. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

1348753928

**Related pages**

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Rollup Pattern Wizard' space='']

**Webinar**

- Total Mass, Cost, and Power Rollups

**Sample model**

The sample model used in the figures of this page is the **Laptop Cost Analysis** that comes with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation' space='CST2024xR2']. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download *[ATTACHMENT filename='LaptopCostAnalysis.mdzip'].*
- Find in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\simulation\LaptopCostAnalysis.mdzip

Block" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to create new rollup pattern Block >]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Specify options:<br />- <strong>Apply Recursively </strong>to apply the pattern Block recursively.<br />- <strong>Set Role Name</strong> to set the role names of Part Properties.<br />- <strong>Set Subsetted Properties</strong> to set the Subsetted Properties of the Part Properties.<br />- <strong>Create Value Properties and Redefine</strong> to create and redefine Value Properties.<br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />The pattern Block is applied to the model elements according to selected options.</span><span style="color: rgb(0,51,102);"> <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="after_applying_cost_pattern.png" /></ac:image><br /></span><span style="color: rgb(0,51,102);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(0,51,102);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br /></span></span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5eea87a7-30a1-4cc8-9b8f-cbc89ec25bd2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1348753928</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="ecd1a963-e394-473d-9969-435c40cc1bf4"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Rollup Pattern Wizard" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Webinar</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tBGgn_BAqTw&amp;ab_channel=CATIAMBSE">Total Mass, Cost, and Power Rollups</a></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The sample model used in the figures of this page is the <strong>Laptop Cost Analysis</strong> that comes with <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024xR2" ri:content-title="Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cameo Simulation Toolkit Plugin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. To open this sample do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Download<span class="confluence-link"><em> <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="LaptopCostAnalysis.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="_sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.<br /></em></span></li><li>Find in modeling tool <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\simulation\LaptopCostAnalysis.mdzip</em></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189147617 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00024: Association Block

- page_id: `189147617`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189147617/Association+Block
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T14:46:14.850+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Glossary of SysML concepts > Element descriptions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Association Block can own properties and connectors, like any other [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']. Each instance of an Association Block can link together instances of the end classifiers of the association. To refer to linked objects and values of an instance of an Association Block, it is necessary for the modeler to specify which [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Participant Property' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] of the Association Block identify the instances being linked at which end of the association. The value of a participant property on an instance (link) of the Association Block is the value or object at the end of the link corresponding to this end of the association.[How to create Association Block >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/SYSMLP2024xR2/Defining+Blocks+in+Block+Definition+Diagram#DefiningBlocksinBlockDefinitionDiagram-CreatingAssociationBlock)

The Association Block can be the type of multiple other Connectors to reuse the same internal structure for all the Connectors. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Association Block as Connector type' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']>]]>

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An Association Block can own properties and connectors, like any other <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Block" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ac:link>. Each instance of an Association Block can link together instances of the end classifiers of the association. To refer to linked objects and values of an instance of an Association Block, it is necessary for the modeler to specify which <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Participant Property" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Participant Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the Association Block identify the instances being linked at which end of the association. The value of a participant property on an instance (link) of the Association Block is the value or object at the end of the link corresponding to this end of the association.<a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/SYSMLP2024xR2/Defining+Blocks+in+Block+Definition+Diagram#DefiningBlocksinBlockDefinitionDiagram-CreatingAssociationBlock"> How to create Association Block &gt;&gt;</a></p><p>The Association Block can be the type of multiple other Connectors to reuse the same internal structure for all the Connectors. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using Association Block as Connector type" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to use the Association Block as Connector type >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189146748 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00026: Automatic delegation

- page_id: `189146748`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189146748/Automatic+delegation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-08-21T11:42:17.142+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Modeling structure with Blocks > Connecting Blocks in SysML Internal Block Diagram
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2135394829

2135394841

1096999631

**On this page**

2135394830

In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'], the automatic delegation functionality:

- Creates a connector from the port to the nearest border.
- Creates connectors with relevant ports between the ports of the nested parts (delegate).

##### Delegating ports and connectors automatically via port

To delegate ports and connectors automatically via port

1. Select a port. To create connectors with relevant ports for ports within a nested ports structure, make sure to invoke the **Delegate Port(s)** action either from the part or the port enclosing a nested ports structure. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. In the diagram toolbar, do one of the following:
  1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='tools_button.png'] Tools > Delegate Port(s) .
  2. Right-click the port, and in its shortcut menu, select Delegate Port(s) .

The needed port and connector are created to the nearest border:

- If a port is enclosed within a part that is not nested within another part, a connector is created to the nearest border - the diagram frame. [ATTACHMENT filename='Delegating_ports_and_connectors_automatically_1.png']
- If a port is enclosed within a part that is nested within another part, a connector is created to the nearest border - the enclosing part. [ATTACHMENT filename='Delegating_ports_and_connectors_automatically_2.png']

##### Delegating ports and connectors automatically via part

To delegate ports and connectors automatically via part

1. Select a part containing a port.
2. In the diagram toolbar, do one of the following:
  1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='tools_button.png'] Tools > Delegate Port(s) .
  2. Right-click the part, and in its shortcut menu, select Delegate Port(s) .

The needed port and connector are created automatically.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Delegating ports and connectors automatically via connector

To delegate ports and connectors automatically via connector

1. Select a connector between two nested parts.
2. In the diagram toolbar, do one of the following:
  1. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='tools_button.png'] Tools > Delegate Port(s) .
  2. Right-click the connector, and in its shortcut menu, select Delegate Port(s) .

The needed ports and connectors are created automatically. If the connector has a flow, that flow is also applied to the delegated connectors (as shown in the figure below). Some adjustments might be necessary. They will be indicated by the validation rules.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

2135394827

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Autowire Parts' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Item Flow management' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189146855 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00027: Automatic Instantiation wizard

- page_id: `189146855`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189146855/Automatic+Instantiation+wizard
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T14:46:04.330+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Modeling structure with Blocks
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1349135865

1349135867

1349386399

**On this page**

33

1349135866

##### Introduction

The **Automatic Instantiation**wizard analyzes the structure of a selected element and collects all available information about that element from the model (attributes, properties, and so on). It evaluates collected information and suggests all possible instances to be created.

This tool allows you to create instances of various entities with just a few clicks. It is useful for working with complex models, and for assembling large systems from parts. For properties having a multiplicity of more than 1, you can create as many instances of the same type as you need, since parallel parts can be added while creating instances.While creating instances automatically, you can:

- Select properties for which the instances will be created.
- Change types of the instantiated properties.
- Change names of the instantiated properties.
- Apply custom stereotypesto the instantiated properties.
- Assign default values for the instantiated properties.

The wizard consists of the following areas:

- Steps area: displays all steps of the wizard and shows the current step you are working on.
- Element tree: displays the entire internal structure of the selected element.
- *Selection area*: allows you to select the elements to be instantiated.
- Instantiated properties area: allows you to change the default values and create several different sets of instances .

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Automatic Instantiation wizard of the *Vehicle* Block with the main areas highlighted: Steps area, Element tree, Selection area, and Instantiated properties area.

##### Using the **Automatic Instantiation** wizard

To create and display instance specifications of Blocks in the diagram by using the **Automatic Instantiation** wizard

1. Select the Block for which you want to create instances.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Tools > Create Instance (see the following figure). [ATTACHMENT filename='create_instance_command.png']
3. Follow the steps of the **Automatic Instantiation**wizard. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Steps of the Automatic Instantiation wizard' space='']How to work in each step of the **Automatic Instantiation**wizard >> If you are satisfied with the default values provided in the **Automatic Instantiation**wizard, you can finish the wizard in the first step without making any changes. The instances will be created with the default values and in your working package. To change default values or create several different configurations, read [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Steps of the Automatic Instantiation wizard' space=''].
4. Click Finish when you are done. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='MD2024xR2'] and a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Block Definition Diagram' space=''] are created for the selected Block (see the figure below). All part instances are grouped under their owners which allows you to visually identify the structure of nested instances in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space=''] .

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Block Definition Diagram with instances of the vehicle Block is created by using the Automatic Instantiation wizard.

1349135864

**Related pages**

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the**VehicleStructure**sample model. To open this sample, download*[ATTACHMENT filename='VehicleStructure.mdzip'].*

style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Apply custom <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">stereotypes </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">to the instantiated properties.</span> </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Assign default values for the instantiated properties.</span></li></ul><p>The wizard consists of the following areas:</p><ul><li><em>Steps area:</em> displays all steps of the wizard and shows the current step you are working on.</li><li><em>Element tree:</em> <span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span> <span>displays the entire internal structure of the selected element.</span> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span></li><li><span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span> <span> <em>Selection area</em>: allows you to select the elements to be instantiated. </span> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span></li><li><em>Instantiated properties area:</em> allows you to <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">change the default values and create several different </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">sets of instances</span>.</li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-bottom: 0.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="automatic_instantiation.png" /></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Automatic Instantiation wizard of the <em>Vehicle</em> Block with the main areas <span style="color: rgb(94,108,132);">highlighted</span>: Steps area, Element tree, Selection area, and Instantiated properties area.</h6><h3><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Using the <strong>Automatic Instantiation</strong> wizard</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To create and display instance specifications of Blocks in the diagram by using the <strong>Automatic Instantiation</strong> wizard</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span>Select the Block for which you want to create instances.</span></li><li>From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Instance</strong> (see the following figure).<br /> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_instance_command.png" /></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Follow the steps of the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <strong>Automatic Instantiation </strong>wizard</span></span>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Steps of the Automatic Instantiation wizard" /><ac:link-body>How to work in each step of the <strong>Automatic Instantiation </strong>wizard &gt;&gt;</ac:link-body></ac:link></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="648d4921-120b-4ec6-9bac-43292178a13d"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">If you are satisfied with the default values provided in the <strong>Automatic Instantiation </strong>wizard, you can finish the wizard in the first step without making any changes. The instances will be created with the default values and in your working package. To change default values or create several different configurations, read <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Steps of the Automatic Instantiation wizard" /></ac:link>.</span> </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br /> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Instance Specification" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Instance Specifications]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and a new<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Block Definition Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ SysML Block Definition Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> are created for the selected Block (see the figure below). All part instances are grouped under their owners which allows you to visually identify the structure of nested instances in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li></ol><h6 style="margin-bottom: 0.0px;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="displayed instances of the vehicle structure.png" /></ac:image></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-bottom: 20.0px;">The Block Definition Diagram with instances of the vehicle Block is created by using the Automatic Instantiation wizard.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="856d6757-5dbb-4e64-8a1c-cbea8e2e2b95"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1349135864</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f0f6a906-a89c-4b8b-ae80-42eb662a2471" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p><span>The model used in the figures of this page is the </span><strong>VehicleStructure</strong><span> sample model. To open this sample, download</span><span class="confluence-link"><em> <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="VehicleStructure.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="_sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.</em></span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189146740 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=3 -->
## PAGE 00028: Autowire Parts

- page_id: `189146740`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189146740/Autowire+Parts
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-11-04T14:19:51.756+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Modeling structure with Blocks > Connecting Blocks in SysML Internal Block Diagram
- labels: ['automatic-connections', 'automatic-connectors', 'connect-parts', 'autowire']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Autowire Parts** capability analyzes and finds compatible ports/interfaces, and automatically creates [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MD2024xR2'] between [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='']. The figure below demonstrates the *Thermal View* Internal Block diagram before and after Autowire Parts when the entire diagram is selected. This functionality provides a quick and easy way to create multiple connectors simultaneously.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='']and ports are displayed or created in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''], the parts can be related to one another via [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MD2024xR2']Connectors. You can manually create these connections by using the **Connector** button from the part's/port's shape smart manipulator toolbar or from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024xR2']. You can also do this automatically by using the **Autowire Parts** command from the Tools menu ([IMAGE alt='' src='']) in the diagram toolbar, if the following conditionsconditions are satisfied:

- Parts and ports are displayed on the Internal Block diagram pane.
- The displayed ports are [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Full Port' space=''].
- Ports are typed by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface Block' space=''] that have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Property' space=''] defined.
- Types and directions of these flow properties are compatible.
- The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MD2024xR2'] do not exist in the model.
- Only one-to-one connection is supported.
- Multiple interfaces are not supported.

See an example [ATTACHMENT filename='Autowire-Parts.mdzip']

The following procedure describes how to use the **Autowire Parts** function. This functionality works for an entire diagram or individually selected parts and ports.

To connect Part Properties via compatible ports automatically

1. Select any number of part or port shapes, or the diagram pane.
2. In the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Tools > **Autowire Parts**, or press Ctrl+Alt+W. InformationIf new connectors are not created after clicking the **Autowire Parts**, check if all required are satisfied.
3. In the opened Messages window, you can specify two options: [ATTACHMENT filename='autowiring_message.png']
  - Highlight new connections - leave the check box selected, if you want the newly created Connectors to be highlighted on the diagram pane, and be able to confirm or delete them manually.
  - Show this message next time - leave the check box selected, if you want this message to appear the next time you click the Autowire Parts command.
4. Click OK .
5. The Connector(s) between Part Properties via compatible interfaces are created in the model. If you left the Highlight new connection check box selected in the Messages window (step 3, first bullet), you must review the newly created Connectors in the following way (see the figure below): a. Select the Connector. b. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='confirm_connector.png'] and choose: - Confirm Connector - removes the highlight from the Connector. - Delete Connector - removes the Connector from the model. - Ignore - removes the highlight from the Connector. - Select In Validation Results - opens the Active Validation Results table. [ATTACHMENT filename='confirm_connection.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The <strong>Autowire Parts</strong> capability analyzes and finds compatible ports/interfaces, and automatically <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">creates <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Connector" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Connectors]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> between <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Part Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>. The figure below demonstrates the <em>Thermal View</em> Internal Block diagram before and after Autowire Parts when the entire diagram is selected. This functionality provides a quick and easy way to create multiple connectors simultaneously.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="The Thermal View Internal Block diagram before and after Autowiring." ac:alt="The Thermal View Internal Block diagram before and after Autowiring."><ri:attachment ri:filename="autowiring_ibd.png" /></ac:image></p><p>After <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[parts ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>and ports are displayed or created in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Internal Block Diagram" /></ac:link>, the parts can be related to one another via <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Connector" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Connector</span>s</ac:link-body></ac:link>. You can manually create these connections by using the <strong>Connector</strong> button from the part's/port's shape smart manipulator toolbar or from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram palette]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. You can also do this automatically by using the <strong>Autowire Parts</strong> command from the Tools menu (<ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="14"><ri:attachment ri:filename="tools.png" /></ac:image>) in the diagram toolbar, if the following <ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3c4ff79e-dd59-454d-96cd-b0abd4201673"><ac:parameter ac:name="">conditions</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>conditions are satisfied:</p><ul><li>Parts and ports are displayed on the Internal Block diagram pane.</li><li><p>The displayed ports are <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Proxy Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Proxy Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Full Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Full Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></li><li>Ports are typed by <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Interface Block" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Interface Blocks]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> that have <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Flow Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[flow properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> defined.</li><li><p>Types and directions of these flow properties are compatible.</p></li><li><p>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Connector" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Connectors]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> do not exist in the model.</p></li><li>Only one-to-one connection is supported. </li><li>Multiple interfaces are not supported.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a49c8f77-3b0b-4c0e-a699-7e3c5f097604"><ac:rich-text-body><p>See an example <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Autowire-Parts.mdzip" /></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br />The following procedure describes how to use the <strong>Autowire Parts</strong> function. This functionality works for an entire diagram or individually selected parts and ports.</p><p><br /></p><p>To connect Part Properties via compatible ports automatically</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Select any number of part or port shapes, or the diagram pane.</p></li><li><p>In the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="14"><ri:attachment ri:filename="tools.png" /></ac:image> Tools &gt; <strong>Autowire Parts</strong>, or press Ctrl+Alt+W.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1da4ecc1-043b-41d5-87e2-788412402bd8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If new connectors are not created after clicking the <strong>Autowire Parts</strong>, check if all required<ac:link ac:anchor="conditions"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ conditions]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> are satisfied.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p>In the opened Messages window, you can specify two options:</p><ul><li>Highlight new connections - leave the check box selected, if you want the newly created Connectors to be highlighted on the diagram pane, and be able to confirm or delete them manually.</li><li>Show this message next time - leave the check box selected, if you want this message to appear the next time you click the <strong>Autowire Parts</strong> command.</li></ul><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="autowiring_message.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>The Connector(s) between Part Properties <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">via compatible interfaces</span> are created in the model. If you left the <strong>Highlight new connection</strong> check box selected in the Messages window (step 3, first bullet), you must review the newly created Connectors in the following way (see the figure below):<br />       a. Select the Connector.<br />       b. Click <ac:image ac:title="Confirm connection!"><ri:attachment ri:filename="confirm_connector.png" /></ac:image> and choose:<strong><br />             - Confirm Connector </strong>- removes the highlight from the Connector.<br /><strong>             - Delete Connector </strong>- removes the Connector from the model.<strong> </strong><br />            <strong> - Ignore</strong> - removes the highlight from the Connector.<strong> </strong><br />             <strong>-</strong> <strong>Select In Validation Results</strong> - opens the Active Validation Results table.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Confirming newly created Connector after Autowiring function when the Highlight new connection check box was selected in the Messages window." ac:alt="Confirming newly created Connector after Autowiring function when the Highlight new connection check box was selected in the Messages window."><ri:attachment ri:filename="confirm_connection.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189146878 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00030: Behavior to structure synchronization

- page_id: `189146878`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189146878/Behavior+to+structure+synchronization
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-29T09:51:10.431+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1084440424

1084440437

1084440427

The behavior to structure synchronization checks the consistency of the Internal Block diagram (IBD) with Activities, automatically detects inconsistencies and enables users to handle each inconsistency individually by providing solvers. As a result, the coherence of IBDs with Activities is ensured at any stage of the model development process.

The behavior to structure synchronization validates the Part Properties displayed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] to check for compatible

- Proxy Ports
- Flow Properties
- Interface Blocks
- Connectors
- Item Flows

To verify whether or not the structural elements (e.g., a Proxy Port and an Interface Block) and behavioral elements (e.g., a flow) are compatible, validation rules are used. If any inconsistencies are found, they are highlighted in yellow in the Internal Block Diagram and displayed in the**Validation Results** panel. Then, they can be individually handled using solvers.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Synchronizing behavior to structure.

##### Checking behavior to structure synchronization

You need to set the **Allocation Mode**(**Options**>**Project**>**SysML**>**Allocation Mode**)project optionto **Usage** before you can synchronize behavior to structure.

To check the structure model consistency with the behavior model

1. In the toolbar of the Internal Block Diagram, click [ATTACHMENT filename='tools_button.png'] Tools.
2. In the open drop-down menu, select Behavior to Structure Synchronization. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

The behavior to structure synchronization check is initiated.

484219123

**Continue reading**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="841a5f4d-74a0-4fa4-aaed-99dc00301c21"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1084440424</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0a176ceb-ea86-431b-a67d-47098a620fcc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1084440437</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c95de57f-624d-4ab8-8f50-e48cce559498"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1084440427</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The behavior to structure synchronization checks the consistency of the Internal Block diagram (IBD) with Activities, automatically detects inconsistencies and enables users to handle each inconsistency individually by providing solvers. As a result, the coherence of IBDs with Activities is ensured at any stage of the model development process.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ea75ada-62b5-429b-ae2d-cb2efce51e89"><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-sd8lDPHtlk" /></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>   <ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3216511a-9150-41ad-9be8-7f35af1e11bc"><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Zuphq7gFL1w" /></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p>The behavior to structure synchronization validates the Part Properties displayed in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Internal Block Diagram" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Internal Block Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to check for compatible</p><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="784a8dde-e6d8-4776-b689-e7f7eddb8085">Proxy Ports</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="784a8dde-e6d8-4776-b689-e7f7eddb8085">Flow Properties</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="784a8dde-e6d8-4776-b689-e7f7eddb8085">Interface Blocks</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="784a8dde-e6d8-4776-b689-e7f7eddb8085">Connectors</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="784a8dde-e6d8-4776-b689-e7f7eddb8085">Item Flows</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul><p>To verify whether or not the structural elements (e.g., a Proxy Port and an Interface Block) and behavioral elements (e.g., a flow) are compatible, validation rules are used. If any inconsistencies are found, they are highlighted in yellow in the Internal Block Diagram and displayed in the<strong> Validation Results</strong> panel. Then, they can be individually handled using solvers.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="structure_and_behavior_models_synchronization.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Behavior to structure synchronization" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Synchronizing behavior to structure.</h6><h3>Checking behavior to structure synchronization </h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e7e7c0f2-00c7-404d-a569-4855cca3bc1b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You need to set the <strong>Allocation Mode</strong><span> </span><span>(</span><strong>Options</strong><span> &gt;</span><strong> Project</strong><span> &gt; </span><strong>SysML</strong><span> &gt; </span><strong>Allocation Mode</strong><span>) </span>project option<span> </span>to <strong>Usage</strong> before you can synchronize behavior to structure.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br />To check the structure model consistency with the behavior model</p><hr /><ol><li>In the toolbar of the Internal Block Diagram, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="tools_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Behavior to structure synchronization" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>Tools.</li><li>In the open drop-down menu, select <strong>Behavior to Structure Synchronization.</strong><br /><strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="behavior_to_structure_sync_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Behavior to structure synchronization" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></strong></li></ol><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a4344bbf-9a03-49f3-8337-2857606c756f">The behavior to structure synchronization check is initiated.</ac:inline-comment-marker> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6b67cc1f-75c3-42fa-b535-cd642921b424"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">484219123</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Continue reading</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="3cb859a5-2ed8-42c2-b553-a77c7e0bf462" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189147620 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00031: Binding Connector

- page_id: `189147620`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189147620/Binding+Connector
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T14:46:14.962+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Glossary of SysML concepts > Element descriptions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

711187185

711187197

711187186

A Binding connector is the only connector that allows to bind [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Parameter' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] to the properties of other Block. It is a connector which specifies that the properties at both ends of the connector have equal values. If the properties at both ends of a binding connector are typed by Data Types or Value Types, it means that the instances of the properties at both ends must hold equal values, recursively through any nested properties within the connected properties. If the properties at both ends of a binding connector are typed by Blocks, it means that the instances of the properties must refer to the same block instance. As with any connector owned by a SysML Block, each end of a binding connector may be nested within a multi-level path of properties accessible from the owning Block. The NestedConnectorEnd stereotype is used to represent such nested ends, just as for nested ends of other SysML connectors.

The two ends of a binding connector must have either the same type or types that are compatible so that equality of their values can be defined.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

711187183

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Parametric Diagram' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']
- [Creating Binding Connector](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/SYSMLP2024xR2/Creating+Binding+Connector)

If the properties at both ends of a binding connector are typed by Data Types or Value Types, it means that the instances of the properties at both ends must hold equal values, recursively through any nested properties within the connected properties. If the properties at both ends of a binding connector are typed by Blocks, it means that the instances of the properties must refer to the same block instance. As with any connector owned by a SysML Block, each end of a binding connector may be nested within a multi-level path of properties accessible from the owning Block. The NestedConnectorEnd stereotype is used to represent such nested ends, just as for nested ends of other SysML connectors.</p><p>The two ends of a binding connector must have either the same type or types that are compatible so that equality of their values can be defined.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Binding Connector" ac:alt="Binding Connector"><ri:attachment ri:filename="binding_connector.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Binding Connector" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bc5995b5-c75f-4a85-947c-5f31267419ec"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">711187183</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Parametric Diagram" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><a class="current" href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/SYSMLP2024xR2/Creating+Binding+Connector">Creating Binding Connector</a></p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189147608 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00032: Blackbox ICD Table

- page_id: `189147608`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189147608/Blackbox+ICD+Table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-01-08T13:03:34.737+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Glossary of SysML concepts > Diagram descriptions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Blackbox ICD Table represents all external Ports/interfaces of the selected Block. The table is useful when trying to identify what kind of inputs and outputs are available in the selected system.

In the following example, the distiller system has two inputs (*dirty water*and *q in*) and three outputs (*bypass*, *purified* and *sludge*). In this case, the Port *dirty water*, Port *purified*, Port *bypass* support such value properties as: *water temp*, *water press*, etc. Pay attention, that the input to distiller is a *dirty water* (row 1) and the output from distiller is *purified*water (row 4). Both of them contains the same properties but it may have different values defined (they are not defined in the figure). [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Interface Control Document tables' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']>]]>

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Blackbox ICD Table of the Distiller Block.

A Blackbox ICD Table consists of the following columns.

| Column name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| # | A row number. |
| ~ ^ | Displays if Port is conjugated (~) or inherited (^). |
| Port Name | A Port name. |
| Port Type | A Port type. |
| Type Features | Displays all the features (e.g., Flow property, Part property) of the Port type. |
| Direction | Displays a combined direction prefix of the Port. |
| Documentation | Displays a documentation text. If it is not described, click to edit. |
