<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189146910 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00067: Creating and displaying contextual relationships

- page_id: `189146910`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189146910/Creating+and+displaying+contextual+relationships
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T14:46:04.619+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Contextual relationships
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Once you enable the creation of contextual relationships in the **Project Options** dialog, you can start creating them immediately.

multiple places listYou can create contextual relationships from multiple places in your modeling tool:

- Structure tab ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024xR2'])
- Internal Block Diagram andBlock Definition Diagram
- 
- 

##### Creating contextual relationships from all the places listed above

To create contextual relationships from the **Create Relations** dialog

1. Right-click an element, click Create Relation > Outgoing / Incoming , and then select the contextual relationship type to create. The Create New <relation name> (contextual) To / From dialog opens.
2. Do one or more of the following :
  - In the Model Browser, select the element to create a relationship to / from.
  - On the diagram pane , select the element to create a relationship to / from.
  - In the **Create New <relation name> (contextual) To** /**From** dialog, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and select the element to create a relationship to / from.
3. Click OK .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating contextual relationships using the Create Relations dialog.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a relationship' space='MD2024xR2']>]]>

##### contextual relationships in diagramsCreating and displaying contextual relationships in diagrams

To create contextual relationships from the **diagram palette**or **smart manipulator toolbar**

1. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram palette, select the relationship type to create.
  - On the diagram pane, select the relationship end and click [ATTACHMENT filename='satisfy_relationship_icon.png'] in the smart manipulator toolbar.
2. Select the appropriate elements on the diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating contextual relationships from the diagram palette or smart manipulator toolbar.

To contextualize relationships from the **smart manipulator toolbar**

1. In the diagram pane, click on the existing relationship. The smart manipulator toolbar appears.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='contextualize.png'] to contextualize the selected relationship.

You can only contextualize *Allocate*,*Refine*,*Satisfy*,*Trace*,and*Verify*relationships.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Contextualizing relationships using the smart manipulator toolbar.

You can decontextualize the existing contextual relationship by clicking[IMAGE alt='' src=''](Decontextualize) in the smart manipulator toolbar.

For a more compact Internal Block Diagram view, contextual relationships can be represented in the **Element Properties** compartment of the element shape. For this, you need to create the appropriate [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extending Metamodel with Derived Properties' space='MD2024xR2'] first. Note that this expression must be identical to the dependency matrix criteria for displaying contextual relationships.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Displaying Contextual Relationship in the element compartment.

##### contextual relationships in dependency matricesCreating contextual relationships in dependency matrices

Contextual relationships can only be created in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying scope' space='MD2024xR2']**Structure** tree-view dependency matrices.

Contextual Relationships in Dependency MatricesTo create contextual relationships in **dependency matrices**

1. In the Criteria area of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix' space='MD2024xR2'] , next to Dependency Criteria , click [ATTACHMENT filename='add_elements_from_the_model.png'] .
2. Click the Simple Navigation operation and scroll down to the Contextual Relations category.
3. Under Relation Criterion , select the contextual relationship type to create.
4. When you are done, click OK .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Specifying Relation Criterion to build a Dependency Matrix.

##### contextual relationships in tables

Contextual relationships can only be created in[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying row elements hierarchically' space='MD2024xR2']**Structure**tree-view tables.

Before you can create contextual relationships in your structure tree-view table, you need to add a custom column or create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extending Metamodel with Derived Properties' space='MD2024xR2'] to represent them.

To add a custom column/derived property to represent contextual relationships

1. In the diagram toolbar, click**Columns** >**New Custom Column**/**New Derived Property**. Select **New Custom Column** to add a custom column to the specific table only.Select **New Derived Property** to add a column to be able to use in all future tables.
2. In the Expression dialog, click the Simple Navigation operation and scroll down to the Contextual Relations category.
3. Under Relation Criterion , select the contextual relationship ty pe (e.g., contextual Satisfy) to create. [ATTACHMENT filename='collecting_contextual_satisfy.png']
4. When you are finished, click OK .

Once you have added a custom column, you can now create contextual relationships directly in your table.

To create contextual relationships in **structure tree-view tables**

1. In the structure tree-view table, select the appropriat e row and th en click [ATTACHMENT filename='three_dots_button.png'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='structure_tree_view_table_contextual_relationships.png']
2. In the open Select Elements dialog, select the element(s) to create the contextual relationship(s). [ATTACHMENT filename='selecting_requirement_in_select_elements_dialog.png']
3. When you are finished, click OK .

The contextual relationship is created in the model and represented in the table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189146612 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00068: Creating and managing Block properties

- page_id: `189146612`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189146612/Creating+and+managing+Block+properties
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T14:45:59.779+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Modeling structure with Blocks > Decomposing Blocks
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1096811557

1096811559

1096811556

**On this page**

43

1096811558

When the same [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space=''] is represented in several [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Block Definition Diagram' space=''], all [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='MD2024xR2'] roles are represented in the Block [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML specific compartments' space=''].

You can create and manage the Block properties in several ways:

33

##### Using the smart manipulator

44

###### Creating a property for a Block

You can create and manage properties for a Block directly on the diagram pane by using the symbol [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulators' space='MD2024xR2'].

To create a property for a Block

1. Select the Block shape.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='create_element_smart_manipulator.png'] smart manipulator.
3. From the opened menu select the property you want to create for a Block. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Type its name. [ATTACHMENT filename='typing_name_for_part_property.png']
5. Specify its type. [ATTACHMENT filename='specifying_type_for_part_property.png'] The property is created and displayed on the Block shape in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML specific compartments' space=''] . [ATTACHMENT filename='part_property_in_Block_compartments.png']

###### Removing a Block property from the Block shape

To remove a Block property from the Block shape

1. Select the property on Block shape.
2. Press Delete . The property is removed from the Block compartments but not from the model.

###### Deleting a Block property from the model

To delete a Block property from the model

1. Right-click the property on the Block shape.
2. From the shortcut menu select the Select in Containment Tree command. The property is selected in the Containment tree.
3. Do one of the following: If a property is shared with other people, the **Question** dialog opens. You can then confirm or cancel the **Delete** command. The Block property is removed from the model.
  - Press Delete .
  - Right-click and from the shortcut menu and select the Delete command.

##### Using the Specification window

All owned and inherited Block properties are collected on the left of the Block [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2'] > **Properties**. Block properties are grouped in the Block compartments. Manage them by creating, redefining or deleting directly in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2'].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Column name |  |
| --- | --- |
| Name | Property name. |
| Type | Property type. |
| Default Value | Property default value. |
| Owner | Block name that contains the current property. |

****

| Button name | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | Opens the Specification window of the selected property. |
| Create | Opens the list of available properties to create. Click to create the Connector property, Part property, Reference property, Value property, Constraint property, Flow property. |
| Redefine | Duplicates the selected item and marks its name in ascending order. |
| Delete | Removes the selected item from the list. |

****

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="90bf0532-0b80-4cc1-a1ad-988ac22738f4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1096811557</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac4249ad-18f7-41cd-8f51-83fdc0381d48"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1096811559</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="43bf43f0-43f8-481d-a2fb-502c4013a023"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1096811556</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a1916cc6-a999-497b-b1e8-9a4fbc89e178"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="63ae355d-7cdb-4ca6-abab-af74a51ff149"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1096811558</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><br /></p><p>When the same <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Block" /></ac:link> is represented in several <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Block Definition Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Block Definition Diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, all <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Association" /></ac:link> roles are represented in the <span class="confluence-link">Block</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML specific compartments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[compartment area]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>You can create and manage the Block properties in several ways:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1bf91090-a552-49d1-8975-6340c358e4a9"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Using the smart manipulator</h3><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b2e1dbc5-be9d-4367-8f6d-fc3c41c7a9ea"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p><br /></p><h4>Creating a property for a Block</h4><p>You can create and manage properties for a Block directly on the diagram pane by using the symbol <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Smart manipulators" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulators]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>To create a property for a Block</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Block shape.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Create Element" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Create Element" ac:height="15"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_element_smart_manipulator.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>smart manipulator.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">From the opened menu select the property you want to create for a Block.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_part_property_from_smart_manipulator.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li>Type its name.<br /><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="115"><ri:attachment ri:filename="typing_name_for_part_property.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Specify its type.<br /><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="125"><ri:attachment ri:filename="specifying_type_for_part_property.png" /></ac:image><br />The property is created and displayed on the Block shape in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML specific compartments" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[compartments area]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="106"><ri:attachment ri:filename="part_property_in_Block_compartments.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><h4>Removing a Block property from the Block shape</h4><p><br /></p><p>To remove a Block property from the Block shape</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the property on Block shape.</li><li>Press <strong>Delete</strong>.<br />The property is removed from the Block compartments but <strong>not</strong> from the model.</li></ol><p><br /></p><h4>Deleting a Block property from the model</h4><p>To delete a Block property from the model</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the property on the Block shape.</li><li>From the shortcut menu select the <strong>Select in Containment Tree</strong> command. The property is selected in the Containment tree.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Press <strong>Delete</strong>.</li><li>Right-click and from the shortcut menu and select the <strong>Delete</strong> command.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="862c77d5-8802-4bb3-a8d7-72fb75e88f50"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If a property is shared with other people, the <strong>Question</strong> dialog opens. You can then confirm or cancel the <strong>Delete</strong> command.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">The Block property is removed from the model.</p></li></ol><h3>Using the Specification window</h3><p>All owned and inherited Block properties are collected on the left of the Block <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> &gt; <strong>Properties</strong>. Block properties are grouped in the Block compartments. Manage them by creating, redefining or deleting directly in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[General Specification pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="properties_property_group.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 45.628%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 20.224%;" /><col style="width: 79.7353%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Column name</th><th><br /></th></tr><tr><td><strong>Name</strong></td><td>Property name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Type</strong></td><td>Property type.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Default Value</strong></td><td>Property default value.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Owner</strong></td><td>Block name that contains the current property.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 45.9459%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 19.041%;" /><col style="width: 80.9185%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button name</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:title="Open Specification button" ac:alt="Open Specification button"><ri:attachment ri:filename="open_specification_button.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td>Opens the Specification window of the selected property.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Create</strong></td><td><p>Opens the list of available properties to create. Click to create the Connector property, Part property, Reference property, Value property, Constraint property, Flow property.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Redefine</strong></td><td>Duplicates the selected item and marks its name in ascending order.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Delete</strong></td><td colspan="1">Removes the selected item from the list.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189147024 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00069: Creating Binding Connector

- page_id: `189147024`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189147024/Creating+Binding+Connector
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T14:46:05.497+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Modeling parametric constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Binding Connector' space=''] in the following ways:

44

###### Using the Parametric diagram toolbar

To create a Binding Connector from the Parametric diagram toolbar

1. Click the Binding Connector button.
2. Select the source of the connector ( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Parameter' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space=''] ).
3. Select the target of the connector.

###### Using the smart manipulator toolbar

To create a Binding Connector from the smart manipulator toolbar

1. Select the connector's source (either [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Parameter' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space=''] ).
2. From the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulator toolbar' space='MD2024xR2'] , select the Binding Connector button.
3. Select the target of the connector.

A a result the Binding Connector is created with keyword «equal». You can change keyword«equal»to sign "=" in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Symbol Properties dialog' space='MD2024xR2']**Symbol Properties** dialogby selecting the**All**properties mode and setting the**Show Equal Sign Notation**property value to true.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Binding Connector" /></ac:link> in the following ways:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c8939534-962d-4f76-aac2-abde89329f1d"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h4>Using the Parametric diagram toolbar</h4><p>To create a Binding Connector from the Parametric diagram toolbar</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Binding Connector</strong> button.</li><li>Select the source of the connector (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Constraint Parameter" /></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part Property" /></ac:link>).</li><li>Select the target of the connector.</li></ol><h4>Using the smart manipulator toolbar</h4><p>To create a Binding Connector from the smart manipulator toolbar</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the connector's source (either <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Constraint Parameter" /></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part Property" /></ac:link>).</li><li>From the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Smart manipulator toolbar" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator toolbar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, select the <strong>Binding Connector</strong> button.</li><li>Select the target of the connector.</li></ol><p>A a result the Binding Connector is created with keyword <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">«equal</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">»</span>. <span>You can change keyword </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">«equal</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">» </span><span>to sign &quot;=&quot; in the</span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Symbol Properties dialog" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link><span> by selecting the </span><strong>All</strong><span> properties mode and setting the</span><strong> Show Equal Sign Notation</strong><span> property value to true.</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_equal_sign_notation.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189146963 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00070: Creating Constraint Parameters automatically

- page_id: `189146963`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189146963/Creating+Constraint+Parameters+automatically
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-04-20T14:08:56.104+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Modeling parametric constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create Constraint Parameters automatically when:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Constraint Parameters automatically' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']
- Using the Parse and Create Parameters button[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Constraint Parameters automatically' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] .

bindingTo automatically create new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Parameter' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] when creating a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Binding Connector' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']

1. Do one of the following:
  - Select an appropriate property shape and, from the smart manipulator toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='binding_connector.png'] .
  - From the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='binding_connector_from_toolbar.png'] , and select an appropriate property shape.
2. Connect it to a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] shape: - if all parameters from the constraint equation are already created in the model, a new parameter is created with the same name as the property name. [ATTACHMENT filename='automatically_ created_parameter.png'] - if one or more parameters from the constraint equation are not created in the model, choose them from the Select Parameter menu list. [ATTACHMENT filename='automatically_ created_parameter_from_menu_list.png']

equationTo create [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Parameter' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] automatically using the **Parse and Create Parameters** button

1. Create a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] shape or select an existing one.
2. Type a constraint expression or a new variable for an existing expression.
3. Do either: Constraint Parameters are created. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_parameters_automatically_from_smart_manipulator_toolbar1.png']
  - Click the shape and from the smart manipulator toolbar, select [ATTACHMENT filename='parse_and_create_parameters.png'] .
  - Right-click the shape and select Tools > Parse and Create Parameters .

Note that the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] type is always a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'], thus all [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Parameter' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] are created under that particular Constraint Block.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189146934 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00071: Creating contextual Satisfy

- page_id: `189146934`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189146934/Creating+contextual+Satisfy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-07-22T19:46:30.261+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Contextual relationships
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create a contextual Satisfy relationship by simply dragging-and-dropping the selected property onto the requirement directly from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'][CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Structure tab' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'].

To create a contextual Satisfy relationship

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Structure tab' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] , locate the property that satisfies the selected requirement.
2. Place it onto the requirement and select Create Satisfy (contextual) . The relationship is created . [ATTACHMENT filename='dragging_and_dropping_to_create_contextual_satisfy.png'] Creating a contextual Satisfy relationship from the Structure tab.

Dragging-and-dropping a property from another [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] tab results in the automatic creation of a non-contextual Satisfy relationship.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create a contextual Satisfy relationship by simply dragging-and-dropping the selected property onto the requirement directly <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ce4c533e-5531-426a-9b65-844bdb0dd7d0">from</ac:inline-comment-marker> the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[S]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Structure tab" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[tructure tab]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </p><p><br /></p><p>To create a contextual Satisfy relationship</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Structure tab" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ac:link>, locate the property that satisfies the selected requirement.</li><li>Place it onto the requirement and select <strong>Create Satisfy (contextual)</strong>. The relationship is <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e0bae97d-fa3c-4636-beda-842d8d876281">created</ac:inline-comment-marker>. <br /><br /><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dragging_and_dropping_to_create_contextual_satisfy.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating contextual Satisfy" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><h6 style="text-align: center;">Creating a contextual Satisfy relationship from the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="978bc76b-d0cf-47ee-bf4b-45a4e9a61101">Structure</ac:inline-comment-marker> tab.</h6></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e488cf16-4d91-49f8-94bf-815179b5455b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Dragging-and-dropping a property from another <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ac:link> tab results in the automatic creation of a non-contextual Satisfy relationship.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target" />
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189146808 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00073: Creating Interface Control Document tables

- page_id: `189146808`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189146808/Creating+Interface+Control+Document+tables
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T14:46:03.487+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Modeling structure with Blocks

33

1348036670

##### Introduction

An Interface Control Document (ICD) table describes interfaces between systems or/and subsystems. That helps to ensure a compatibility between system components.The ICD tables can describe the following:

- The inputs and outputs of a single system.
- The interface between two systems or subsystems.
- The complete interface protocol from the lowest physical elements (e.g., the mating plugs, the electrical signal voltage levels) to the highest logical levels (e.g., the level 7 application layer of the OSI model), or some subset thereof.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Blackbox ICD table of the Distiller Block.

The views for system components interface are generated automatically from the system model. This provides up to date, efficiently managed, and fully featured model-based ICDs. The true model-based document-free approach is supported, along with all the capabilities of table export to document (images, *html*, *.xls*, *.csv*files).You can create the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Blackbox ICD Table' space='']and/or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Whitebox ICD Table' space=''] directly from the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Block Definition Diagram' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''].

##### Creating Blackbox ICD Table

To create a Blackbox ICD Table

1. In the Containment tree or on the diagram pane, select the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space=''] which ports/ interfaces you want to represent in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Blackbox ICD Table' space=''] .
2. Do one of the following:
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram . Type “bla” and press Enter .
  - On the main toolbar, click the Create Diagram button. Type “bla” and press Enter .
  - Press Ctrl+N . Type “bla” and press Enter .
  - Right-click the Block and select Create Diagram > Blackbox ICD Table .
  - Open the Specification window of the Block, select the Ports/Interfaces property group and click the Create ICD Table button. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_icd_table_button_in_block_specification_window.png'] The Blackbox ICD Table is created. All external Ports/interfaces of the selected Block are represented in the table automatically. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Blackbox ICD Table' space='']>]]>
3. Type a table name and press Enter.

##### Creating Whitebox ICD Table

To create a Whitebox ICD Table

1. In the Containment tree or on the diagram pane, select the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space=''] which assembly you want to represent in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Whitebox ICD Table' space=''] .
2. Do one of the following:
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram . Type “wh” and press Enter .
  - On the main toolbar, click the Create Diagram button. Type “wh” and press Enter .
  - Press Ctrl+N . Type “wh” and press Enter .
  - Right-click the Block and select **Create Diagram** > **Whitebox ICD Table**. 
The Whitebox ICD Table is created. All Parts, their Ports/interfaces and flows are represented in the table automatically. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Whitebox ICD Table' space='']>]]> [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Type a table name and press Enter.

##### Customizing the representation of the ICD table

You can customize the representation of the ICD table by using the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table toolbars' space='MD2024xR2'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table Criteria area' space='MD2024xR2'].

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189146552 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00074: Creating Item Flow

- page_id: `189146552`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189146552/Creating+Item+Flow
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T14:45:59.251+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Item Flow management
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**On this page:**

##### Creating Item Flows

The following procedure showcases how to create Item Flows whether you are working with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Item Flow management' space=''] creation mode.

To create an Item Flow

1. Do one of the following:
  - Select a Connector or an Association (in Direct mode) or an Association, Connector, Message, Control Flow, or Object Flow (in Between Part Types mode) on the diagram pane, and on its [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulator toolbar' space='MD2024xR2'] , click [ATTACHMENT filename='new_item_flow.png'] .
  - Drag the classifier you want to use as the Conveyed Item onto the intended Connector.
2. In the Create/Edit Item Flow dialog, Create new or Select Existing Item Flow step, specify the following options:
  - **Item Flow**. From the drop-down menu, select an existing Item Flow or create a new one by selecting <NEW>. If you select an existing Item Flow, you don't need to specify the rest of the options in this dialog.
  - **Direction**. From the drop-down menu, select the direction of the new Item Flow. If you are creating the Item Flow on the directed relationship, the **Direction** is set automatically and is not available for editing.If you are creating the Item Flow between an Activity Parameter Node and an Action, the**Direction**field displays suggested sender/receiver elements based on the element typing the parts to which actions are allocated.
  - **Conveyed Items**. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] From the **Select Conveyed Classifier** dialog, select a classifier to be used as the Conveyed Classifier. Click **OK**. If you have invoked the **Create/Edit Item Flow** dialog by dragging a classifier onto the Connector and do not want to change it, you may skip this option.
  - (Optional) **Item Property**. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] From the **Select Item Property** dialog, select a Part Property to use as the Item Property. Click **OK**. This option is available when creating an Item Flow in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''].
  - (Optional) **Add Item Flow to corresponding relationships**. Select the check box to synchronize the Item Flow for Associations, Activity Edges, Connectors, and Messages with appropriate Item Flow ends. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the **Edit Item Flow Realization between Activities**step, review the activities.
  - If the **Add Item Flow to corresponding relationships**option is selected and multiple Activity Pairs are detected, remove the unnecessary pairs via the **Remove From Table**button. This step is available only in the Between Part Types creation mode. However, this step is not available if you are creating the Item Flow between Part Properties with no types set. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Click**Review or update affected diagrams** to see the list of diagrams where the selected Item Flow can be realized and displayed. You can choose one or more diagrams you want the specified flow to be displayed. If the diagrams are grayed out, this means there are no Conveyed Items of the selected Item Flow to be displayed. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space=''] **Show Item Flows on Affected Diagrams** controls how the diagrams will be updated for newly created or modified flows. By default, the option is set to true to update all affected diagrams and show the Conveyed Items of Item Flows whenever a new flow is created or an existing one is modified.To learn more about previewing affected diagrams, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Preview Affected Diagrams dialog' space='']. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Click **Finish**. An Item Flow with the selected Conveyed Item (and Item Property if it was defined) is created and is set for the Connector and the corresponding relationships if the **Add Item Flow to corresponding relationships**was selected.

If you want to reverse the Item Flow direction and update it in all related diagrams, read [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Reversing Information Flow direction' space='MD2024xR2'].

##### Specifying the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane

You can also specify a Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane if you have already created an Item Flow.

To specify the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane

1. In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Item.
2. Drag the selected Conveyed Item onto the Item Flow on the diagram pane. The Create/Edit Item Flow dialog opens. If the Item Flow already has Conveyed Item(s), the Conveyed Items field is appended with the new item.
3. Follow the steps of the dialog. See the procedure steps 2-4.
4. Click Finish when you are done. [ATTACHMENT filename='set_as_conveyed_item.png']

##### Showing/hiding the Conveyed Item

You can choose to show or hide the Conveyed Item on the element realizing the Item Flow (Association, Connector, Activity Edge, Message).

To show/hide the Conveyed Item

1. On the diagram pane, select the element realizing an Item Flow.
2. On the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Smart manipulator toolbar' space='MD2024xR2'] :
  1. If the Conveyed Item is not displayed , the tooltip for the button is [ATTACHMENT filename='Show_Realized_Item_Flow.png'] Show Realized Item Flows . Click it to show the Conveyed Item.
  2. If the Conveyed Item is displayed , the tooltip for the button is [ATTACHMENT filename='Hide_Realized_Item_Flow.png'] Hide Realized Item Flows . Click it to hide the Conveyed Item. If the element realizes only one Item Flow , the Conveyed Item is displayed/hidden instantly with the click of a button.
3. If the element realizes multiple Item Flows, the Conveyed Items menu opens. In it, do the following:
  1. Select the check boxes for the Item Flows whose Conveyed Item you want to be displayed.
  2. Clear the check boxes for the Item Flows whose Conveyed Item you want to be hidden. To select all check boxes, click the **Select All**button. To clear all check boxes, click the **Clear All**button.
  3. Click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='Show_Hide_Flowing_Items.png']

When you hide the realized exchanges, you can choose whether you want to hide the arrow notation on the realizing elements. The **Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information Is Missing** [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space=''] controls the arrow visibility.

To change the arrow notation visibility on realizing element when the Conveyed Item is hidden or unspecified

1. From the modeling tool main menu, choose Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. In the dialog, go to General > Diagrams .
3. In the Diagrams property group, find the Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information Is Missing option and set it to true .
4. Click OK when you are done.

##### Realizing Item Flows

You can realize hidden Item Flows in two ways:

- Through the Item Flow Manager.
- From the selected relationship's Smart Manipulator toolbar.

To realize Item Flows through the Item Flow Manager dialog

1. Select an Association, Connector,Control Flow , Object Flow, or Message in the diagram pane.
2. On the smart manipulator, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . The Item Flow Manager dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='item_flow_manager_realize.png']
3. Do one of the following:
  - Set the Realize column option to true for those Item Flows you want to realize.
  - Click the Realize All button, if you want to realize all Item Flows.
4. Click Close when you are finished.

To realize Item Flows from the Smart Manipulator toolbar

1. Select Association, Connector, Association,Control Flow , Object Flow, or Message in the diagram pane.
2. From the Smart Manipulator toolbar, select [ATTACHMENT filename='realize_all.png'] Realize All Item Flows.

##### Removing not realized Item Flows

If you remove the realizing elements from the model, the no longer used Item Flows are left as a residue. However, you can specify the **Remove Not Realized Item Flows** project option to remove ItemFlows automatically after the last element that realizes them is deleted.

To specify the Remove Not Realized Item Flows project option

1. In the Main toolbar, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. In the General tab, select SysML .
3. Set the **Remove Not Realized Item Flows**option to *true*. The default value is *false*.

To find all not realized Item Flows, you can run the 'SysML ValSuite - Ports and Flows'validation suite. The **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Not Realized Item Flow' space='']** validation rule will check for any residual Item Flows. 
[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Starting the validation' space='']>]]>

##### Navigating to diagrams where Item Flow is realized

To navigate to diagrams where Item Flow is realized

- Right-click the Item Flow element and in its shortcut menu, select Go To , then Usage in Diagrams , then Realizing Elements , then select the realizing element type. In its submenu, select a diagram in the displayed list.

If the diagrams are grayed out, this means there are no Conveyed Items of the selected Item Flow to be displayed. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="17ea95c0-fc33-43df-b62b-9809f99acd14"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[project option]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <strong>Show Item Flows on Affected Diagrams</strong> controls how the diagrams will be updated for newly created or modified flows. By default, the option is set to true to update all affected diagrams and show the Conveyed Items of Item Flows whenever a new flow is created or an existing one is modified.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">To learn more about previewing affected diagrams, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Preview Affected Diagrams dialog" /></ac:link>.</p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Creating_Item_Flows_3.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>Finish</strong>. An Item Flow with the selected Conveyed Item (and Item Property if it was defined) is created and is set for the Connector and the corresponding relationships if the <strong>Add Item Flow to corresponding relationships </strong>was selected.</p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d13fa5f5-7dfc-4e08-b899-febd3d68717b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to reverse the Item Flow direction and update it in all related diagrams, read <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Reversing Information Flow direction" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><span>Specifying the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane</span></h3><p>You can also specify a Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane if you have already created an Item Flow.</p><p>To specify the Conveyed Item directly on the diagram pane</p><hr /><ol><li>In the diagram palette, select the needed Conveyed Item.</li><li>Drag the selected Conveyed Item onto the Item Flow on the diagram pane. The <strong>Create/Edit Item Flow</strong> dialog opens. If the Item Flow already has Conveyed Item(s), the Conveyed Items field is appended with the new item.</li><li>Follow the steps of the dialog. See the <ac:link ac:anchor="Creating Item Flows" /> procedure steps 2-4.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish </strong>when you are done. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="set_as_conveyed_item.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><h3>Showing/hiding the Conveyed Item</h3><p>You can choose to show or hide the Conveyed Item on the element realizing the Item Flow (Association, Connector, Activity Edge, Message).</p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="12c70495-6cf3-4014-8e0f-351280f67916">To show/hide the Conveyed Item</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ol><li>On the diagram pane, <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="67802118-2580-4304-8bc8-4afe81397337">select the element realizing an Item Flow.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li>On the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Smart manipulator toolbar" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[smart manipulator toolbar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>:<ol><li>If the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a1378b15-1423-474f-a109-43b24ff8f93b">Conveyed Item is not displayed</ac:inline-comment-marker>, the tooltip for the button is<ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Show_Realized_Item_Flow.png" /></ac:image><strong>Show Realized Item Flows</strong>. Click it to show the Conveyed Item.</li><li>If the Conveyed Item<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="9afaee50-8621-4ed1-a546-1885f349de7c"> is displayed</ac:inline-comment-marker>, the tooltip for the button is <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Hide_Realized_Item_Flow.png" /></ac:image> <strong>Hide Realized Item Flows</strong>. Click it to hide the Conveyed Item.<br />If the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="76221ad9-f793-49a0-8cfb-c527c75f8dc9">element realizes only one Item Flow</ac:inline-comment-marker>, the Conveyed Item is displayed/hidden instantly with the click of a button.</li></ol></li><li>If the element realizes multiple Item Flows, the Conveyed Items menu opens. In it, do the following:<ol><li>Select the check boxes for the Item Flows whose Conveyed Item you want to be displayed.</li><li><p>Clear the check boxes for the Item Flows whose Conveyed Item you want to be hidden.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="efdf0cb5-5869-4fbe-9fa1-abaa8b869b62"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To select all check boxes, click the <strong>Select All </strong>button. To clear all check boxes, click the <strong>Clear All </strong>button.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Show_Hide_Flowing_Items.png" /></ac:image></li></ol></li></ol><p>When you hide the realized exchanges, you can choose whether you want to hide the arrow notation on the realizing elements. The <strong>Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information Is Missing</strong> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[project option]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> controls the arrow visibility.</p><p><br /></p><p><span>To change the arrow notation visibility on realizing element when the Conveyed Item is hidden or unspecified</span></p><hr /><ol><li>From the modeling tool main menu, choose <strong>Options </strong>&gt; <strong>Project</strong>. The <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the dialog, go to <strong>General </strong>&gt; <strong>Diagrams</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Diagrams </strong>property group, find the <strong>Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information Is Missing </strong>option and set it to <em>true</em>.</li><li>Click <strong>OK </strong>when you are done.</li></ol><h3>Realizing Item Flows</h3><p>You can realize hidden Item Flows in two ways:</p><ul><li>Through the Item Flow Manager.</li><li>From the selected relationship's Smart Manipulator toolbar.<br /><br /></li></ul><p>To realize Item Flows through the Item Flow Manager dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an Association, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Connector,<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Control Flow</span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">, Object Flow, </span>or Message in the diagram pane.</li><li>On the smart manipulator, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper confluence-embedded-manual-size"> <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="14"><ri:attachment ri:filename="item_flow_manager_icon.png" /></ac:image> </span>. The <strong>Item Flow</strong> <strong> Manager</strong> dialog opens.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="item_flow_manager_realize.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Set the <strong>Realize</strong> column option to true for those Item Flows you want to realize.</li><li>Click the <strong>Realize All</strong> button, if you want to realize all Item Flows.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong> when you are finished.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To realize Item Flows from the Smart Manipulator toolbar</p><hr /><ol><li>Select Association, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Connector, Association,<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Control Flow</span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">, Object Flow, </span>or Message in the diagram pane.</li><li>From the Smart Manipulator toolbar, select <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="realize_all.png" /></ac:image> Realize All Item Flows.</li></ol><h3><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Removing not realized Item Flows</span></h3><p>If you remove the realizing elements from the model, the no longer used Item Flows are left as a residue. However, you can specify the <strong>Remove Not Realized Item Flows</strong> project option to remove Item<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"> Flows automatically after the last element that realizes them is deleted.</span></p><p>To specify the <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Remove Not Realized Item Flows project option</span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the Main toolbar, click <strong>Options </strong>&gt; <strong>Project</strong>. The <strong>Project Options </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>General </strong>tab, select <strong>SysML</strong>.</li><li>Set the <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><strong>Remove Not Realized Item Flows </strong>option to <em>true</em>. The default value is <em>false</em>.</span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">To find all not realized Item Flows, you can run the 'SysML ValSuite - Ports and Flows' </span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">validation suite. The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Not Realized Item Flow" /></ac:link></strong> validation rule will check for any residual Item Flows.<br /><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Starting the validation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about starting the validation >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </span></p><h3><span>Navigating to diagrams where Item Flow is realized</span></h3><p>To navigate to diagrams where Item Flow is realized</p><hr /><ul><li>Right-click the Item Flow element and in its shortcut menu, select <strong>Go To</strong>, then <strong>Usage in Diagrams</strong>, then <strong>Realizing Elements</strong>, then select the realizing element type. In its submenu, select a diagram in the displayed list.</li></ul><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189146668 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00075: Creating nested parts by pasting items from the list

- page_id: `189146668`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189146668/Creating+nested+parts+by+pasting+items+from+the+list
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-07-27T16:47:56.529+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Modeling structure with Blocks > Decomposing Blocks
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1940928771

1940928782

1940928772

When you have an indented list of the items you want to create as parts in the diagram, you can copy the list and paste it in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] diagram pane.

You can copy the list from a text editor (Word, Notepad, etc.), web page, PDF file, etc.

To create parts by pasting items from the list

1. Copy the list.
2. Go to the diagram and press Ctrl+V.
3. In the opened menu, select Element .
4. Select Part Property (or other Property) element type.
5. In the question dialog click Yes if you need to create nested parts.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The nested parts are created according to the copied list

1940928770

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating elements from other resources' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']

page, PDF file, etc.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create parts by pasting items from the list</p><hr /><ol><li>Copy the list.</li><li>Go to the diagram and press Ctrl+V.</li><li>In the opened menu, select <strong>Element</strong>.</li><li>Select Part Property (or other Property) element type.</li><li>In the question dialog click <strong>Yes</strong> if you need to create nested parts.</li></ol><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="nested_parts.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating nested parts by pasting items from the list" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189146828 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00077: Creating new Rollup Pattern Block

- page_id: `189146828`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189146828/Creating+new+Rollup+Pattern+Block
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T14:46:03.834+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Modeling structure with Blocks > Rollup Pattern Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1348678326

1348678329

1348678328

You can apply the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Default Rollup Pattern Blocks' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] or anewly created one by using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Rollup Pattern Wizard' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']**Rollup Pattern** **Wizard**. If you want to apply different kinds of calculations than [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Default Rollup Pattern Blocks' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']default rollup patterns for your model, you can create a different type of rollup pattern Block. To do this, copy any default rollup pattern Block, paste it in the model, change its name, and specify all required value properties. The procedure below describes how to create a different kind of rollup pattern Block.

To define a different kind of rollup pattern (e.g.,TimeRollupPattern)

1. Find the default rollup pattern Blocks under the auxiliary resources in your project. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Default Rollup Pattern Blocks' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']]]>
2. Copy any default rollup pattern Block (e.g., CostRollupPattern). [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Paste it in your model (e.g. under the RollUp Pattern Package). [IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. The copy of the pattern Block is created *(*e.g. *Cost**RollupPattern1*). [IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Rename the pattern Block, value properties, part property, Constraint Block, and Parametric diagram according to your needs (e.g., Block *TimeRollupPattern*, Constraint Block *totalTime*, properties*time*).
6. Define the Type property value of a value property in the Specification window according to your needs (e.g., from *Real* to *Seconds*). The different type of rollup pattern Block *(*e.g., *Time**RollupPattern*) is created (see the figure below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1348678325

**Related pages**

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Rollup Pattern Wizard' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']

**Webinar**

- Total Mass, Cost, and Power Rollups

**Sample model**

The sample model used in the figures of this page is the **Laptop Cost Analysis** that comes with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation' space='CST2024xR2']. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download *[ATTACHMENT filename='LaptopCostAnalysis.mdzip'].*
- Find in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\simulation\LaptopCostAnalysis.mdzip

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

/></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="69feb8e4-4da4-4276-8788-5f05f6c47e3b"><ac:parameter class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The sample model used in the figures of this page is the <strong>Laptop Cost Analysis</strong> that comes with <ac:link><ri:page this sample do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Download<span class="confluence-link"><em> <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="LaptopCostAnalysis.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="_sample models" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.<br /></em></span></li><li>Find in modeling tool <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\simulation\LaptopCostAnalysis.mdzip</em></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189147293 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00080: Creating pins automatically for Opaque Action

- page_id: `189147293`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189147293/Creating+pins+automatically+for+Opaque+Action
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2019-10-15T13:51:26.054+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Activity Diagram' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'], you can create an Opaque Action and define a formula. The inputs and output should be shown as pins on that Opaque Action. You can automatically create pins.

To create Pins automatically using the **Parse and Create Pin**button

1. Create a new Opaque Action shape or select an existing one.
2. Type a constraint expression or a new variable for an existing expression.
3. Click the shape and from the smart manipulator toolbar, select [ATTACHMENT filename='parse_and_create_pins.png'] .
4. Pins are created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### On top border of the Opaque Action, the input pins x and y are created, and on the bottom border, the output pin is created.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Activity Diagram" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ac:link>, you can create an Opaque Action and define a formula. The inputs and output should be shown as pins on that Opaque Action. You can automatically create pins.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create Pins automatically using the <strong>Parse and Create Pin </strong>button</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a new Opaque Action shape or select an existing one.</li><li>Type a constraint expression or a new variable for an existing expression.</li><li>Click the shape and from the smart manipulator toolbar, select <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="parse_and_create_pins.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating pins automatically for Opaque Action" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.</li><li>Pins are created.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="parse_and_create_pins_example.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating pins automatically for Opaque Action" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">On top border of the Opaque Action, the input pins x and y are created, and on the bottom border, the output pin is created.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189146675 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00081: Creating ports

- page_id: `189146675`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189146675/Creating+ports
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T14:46:00.844+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Modeling structure with Blocks > Defining interfaces using ports
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

300216385

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

300216387

300222072

**On this page**

43

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

300216386

##### Introduction

You can create the following kinds of ports by using the SysML Plugin:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space=''] - expose features of the owning Block or its internal Part Properties.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Full Port' space=''] - support their own features.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Port' space=''] - since it is deprecated, you use the Proxy Port typed by the Interface Block instead of the non-atomic Flow Port.

##### Creating ports

The procedure below is suitable for all kinds of ports.

To create a port, do one of the following:

- From the diagram palette, select the Proxy Port , Full Port , or Flow Port button and click the element on the diagram pane.
- Select an element on the diagram pane and from the smart manipulator toolbar, click the Proxy Port , Full Port , or Flow Port button.
- In the Block [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='MD2024xR2'] , select the Ports/Interfaces property group, click the Create button, and select Proxy Port , Full Port , or Flow Port button.

You can also create[Proxy Ports](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/SYSMLP2024xR2/Proxy+Port)at both ends of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MD2024xR2']by dragging the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface Block' space='']. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connecting parts through interface' space='']>]]>

##### Converting the Part Property to Full Port

A Full Port is a part on a boundary. You can convert your Parts Properties into Full Ports by dragging them to the diagram frame.

#### INFO: Note

Note

All ports, connectors, and other information, including the layout, will remain unchanged.

To convert a Part Property to the Full Port

1. Select the Part Property shape and drag it to the diagram frame.
2. When the diagram frame is highlighted in blue, drop the shape. 
The Full Port is created on the diagram frame. NoteWhen the Full Port is created on the diagram frame, you cannot drag it back (except to Undo the conversion).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Part Property converted to the Full Port.

##### Creating a port while creating a Connector

The procedure below is suitable for all kinds of ports.

To create a port automatically when drawing a Connector from a port to a Part Property

1. Select a port shape on the diagram pane.
2. On the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. Select Part Property shape appears. The menu appears if a new port can be created on the Part Property or it has hidden ports.
3. From the Select Port menu, select one of the following commands:

- None - if you want to connect the Connector straight to the part.
- On a port in the list. Hidden ports of the part are listed. The selected port will be represented on the diagram and the connector will be connected to it.
- New Port - to create a new port with the same name, type, and multiplicity as the port from which the connector is drawn.
- New Nested Port - to create a new nested port. Note that the command exists only if the connector is drawn from the nested port.

You can draw a Connector to a port, too, and the menu appears. As a result, the nested port is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The **Select Port** menu opens after drawing the Connector from the *CANPort* Port to Part Property typed by *GPS Module* Block.

300216384

**Related pages**

- Displaying parts and ports
- Connecting parts through interface
- Autowiring function
- Layout templates
- Refactoring
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Full Port' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Port' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Connector' space='MD2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface Block' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='']

**Port features**

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures on this page is the **diagram aspects** sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download *[ATTACHMENT filename='diagram aspects.mdzip'].*
- Find in modeling tool <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML\diagram aspects.mdzip.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189147026 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00083: Creating reusable constraint libraries

- page_id: `189147026`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189147026/Creating+reusable+constraint+libraries
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T14:46:05.593+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Modeling parametric constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can add or create constraints in a Package, such as a library, and export it as a new project, so that you can reuse the library in other projects.

The following sections present instructions on how to export a package as a new project and how to work with shared packages:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exporting packages to new projects' space='MD2024xR2']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sharing project data' space='MD2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can add or create constraints in a Package, such as a library, and export it as a new project, so that you can reuse the library in other projects.</p><p>The following sections present instructions on how to export a package as a new project and how to work with shared packages:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Exporting packages to new projects" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Sharing project data" /></ac:link></li></ul>
````

## PAGE 00084: Creating Signal receptions for Messages

- page_id: `189147135`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189146905 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00085: Creating structure models by behavior models

- page_id: `189146905`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189146905/Creating+structure+models+by+behavior+models
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-09-10T16:54:24.992+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Behavior to structure synchronization
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A structure model can be automatically built according to the existing behavior model. Once the command is initiated, the structure model, including missing interfaces (together with Proxy Ports typed by compatible Interface Blocks), Connectors, and Item Flows is created. Additionally, elements are displayed in the newly-created Internal Block Diagram. As a result, there is no need to manually define the communication in an Internal Block Diagram once again if you have already created an Activity Diagram.

#### WARNING: Important

Important

If the **Create Internal Block Diagram by Behavior Model** command is not available under **Tools** in the Block shortcut menu, make sure:

- The Allocation Mode is set to Usage ( **Options** >**Project** > **SysML** > **Allocation** Mode ).
- The selected Block contains typed Part Properties that communicate with other Part Properties in Activities.

To create a structure model by the behavior model

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] , right-click a Block.
  - On the diagram pane, right-click a Block shape.
2. In the open shortcut menu, select Tools > Create Internal Block Diagram by Behavior Model . [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_ibd_by_behavior_model.png']

A structure model is automatically created according to the defined behavior model, and compatible elements are displayed on the Internal Block Diagram pane.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A structure model can be automatically built according to the existing behavior model. Once the command is initiated, the structure model, including missing interfaces (together with Proxy Ports typed by compatible Interface Blocks), Connectors, and Item Flows is created. Additionally, elements are displayed in the newly-created Internal Block Diagram. As a result, there is no need to manually define the communication in an Internal Block Diagram once again if you have already created an Activity Diagram.  </p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="88cdcbe9-295e-4d19-bddc-f507bc82474a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the <strong>Create Internal Block Diagram by Behavior Model</strong> command is not available under <strong>Tools</strong> in the Block shortcut menu, make sure:</p><ul><li>The <strong>Allocation Mode</strong> is set to <strong>Usage </strong>(<strong><strong>Options</strong> &gt;<strong> Project</strong> &gt; <strong>SysML</strong> &gt; <strong>Allocation</strong></strong> <strong>Mode</strong>).</li><li><p>The selected Block contains typed Part Properties that communicate with other Part Properties in Activities.</p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>To create a structure model by the behavior model</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span>, right-click a Block. </span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">On the diagram pane, right-click a Block shape.</p></li></ul></li><li>In the open shortcut menu, select<strong> Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Create Internal Block Diagram by Behavior Model</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_ibd_by_behavior_model.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating structure models by behavior models" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p>A structure model is automatically created according to the defined behavior model, and compatible elements are displayed on the Internal Block Diagram pane. </p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189146482 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00086: Creating SysML projects

- page_id: `189146482`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189146482/Creating+SysML+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-05-03T14:03:13.726+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > Getting started
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are three ways to create the SysML projects:

##### Creating the blank SysML project

To create a blank SysML project

1. Do one of the following:
  - Select **File**> **New** **Project**.
  - On the main toolbar, click the **New** **Project** button.
  - Press Ctr+Shiftl+N.
2. In the **New****Project** dialog, under **Systems Engineering**, select **SysML****Project**.
3. Specify name and location.
4. Click **OK** when you are done. 
The blank project is created and stored in your file system with defined name. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Creating SysML project from template

To create a SysML project from a template

1. Do one of the following:
  - Select **File**>**New** **Project**.
  - On the main toolbar, click the **New** **Project** button.
  - Press Ctr+Shiftl+N.
2. In the **New****Project** dialog, under **Other**, select **Project from Template**.
3. Specify name and location.
4. Select the **SysML** template.
5. Click **OK** when you are done. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Creating MagicGrid projects

To create a MagicGrid project

1. Do one of the following:
  - Select **File**>**New** **Project**.
  - On the main toolbar, click the **New** **Project** button.
  - Press Ctr+Shiftl+N.
2. In the **New****Project** dialog, under **Systems Engineering**, select either:
  1. **MagicGrid QuickStart** - to create a project with a structure and guidelines on how to use the MagicGrid approach.
  2. **MagicGrid Blank**- to create a blank project with a predefined package structure of the MagicGrid approach.
  3. **MagicGrid v2 QuickStart** - to create a project with a structure and guidelines on how to use the MagicGrid v2 approach.
  4. **MagicGrid v2 Blank**- to create a blank project with a predefined package structure of the MagicGrid v2 approach.
3. Specify name and location.
4. Click **OK** when you are done. When youcreatea new MagicGrid project, a question dialog is prompted, asking you to switch the perspective. Clicking **Yes**changes the modeling tool's perspective to the MagicGrid perspective. The MagicGrid project is created and stored in your file system with a defined name. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
