diagram.</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span> <span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span><br /><span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span></span></span></span><br /></span></li></ul><p><span><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="The Display Parameters/Properties dialog of the Coffee Grinder Block with the four highlighted main sections of the dialog: Toolbar, Element tree, Panel, and Options area."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_parameters_properties_dialog.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></span></span></span></span></span></p><p><strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></strong></p><p>The following procedure outlines working with the <span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Display Parameters/Properties</strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="29785cb7-de15-492f-ae5a-25c23ea254d4"> </ac:inline-comment-marker></span></span> dialog when you want to display required parameters and properties on the diagram pane.</p><p><br /></p><p>To display parameters and properties using the <span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Display Parameters/Properties</strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="29785cb7-de15-492f-ae5a-25c23ea254d4"> </ac:inline-comment-marker></span></span>dialog</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Open the <span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Display Parameters/Properties</strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="29785cb7-de15-492f-ae5a-25c23ea254d4"> </ac:inline-comment-marker></span></span> dialog:</p><ul><li>When creating a new <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Parametric Diagram" /></ac:link></span>, the dialog opens automatically.<span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span></li><li>When editing an already created<span class="confluence-link"> </span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Parametric Diagram" /></ac:link></span>, the dialog can be opened in the following ways:<br />- Select either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes, and, o<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">n the diagram toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image></span> and choose <span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Display Parameters/Properties</strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="29785cb7-de15-492f-ae5a-25c23ea254d4"> </ac:inline-comment-marker></span></span>.</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br />- Right-click either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes, and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Display Parameters/Properties<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="29785cb7-de15-492f-ae5a-25c23ea254d4"> </ac:inline-comment-marker></span></strong>.<br /><br /></span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span>(Optional) Enable buttons from the <span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Display Parameters/Properties</strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="29785cb7-de15-492f-ae5a-25c23ea254d4"> </ac:inline-comment-marker></span></span>dialog toolbar if you want to show or hide additional information about listed elements:<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span><ul><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Display the full path of the elements"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_the_full_path_of_the_elements.png" /></ac:image></span> to show the full path of elements.<span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <br /></span></span></li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Display the qualified name of type in the typed elements text"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_the_qualified_name_of_type_in_the typed_elements_text.png" /></ac:image></span> to show the full types of elements.</li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Show Inherited"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_inherited_button.png" /></ac:image></span> to show the inherited elements.<span class="confluence-link"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Show Private Inherited"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Show_private_inherited.png" /></ac:image></span> to show the inherited properties.</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></li></ul></li><li><p>Choose the properties you want to display by using one or more of the following methods:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ebed955-61f3-499b-9133-06607c2df4c4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span>If some <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span>check boxes</span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span> are inactive in the element list, </span></span></span>those elements are already <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span>displayed</span></span></span> in the diagram.<br /></span></span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Use the element tree<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> located on <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">the left side of the dialog:</span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">-</span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Manually select individual check boxes<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">one by one.</span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span><br />- Select recursively nested elements by holding down the Shift key while selecting the corresponding check box for the element.<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span></span><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0493b945-788e-4921-bc9c-25ecc6a7fa93"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 1</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>All <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Constraint Parameter" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Parameters]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><em> C</em>, <em>gt</em>, <em>h</em> of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /></ac:link> typed by <em>Grinding</em> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Constraint Block" /></ac:link> are selected recursively by holding down the Shift key while selecting the corresponding check box from the element tree<span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> in the <strong>Display Parameters/Properties</strong> dialog.</span></span><sub><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <br /></span></span></sub></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Display Parameters/Properties dialog."><ri:attachment ri:filename="recursively_selected_nested_elements_par.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Use the context-sensitive panel<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> located on the right side of the dialog:</span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">- <strong>Select Properties</strong> group - displays all property types <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">(including <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> your own</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[stereotypes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>)</span> that are owned by the selected element <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">from the element tree  located on the left side of the dialog</span>.<br />- <strong>Select Parameters</strong> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">group - displays all parameter types <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">(including your own stereotypes)</span> that are owned by selected element <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog</span>.</span><br /></span>- <strong>Select All</strong> - selects all check boxes that are expanded <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog.</span><br />- <strong>Clear All</strong> - clears all check boxes, including those that are collapsed (deep nested), <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">from the element tree</span> located on <span>the left side of the dialog.</span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="expand" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="728daf61-336a-4786-ba52-fe698621106c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 2</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">All <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Constraint Parameter" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Parameters]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> C, gt, and h of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /></ac:link> typed by <em>Grinding</em> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Constraint Block" /></ac:link> are selected <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">in <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">the element tree</span></span></span> by using the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Select parameters</strong> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">group</span></span> in the context-sensitive panel </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">of the <strong>Display Parameters/Properties</strong> dialog.</span></span></span></p><p><ac:image ac:title="Using the context-sensitive panel of the Display Parameters/Properties dialog when trying to select required parameters."><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_parameters_using_context_sensitive_panel.png" /></ac:image></p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><sub><span><span style="color: rgb(68,68,68);"> </span></span></sub></span></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span>Specify the layout options in the <strong>Options</strong> area:<br /><ul><li>Properties layout: make sure the <strong>Layout Parts</strong> check box is selected; otherwise, the property shapes will not be arranged in the automatic layout.</li><li>Usage of layout template: make sure the <strong>Use Layout Template</strong> check box is selected; otherwise, the layout template will not be applied. To apply the layout template, check the <strong>User Layout Template </strong>box.If you leave this check box selected, the selection in the dialog works in this way: when selecting the Constraint Property, which is included in the layout template, all it's from the layout template are selected automatically.</li><li>Port/parameters layout on Part/Constraint Property shape:<br />- <strong>Top/Bottom</strong> - displays ports/parameters on the Part/Constraint Property shape from top to bottom.<br /><ac:image ac:title="The Constraint Parameters h, C, and gt are displayed on the Constraint Property shape typed by Grinding Constraint Block from top to bottom." ac:alt="The Constraint Parameters h, C, and gt are displayed on the Constraint Property shape typed by Grinding Constraint Block from top to bottom."><ri:attachment ri:filename="parameter_layout_on_part_shape_top_bottom.png" /></ac:image><br />- <strong>Left/Right </strong> - displays ports/parameters on the Part/Constraint Property shape from left to right.<br /><ac:image ac:title="The Constraint Parameters h, C, and gt are displayed on the Constraint Property shape typed by Grinding Constraint Block from left to right." ac:alt="The Constraint Parameters h, C, and gt are displayed on the Constraint Property shape typed by Grinding Constraint Block from left to right."><ri:attachment ri:filename="parameter_layout_on_part_shape_left_right.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</span></span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">After applying the steps outlined above, all <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span>parameters and properties that were selected in the <strong>Display Parameters/Properties</strong> dialog are displayed on the diagram pane.<br /></span></span></p><h4>Using the diagram toolbar and the shortcut menu</h4><p class="confluence-link">In the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Parametric Diagram" /></ac:link></span> that have already been created, you can display<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Constraint Parameter" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link">Constraint</span> Parameters</span></ac:link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, or other properties</span></span> and hide all Constraint Parameters by using the commands from:</p><ul><li class="confluence-link">The <strong>Display </strong>button on the diagram toolbar.</li><li class="confluence-link">The <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Display</strong> command group under the shortcut menu.</span></li></ul><p>You can also use <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span>the </span><strong>Display </strong>button</span></span></span> and <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Display</strong> command group from <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">the shortcut menu as</span></span> their functions are identical. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">All those commands</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span>are interactive and <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">depend upon the selection on the diagram pane. For example, if the Constraint Property shape is selected on the diagram pane, the menu contains <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">only </span>the commands that can be applied to the selected <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Constraint </span> Property shape. If you select nothing in the diagram pane, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">the menu</span> shows<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> commands that can be applied</span> to the whole diagram.</span></span></p><p>These <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link">commands under the <strong>Display</strong> button and shortcut menu are <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">comprised of </span></span></span>elements that are still not displayed on the diagram according to their:</p><ul><li>Metatypes.</li><li>Stereotypes.</li><li>Types stereotypes.</li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link">The following procedures show:</span></span></p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="button"><ac:link-body>How to display Constraint Parameters, Constraint Properties using the <strong>Display</strong> button.</ac:link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="shortcut"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to display Constraint Parameters, Constraint Properties using the shortcut menu.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="hide"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to hide Constraint Parameters.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="151e0ef2-cf5f-42c8-9b7b-b70191dd1dcd"><ac:parameter ac:name="">button</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>To display <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Constraint</span> Parameters, Constraint Properties</span></span> using the <strong>Display</strong> button</p><hr /><ol><li>Select either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes.</li><li>O<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">n the diagram toolbar, click </span><ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image>.<br /><ac:image ac:title="After selecting the Thermal Calculations Parametric diagram pane and clicking the Display button, opened menu allows to display properties and/or parameters."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_related_elements_button.png" /></ac:image></li><li><p>From the opened command menu, select the required properties or parameters:</p><p><br />        - All Parameters that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Parameters</strong> command group.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ef07d2a-8928-47ac-9bee-363460e3bac9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7319cc0e-6a3c-4e44-9809-e9817c6a997b">If nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7319cc0e-6a3c-4e44-9809-e9817c6a997b">Display Parameters</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7319cc0e-6a3c-4e44-9809-e9817c6a997b"> command group will contain commands that allow you to display two levels of structure: Constraint Parameters are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first level Constraint Properties.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>        - All properties that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Properties</strong> command group.</p><p><span><span>After selecting the required command from the</span></span> <strong>Display</strong> button<span><span>, the appropriate properties and parameters are displayed on the diagram pane.</span></span></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f27456dd-5abb-464b-b57a-d14bee0cbd81"><ac:parameter ac:name="">shortcut</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>To display parameters, properties using the shortcut menu</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click either the diagram pane or single/multiple element shapes.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, select the <strong>Display</strong> command group.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="The Display command group menu allows to display Constraint Parameters of the selected Constraint Property shape." ac:alt="The Display command group menu allows to display Constraint Parameters of the selected Constraint Property shape."><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display_command_parameters.png" /></ac:image></li><li><p>From the opened command menu, select the required parameters:<br />        - All parameters that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Parameters </strong>command group.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="327c3ff9-f8cc-4d9e-a0b5-0c763e6c790e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the <strong>Display Parameters </strong>command group allows you to display two levels of structure: ports are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first level parts.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>        - All properties that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Properties</strong> command group.<br />After selecting the required command from the <strong>Display</strong> command menu, the appropriate properties and parameters are displayed on the diagram pane.<br /><br /></p></li></ol><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4794c2e6-db49-41b6-ac94-03bcb79efedc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">hide</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>To hide <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Constraint Parameter" /><ac:link-body>Constraint Parameters<span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span></ac:link-body></ac:link><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><br /></span></p><hr /><ul><li>Select either a diagram pane or any number of <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /></ac:link> shapes. O<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">n the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image>  and choose<strong> Hide All Parameters</strong>.</span></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Right-click either a diagram pane or any number of Constraint Property shapes. Select <strong>Display</strong> &gt;<strong> Hide All Parameters</strong>.<br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">After selecting the command, the parameters of the selected shape will be hidden from the diagram pane. However, they will not be removed from the model</span>.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff629db0-f28c-4b9f-aa61-48e542d03bba"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the <strong>Hide All Parameters </strong>command does not appear, the selection or <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">diagram </span>does not contain any parameters that can be hidden.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:image ac:title="Hiding all Constraint Parameters of the Constraint Property typed by Boiler Water Mass Constraint Block." ac:alt="Hiding all Constraint Parameters of the Constraint Property typed by Boiler Water Mass Constraint Block."><ri:attachment ri:filename="hide_all_parameters.png" /></ac:image><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></p></li></ul><h4>Using the smart manipulator toolbar<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></h4><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link" style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">When the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> are already displayed on the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Parametric Diagram" /></ac:link></span><span class="confluence-link">, </span>you can display their <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Constraint Parameter" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Parameters]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> using the <strong>Display All Parameters </strong>button from the smart manipulator toolbar. Follow the steps below to learn how to display Constraint Parameters of a selected Constraint Property shape.</span></span></span></span></span></p><p>To display <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link">Constraint Parameters</span></span></span></span></span> on <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link">Constraint Property shape</span></span></span></span></span></p><hr /><ol><li>Select<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span> the </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link">Constraint Property shape</span></span></span></span></span> or a diagram frame.</span> </span></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">  From the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Display All Parameters" ac:alt="Display All Parameters"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_all_parameters_button.png" /></ac:image>.<br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span>All <span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">first level</span></span></span></span></span></span></span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link">Constraint Parameters</span></span></span></span></span> are displayed on the selected Constraint Property shape.</span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="feb6590f-3710-4154-9154-2c9ce8edfa7e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p> If the <strong>Display All Parameters </strong>button is not available, the selected element or <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">diagram </span>does not own any <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link">Constraint Parameters</span></span></span></span></span>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <ac:image ac:title="The Display All Parameters button on the smart manipulator toolbar of the Constraint Property shape typed by Boiler Water Mass Constraint Block." ac:alt="The Display All Parameters button on the smart manipulator toolbar of the Constraint Property shape typed by Boiler Water Mass Constraint Block."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_all_parameters_smart_manipulator.png" /></ac:image></span><br /><br /></p></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">After completing the steps outlined above, the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Property]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with all its <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Constraint Parameter" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Parameters]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and/or <span class="confluence-link" style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Part Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> with ports (<span class="confluence-link">including deep nested ports</span>), <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Value Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Value Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span> can be displayed on the <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Parametric Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Parametric diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>.</span></p><h3>Displaying properties of a Constraint Parameter</h3><p>If you display properties of a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Constraint Parameter" /></ac:link>, all properties of its type will be displayed inside the Constraint Parameter shape and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part Property" /></ac:link> compartments will be enabled for that parameter.</p><p><br /></p><p>To enable Part Property compartments on the Constraint Parameter shape</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Constraint Parameter shape whose type has properties.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>O<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">n the diagram toolbar, click </span><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image></span>.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, point to the <strong>Display</strong> command group.</li></ul></li><li>Select the required properties to display.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The properties are displayed inside the Constraint Parameter shape. Part Property compartments are enabled for the Constraint Parameter.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="23cba5ff-d4fc-4e54-b1e0-21e35681a640"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">19420878</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><a href="http://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Stereotype#Stereotype-Applyingastereotype" rel="nofollow">SysML Parametric Diagram</a></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Constraint Parameters automatically" /></ac:link> </li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Applying Stereotypes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </li><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying elements" /></ac:link></p></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Property path notation" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures of this page is the <strong>Coffee Machine</strong> sample model that comes with Cameo Simulation Toolkit. To open this sample do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Download<span class="confluence-link"><em> <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="CoffeeMachine.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="_sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.<br /></em></span></li><li>Find in modeling tool <em><span>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\<span>samples\simulation</span><span>\<span>CoffeeMachine</span>.mdzip.</span></span></em></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189146622 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00121: Displaying parts and ports

- page_id: `189146622`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189146622/Displaying+parts+and+ports
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T14:45:59.965+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Modeling structure with Blocks > Decomposing Blocks
- labels: ['display-parts', 'display-ports', 'hide-ports', 'enable-part-compartments']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Example 4

1096370502

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Example 4

1096370507

1096370497

**On this page**

43

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Example 4

1096370506

You can display ports, properties, and deep nested ports and properties that have already been created in all [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram descriptions' space='']structure diagrams.This functionality is driven by stereotypes, which allow you to display properties [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024xR2'] have been applied to. If you have applied your own [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024xR2'] for the elements (e.g., electrical, mechanical, optical), you can use those stereotypeswhen selecting the properties you want to display on the diagram pane.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

For more information about creating custom stereotypes and applying them, see [UML Profiling and DSL Guide](http://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/UML+Profiling+and+DSL+Guide).

This page contains the following information:

43

##### Ways to display parts and ports

You can display ports and properties in the following ways:

44

###### Using the Display Parts/Ports dialog

You can use the**Display Parts/Ports**dialog to control how much of a structure should be displayed. The **Display Parts/Ports**dialog****opens automatically when creating a new[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''] for a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='']Block element that owns ports and properties. It can also be accessed and opened in thestructurediagrams that have already been created.The**Display Parts/Ports** dialog allows you to:

- Preview all [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space=''] , ports , and other properties in one place.
- Manually or automatically select the required parts , ports , and other properties you want to display.
- Manually or automatically choose the deeply [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Nesting parts that are association ends' space='MD2024xR2'] parts , ports , and other properties you want to display.
- Define layout options for parts .
- Specify layout options for ports .

The **Display Parts/Ports**dialog consists of the following areas:

- *Dialog toolbar* allows you to show or hide additional information about listed elements.
- *Element tree* displays all owned internal structure of a context [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='']Block.
- *Panel* is a context-sensitive area thatdepends on the selected property from the Element tree. It allows you to batch select parts/portsin the Element tree. You canselect all, clear all, or choose properties only by type, stereotype, or aspect.
- *Options* area allows you to choose the layout of the selected parts and portswhen they appear in the diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

****

The following procedure will guide you in working with the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog when you want to display required ports and properties on the diagram pane.

To display ports and properties using the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog

1. Open the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog:
  - When creating a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''] , the dialog opens automatically.
  - When editing an already created [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram descriptions' space='']structure diagram , the dialog can be opened in the following ways: - Select either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes. O n the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and choose **Display Parts/Ports**. - Right-click either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes, and select **Display** > **Display Parts/Ports**.
2. (Optional) Enable buttons from the Display Parts/Ports dialog toolbar if you want to show or hide additional information about listed elements:
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to show the full path of elements.
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to show the full types of elements.
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to show the inherited elements.
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to show the inherited properties.
3. Choose the properties you want to display, using one or more of the following methods: WarningIf certain check boxesare inactive in the element list,those elements are already displayed in the diagram.
  - Use the element treelocated on the left side of the dialog:-Manually select individual check boxesone by one. 
- Select recursively nested elements by holding down the Shift key while selecting the corresponding check box for the element. Example 1All properties (in this example all ports*AirFlowPort, ElectricityPort, AutomotiveBusesPort*) of the *Air Distribution* Part Property are selected recursively by holding down the Shift key while selecting the corresponding check box from the element treein the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - Use the context-sensitive panellocated on the right side of the dialog:- **Select Properties** group - displays all property types (including your own [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='MD2024xR2']) that are owned by the selected element from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog. 
- **Select Ports** group - displays all port types (including your own stereotypes) that are owned by the selected element from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog.- **Select All** - selects all check boxes that are expanded from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog. 
- **Clear All** - clears all check boxes, even those that are collapsed (deep nested), from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog. Example 2All Electrical ports (in this example only one *ElectricityPort* port) of the *Data Transfer* Part Property are selected in the element tree by using the **Select Ports** group in the context-sensitive panelof the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
4. Specify the layout options in the Options area:
  - Parts layout: select the Layout Parts check box if you want the Part Property shapes to be arranged in the automatic layout.
  - Usage of layout template: select the Use Layout Template check box if you want the layout template to be applied. When this check box is selected, the Part Property, which is included in the layout template, and all its Ports from the layout template are selected automatically.
  - Showing connectors: select the Show Connectors check box if you want the connectors to be displayed.
  - Ports layout on Part Property shape: - Top/Bottom - displays ports on the Part Property shape from top to bottom. [ATTACHMENT filename='port_layout_top_bottom.png'] - Left/Right - displays ports on the Part Property shape from left to right. [ATTACHMENT filename='port_layout_left_right.png']
5. Click **OK**.

After those steps, all parts, ports, other properties that were selected in the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog are displayed on the diagram pane.

###### Using the diagram toolbar and the shortcut menu

In the structure diagrams that have already been created, you can display ports, properties, and hide all ports by using the commands from:

- The Display button on the diagram toolbar.
- The **Display** command group under the shortcut menu.

You can also use the**Display**button and **Display** command group from the shortcut menu as their functions are identical. All those commandsare interactive and depend upon the selection on the diagram pane. For example, if the Part Property shape is selected on the diagram pane, the menu contains onlythe commands that can be applied to the selected Part Property shape. If you select nothing in the diagram pane, the menu showscommands that can be applied to the whole diagram.

These commands under the **Display** button and shortcut menu are comprised ofelements that are still not displayed on the diagram according to their:

- Metatypes.
- Stereotypes.
- Types stereotypes.

The following procedures will show you how to:

- How to display Ports, Properties using the **Display** button.
- 
- 

**button**To display ports, properties using the **Display** button

1. Select either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes.
2. O n the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Display.png'] . [ATTACHMENT filename='display_related_element.png']
3. From the opened command menu, select required properties or ports to display: 
 - All ports that are available to display are listed under the **Display Ports** command group. ImportantIf nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the**Display Ports**command group will contain commands that allow you to display two levels of structure: ports are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first level parts. - All properties that are available to display are listed under the **Display Properties** command group. After selecting the required command from the **Display** button, the appropriate properties are displayed on the diagram pane.

**shortcut**To display ports, properties using the shortcut menu

1. Right-click either the diagram pane or single/multiple element shapes.
2. From the shortcut menu, select the Display command group. [ATTACHMENT filename='Display_command.png']
3. From the opened command menu, select required properties to display: 
 - All Ports that are available to display are listed under the **Display Ports** command group. ImportantIf nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the **Display Ports** command group allows you to display two levels of structure: ports are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first level parts. - All Properties that are available to display are listed under the **Display Properties** command group. 
After selecting the required command from the **Display** command menu, the appropriate properties' ports are displayed on the diagram pane.

**hide**To hide ports

- Select either a diagram pane or any number of element shapes. O n the diagram toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and choose**Hide All Ports**.
- Right-click either a diagram pane or any number of element shapes, and select **Display** >**Hide All Ports**. 
After selecting the command, the portsof the selected shape will be hidden from the diagram pane, but not removed from the model. WarningIf the **Hide All Ports** command doesn't appear, the selection or diagramdoesn't contain any portsthat can be hidden. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Using the smart manipulator toolbar

When the properties are already displayed on the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram descriptions' space='']structure diagrams, you can display their direct ports using the **Display All Ports** button from the smart manipulator toolbar. It only allows you to display first level ports, and does not allow you to display deeply nested ports. To display deeply nested ports, you should use the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog. The steps below show how to display ports of a selected property shape.

To display Ports on shapes

1. Select the required element shape or a diagram frame.
2. From the smart manipulator toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. 
All first level Ports of the selected shape will be displayed on the diagram pane. WarningIf the **Display All Ports** button is not available, the selected element or diagramdoesn't own any Port. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Using the drag-and-drop operationin the SysML Internal Block diagram

The most efficient way to create a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space=''] and display it with all ports in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='']SysML Internal Block diagram is by dragging the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space=''] or Property elements. This method only allows you to display first level properties, and does not allow you to display deeply nested properties. To display deeply nested elements, use the **Display Parts/Ports** dialog. The following procedures will show you:

- 
- How to display a Part Property shape and all its ports by dragging the Part Property element.

drag_classYou can create a new Part Property by dragging the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='']element from the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MD2024xR2']Model Browserto the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''] pane. The newly created Part Property will be typed by the dragged [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space=''] and displayed on the diagram pane. If this Block has ports defined, you can display them by using the long drag, and selecting an appropriate option from the opened menu (see Example 3).

****

To create and display a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space=''] by dragging a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space=''] element

- Drag a single or a multiple [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space=''] element from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024xR2'] onto the diagram pane.
- Drag a single or a multiple [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space=''] element from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024xR2'] onto the diagram pane,and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag).Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the **Create Property Symbol** command. 
The new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space=''] is created in the model, and displayed on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024xR2']. The newly created [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space=''] is typed by the dragged [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space=''] automatically.

To create and display a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space=''] and display all its ports by dragging a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space=''] element

- Drag a single or a multiple [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space=''] element from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024xR2'] onto the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024xR2'], and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag).
- Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the **Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports** command. The new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space=''] is created in the model, and displayed with all its ports on the diagram pane. The newly created Part Property is typed by the dragged Block automatically.

#### INFO: Tip

Tip

After creating an element, it is beneficial to find its location in the model tree by performing one of the following: 
 - Right-click the shape, and, from the shortcut menu, choose the **Select in Containment Tree** command. 
 - Select the shape and press Alt+B.

#### TIP: Example 3

Example 3

After selecting the **Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports** command, anddragging (long drag) the Block element to *Control and Air Distribution Group* Internal Block diagram pane, itcreates a new Part Property typed by a *Air Distribution* Block and displays all ports on it.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

drag_propertyYou can display a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space=''] shape by dragging a Part Property element from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024xR2'] to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''] pane. If this Part Property has ports defined, you can display them by using the long drag, and selecting an appropriate option from the opened menu (see Example 4).

The following procedures describe how to display Part Property shape and all its ports by dragging the Part Property element.

To display a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space=''] shape by dragging a Part Property element

- Drag a single or a multiple Part Property element from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024xR2'] onto the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024xR2'] .
- Drag a single or a multiple Part Property element from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024xR2'] onto the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024xR2'] , and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag) . Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the**Create Part Property Symbol** command. The Part Property shape is displayed on the diagram pane .

To display a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space=''] with all of its ports by dragging a Part Property element

- Drag a single or a multiple Part Property element from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024xR2'] onto the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space='MD2024xR2'] , and hold it down until the Tooltip appears (long drag) .
- Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the**Create Part Property Symbol and Display All Ports** command. The Part Property shape is displayed with all its ports on that shape.

#### TIP: Example 4

Example 4

After selecting the**Create Part Property Symbol and Display All Ports**command, and dragging (long drag) the Part Property element to**Control and Air Distribution Group**Internal Block diagram, it displays Part Property shape and all ports on it.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After completing the steps outlined above, the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='']shape and/or all its ports are displayed on the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''].

##### Displaying properties of a port

If you display the properties of a port, all properties of its type will be displayed inside the port shape and Part Property compartments will be enabled for that port.

To enable Part Property compartments on the port shape

1. Select a port shape whose type has properties.
2. Do one of the following:
  - O n the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='Display.png'] .
  - From the shortcut menu, point to the Display command group.
3. Select the required properties to display.

The properties are displayed inside the port shape. The Part Property compartments are enabled for the port.

The example below shows the *p1* port both before and after displaying its type properties. The properties displayed on the port shape are the *Pressure Status* Flow Property and the *suspensionSensor* Part Property, which are the properties of the selected port type *iSuspension* Block. After the display of properties has been selected, compartments are automatically enabled for the *p1* port.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1096461592
