ac:ref="29785cb7-de15-492f-ae5a-25c23ea254d4">You can use the </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="29785cb7-de15-492f-ae5a-25c23ea254d4">Display Parts/Ports</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="29785cb7-de15-492f-ae5a-25c23ea254d4"> dialog</ac:inline-comment-marker> to control how much of a structure should be displayed. The <span> <strong>Display Parts/Ports </strong>dialog<strong> </strong> </span>opens automatically when creating a new<span class="confluence-link"> <span class="confluence-link"><span><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Internal Block Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Internal Block diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span></span></span></span> for a <span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Block" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Block</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> </span> element that owns ports and properties.</span> <span>It can also be accessed and opened in the </span><span class="confluence-link"><span><span><span>structure </span>diagram</span></span>s that have already been created</span>.<span> The<strong> Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog allows you to:</span></p><ul><li>Preview all <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[parts]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>, <span class="confluence-link">ports</span>, and other properties in one place.</li><li>Manually or automatically select the required <span class="confluence-link">parts</span>, <span class="confluence-link">ports</span>, and other properties you want to display.</li><li>Manually or automatically choose the deeply <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Nesting parts that are association ends" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[nested]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> <span class="confluence-link">parts</span>, <span class="confluence-link">ports</span>, and other properties you want to display.</li><li>Define layout options for <span class="confluence-link">parts</span>.</li><li>Specify layout options for <span class="confluence-link">ports</span>.</li></ul><p>The <span> <strong>Display Parts/Ports </strong>dialog consists of the following areas:</span></p><ul><li><span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span> <span><em> Dialog toolbar</em> allows you to show or hide additional information about listed elements.<br /> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span></li><li><span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span> <span> <em>Element tree</em> displays all owned internal structure of a context <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Block" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Block</span></ac:link-body></ac:link>.<br /> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span></li><li><span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span> <span> <em>Panel</em> is a context-sensitive area that<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> depends on the selected property from the Element tree. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">It allows you to batch select <span class="confluence-link">parts</span>/<span class="confluence-link">ports<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">in the Element tree. You can</span></span></span></span></span> </span>select all, clear all, or choose properties only by type, stereotype, or aspect.</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></li><li><span><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span><em style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Options</em> area allows you to choose the layout of the selected parts and <span class="confluence-link">ports </span>when they appear in the diagram.</span> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span> <span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span> </span><br /><span> <span> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span> <span> </span></span></span></span></span><span><br /></span></li></ul><p><span><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="The Display Parts/Ports dialog of the Climate Control Unit Block with the four highlighted main sections of the dialog: Toolbar, Element tree, Panel, and Options area."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_ports_parts_main_sections.png" /></ac:image></span></span></span></span></span></span></p><p><strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></strong></p><p>The following procedure will guide you in working with the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog when you want to display required ports and properties on the diagram pane.</p><p><br /></p><p>To display ports and properties using the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Open the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog:</p><ul><li>When creating a new <span><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><span><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Internal Block Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Internal Block diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span></span></span></span></span>, the dialog opens automatically.<span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span></li><li>When editing an already created <span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram descriptions" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link">structure diagram</span></span></ac:link-body></ac:link>, the dialog can be opened in the following ways:<br /> - Select either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes. O<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">n the diagram toolbar, click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image></span> and choose <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong>.</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br />- Right-click either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes, and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong>.<br /><br /></span></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span></span>(Optional) Enable buttons from the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog toolbar if you want to show or hide additional information about listed elements:<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span><ul><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Display the full path of the elements"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_the_full_path_of_the_elements.png" /></ac:image></span> to show the full path of elements.<span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <br /></span></span></li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Display the qualified name of type in the typed elements text"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_the_qualified_name_of_type_in_the typed_elements_text.png" /></ac:image></span> to show the full types of elements.</li><li>Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Show Inherited"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_inherited_button.png" /></ac:image></span> to show the inherited elements.<span class="confluence-link"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click <span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Show Private Inherited"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Show_private_inherited.png" /></ac:image></span> to show the inherited properties.</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></li></ul></li><li><p>Choose the properties you want to display, using one or more of the following methods:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0ebed955-61f3-499b-9133-06607c2df4c4"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span>If certain <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span>check boxes</span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span> are inactive in the element list, </span></span></span>those elements are already <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span>displayed</span></span></span> in the diagram.<br /></span></span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Use the element tree<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> located on <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">the left side of the dialog:</span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">-</span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Manually select individual check boxes<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">one by one.</span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span><br />- Select recursively nested elements by holding down the Shift key while selecting the corresponding check box for the element.<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <br /></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="58147caf-ac97-47ac-bfdd-939da1408e1f"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 1</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">All properties (in this example all ports<em> AirFlowPort, ElectricityPort, AutomotiveBusesPort</em>) of the <em>Air Distribution</em> Part Property are selected recursively <span><span><span><span><span><span><span><span><span>by holding down the Shift key while selecting the corresponding check box</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span> from the element tree</span></span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> in the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog.</span></span></p><ac:image ac:title="Recursively selected nested properties in the Display Parts/Ports dialog."><ri:attachment ri:filename="recursively_selected_properties.png" /></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Use the context-sensitive panel<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> located on the right side of the dialog:</span></span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">- <strong>Select Properties</strong> group - displays all property types <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">(including <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> your own</span></span></span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[stereotypes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>)</span> that are owned by the selected element <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog</span>.<br />- <strong>Select Ports</strong> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">group - displays all port types <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">(including your own stereotypes)</span> that are owned by the selected element <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog</span>.</span><br /></span>- <strong>Select All</strong> - selects all check boxes that are expanded <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">from the element tree located on the left side of the dialog.</span><br />- <strong>Clear All</strong> - clears all check boxes, even those that are collapsed (deep nested), <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">from the element tree</span> located on <span>the left side of the dialog.<br /></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="34fa4ff9-c35d-49d7-855f-d85a81261e84"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 2</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">All Electrical ports (in this example only one <em>ElectricityPort</em> port) of the <em>Data Transfer</em> Part Property are selected <span><span><span><span><span><span><span><span><span>in <span><span><span><span><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">the element tree</span></span></span></span></span></span> by using the <span><span><span><span><span><span><span><span><strong>Select Ports</strong> <span>group</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span> in the context-sensitive panel </span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">of the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog.</span></p><ac:image ac:title="Using the context-sensitive panel of the Display Parts/Ports dialog when trying to select required ports."><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_ports_using_context_sensitive_panel.png" /></ac:image></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span>Specify the layout options in the <strong>Options</strong> area:<br /><ul><li>Parts layout: select the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Layout Parts</strong> check box if you want the Part Property shapes to be arranged in the automatic layout.</li><li>Usage of layout template: <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">select the </span><strong>Use Layout Template</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> check box if you want the layout template to be applied. When this check box is selected, the Part Property, which is included in the layout template, and all its Ports from the layout template are selected automatically.</span></li><li>Showing connectors: select the <strong>Show Connectors</strong> check box if you want the connectors to be displayed.<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li><li>Ports layout on Part Property shape:<br />- <strong>Top/Bottom</strong> - displays ports on the Part Property shape from top to bottom.<br /><ac:image ac:title="The ports are layouted from top to bottom on the Compressor Part property shape." ac:alt="The ports are layouted from top to bottom on the Compressor Part property shape."><ri:attachment ri:filename="port_layout_top_bottom.png" /></ac:image><br />- <strong>Left/Right </strong> - displays ports on the Part Property shape from left to right.<br /><ac:image ac:title="The ports are layouted from left to right on the Compressor Part property shape." ac:alt="The ports are layouted from left to right on the Compressor Part property shape."><ri:attachment ri:filename="port_layout_left_right.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Click <strong>OK</strong>.</span></span></li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">After those steps, all parts, ports, other properties that were selected in the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog are displayed on the diagram pane.<br /></span></span></p><h4>Using the diagram toolbar and the shortcut menu</h4><p class="confluence-link">In the structure diagrams that have already been created, you can display <span class="confluence-link">ports</span>, <span class="confluence-link">properties</span>, and hide all <span class="confluence-link">ports</span> by using the commands from:</p><ul><li class="confluence-link">The <strong>Display</strong> button on the diagram toolbar.</li><li class="confluence-link">The <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Display</strong> command group under the shortcut menu.</span></li></ul><p>You can also use <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span>the </span><strong>Display </strong>button</span></span></span> and <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Display</strong> command group from <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">the shortcut menu as</span></span></span></span></span> their functions are identical. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">All those commands</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span>are interactive and <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">depend upon the selection on the diagram pane. For example, if the Part Property shape is selected on the diagram pane, the menu contains <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">only </span></span></span></span></span></span>the commands that can be applied to the selected <span class="confluence-link">Part</span> Property shape. If you select nothing in the diagram pane, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">the menu</span></span></span></span></span></span> shows<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> commands that can be applied</span></span></span> to the whole diagram.</span></span></span></span></span></span></p><p>These <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link">commands under the <strong>Display</strong> button and shortcut menu are <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">comprised of </span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span>elements that are still not displayed on the diagram according to their:</p><ul><li>Metatypes.</li><li>Stereotypes.</li><li>Types stereotypes.</li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span class="confluence-link">The following procedures will show you how to:</span></span></span></span></span></span></p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="button"><ac:link-body>How to display Ports, Properties using the <strong>Display</strong> button.</ac:link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="shortcut"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to display Ports, Properties using the shortcut menu.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="hide"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to hide Ports.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="151e0ef2-cf5f-42c8-9b7b-b70191dd1dcd"><ac:parameter ac:name="">button</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>To display ports, properties using the <strong>Display</strong> button</p><hr /><ol><li>Select either a diagram frame or single/multiple element shapes.</li><li>O<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">n the diagram toolbar, click </span><ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image>.<br /><ac:image ac:title="After selecting the Data Transfer and Control Groups Internal Block diagram pane and clicking the Display button, opened menu allows to display properties and/or ports."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_related_element.png" /></ac:image></li><li><p>From the opened command menu, select required properties or ports to display:<br />        - All ports that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Ports</strong> command group.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ef07d2a-8928-47ac-9bee-363460e3bac9"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7319cc0e-6a3c-4e44-9809-e9817c6a997b">If nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7319cc0e-6a3c-4e44-9809-e9817c6a997b">Display Ports</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7319cc0e-6a3c-4e44-9809-e9817c6a997b"> command group will contain commands that allow you to display two levels of structure: ports are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first level parts.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>        - All properties that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Properties</strong> command group.</p><p><span><span>After selecting the required command from the</span></span> <strong>Display</strong> button<span><span>, the appropriate properties are displayed on the diagram pane.</span></span></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f27456dd-5abb-464b-b57a-d14bee0cbd81"><ac:parameter ac:name="">shortcut</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>To display ports, properties using the shortcut menu</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click either the diagram pane or single/multiple element shapes.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, select the <strong>Display</strong> command group.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="The Display command group menu allows to display properties and ports of the Climate Control Unit Part Property shape." ac:alt="The Display command group menu allows to display properties and ports of the Climate Control Unit Part Property shape."><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display_command.png" /></ac:image></li><li><p>From the opened command menu, select required properties to display:<br />        - All Ports that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Ports</strong> command group.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="327c3ff9-f8cc-4d9e-a0b5-0c763e6c790e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If nothing is selected on the diagram pane, the <strong>Display Ports</strong> command group allows you to display two levels of structure: ports are displayed on the diagram frame and on the first level parts.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>        - All Properties that are available to display are listed under the <strong>Display Properties</strong> command group.<br />After selecting the required command from the <strong>Display</strong> command menu, the appropriate properties' ports are displayed on the diagram pane.<br /><br /></p></li></ol><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4794c2e6-db49-41b6-ac94-03bcb79efedc"><ac:parameter ac:name="">hide</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></strong>To hide ports</p><hr /><ul><li>Select either a diagram pane or any number of element shapes. O<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">n the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image>  and choose<strong> Hide All Ports</strong>.</span></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Right-click either a diagram pane or any number of element shapes, and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt;<strong> Hide All Ports</strong>.<br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">After selecting the command, the <span class="confluence-link">ports </span>of the selected shape will be hidden from the diagram pane, but not removed from the model</span>.<br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ff629db0-f28c-4b9f-aa61-48e542d03bba"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the <strong>Hide All Ports</strong> command doesn't appear, the selection or <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">diagram </span>doesn't contain any <span class="confluence-link">ports </span>that can be hidden.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:image ac:title="Hiding all Proxy Ports of the Part Property typed by Data Transfer Block." ac:alt="Hiding all Proxy Ports of the Part Property typed by Data Transfer Block."><ri:attachment ri:filename="hide_all_ports.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></p></li></ul><h4>Using the smart manipulator toolbar<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></span></span></h4><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span>When the properties are already displayed on the<span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagram descriptions" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">structure diagram</span>s</ac:link-body></ac:link></span>, you can display their direct <span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link">ports</span></span> using the <strong>Display All Ports</strong> button from the smart manipulator toolbar. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">It only allows you to display first level ports, and does not allow you to display deeply nested <span class="confluence-link">ports</span>. To display deeply nested ports, you should use the <span class="confluence-link"><strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog</span>.</span> The steps below show how to display ports of a selected property shape.</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></p><p>To display Ports on shapes</p><hr /><ol><li>Select<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span> the required</span></span> element shape or a diagram frame.</span> </span></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">From the smart manipulator toolbar, click <ac:image ac:title="Display All Ports" ac:alt="Display All Ports"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_all_ports.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>.<br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span>All <span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">first level</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span> Ports of <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">the selected shape</span></span></span> will be displayed on the diagram pane.</span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span></span><br /></span></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="feb6590f-3710-4154-9154-2c9ce8edfa7e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p> If the <strong>Display All Ports</strong> button is not available, the selected element or <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">diagram </span>doesn't own any Port.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:image ac:title="The Display All Ports button on the smart manipulator toolbar of the Part Property shape typed by Data Transfer Block." ac:alt="The Display All Ports button on the smart manipulator toolbar of the Part Property shape typed by Data Transfer Block."><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_all_ports.png" /></ac:image><br /></span></span></span></p></li></ol><h4>Using the drag-and-drop operation<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> in the SysML Internal Block diagram</span></h4><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The most efficient way to create a new <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part Property" /></ac:link> and display it with all <span class="confluence-link">ports</span> in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Internal Block Diagram" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">SysML Internal Block diagram</span></ac:link-body></ac:link> is by dragging the <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Block" /></ac:link></span> or Property elements. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">This method only allows you to display first level properties, and does not allow you to display deeply nested properties. To display deeply nested elements, use the <strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The following procedures will show you:</span></span></span></span></span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link ac:anchor="drag_class"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to create a Part Property shape and how to display all its ports when dragging the Block element.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:link ac:anchor="drag_property"><ac:link-body>How to display a Part Property shape and all its <span class="confluence-link">ports</span> by dragging the Part Property element.</ac:link-body></ac:link></span></span></span></li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></span></span></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e9f47650-536d-46c6-8f68-c5881f221387"><ac:parameter ac:name="">drag_class</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can create a new Part Property by dragging the <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Block" /></ac:link> </span>element <span class="confluence-link">from the<span class="confluence-link"> </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Model Browser" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">Model Browser</span> </ac:link-body></ac:link>to the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Internal Block Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Internal Block diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> pane</span>. The newly created Part Property will be typed by the dragged <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Block" /></ac:link></span> and displayed on the diagram pane. If this <span class="confluence-link">Block</span> has <span class="confluence-link">ports</span> defined, you can display them by using the long drag, and selecting an appropriate option from the opened menu (see Example 3). <br /></span></span></p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p>To create and display a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part Property" /></ac:link> by dragging a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Block" /></ac:link> element</p><hr /><ul><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drag a single or a multiple <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Block" /></ac:link> element from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Browser]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> onto the diagram pane.</span></span></span></p></li><li><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drag a single or a multiple <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Block" /></ac:link> element from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Browser]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> onto the diagram pane, </span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">and hold it down <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">until the Tooltip appears (long drag)</span>.</span></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the <strong>Create Property Symbol</strong> command.</span></span></span><br />The new <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part Property" /></ac:link> is created in the model, and displayed on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The newly created <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part Property" /></ac:link> is typed by the dragged <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Block" /></ac:link> automatically.</span></span></span></span> </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></p></li></ul><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="d33f1ab5-07e0-42c6-8681-25e8e07df1d5">To create and display a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part Property" /></ac:link> and display all its ports by dragging a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Block" /></ac:link> element</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drag a single or a multiple <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Block" /></ac:link> element from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Browser]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> onto the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and hold it down <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">until the Tooltip appears (long drag)</span>.</span></span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the <strong>Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports</strong> command.</span></span></span><br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span></span></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The new <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part Property" /></ac:link> is created in the model, and displayed with all its ports on the diagram pane. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The newly created Part Property is typed by the dragged Block automatically.</span></span></span></span></span></span></span><br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35a694c8-df84-455d-8e7f-eaa249205f99"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>After creating an element, it is beneficial to find its location in the model tree by performing one of the following:<br />        - Right-click the shape, and, from the shortcut menu, choose the <strong>Select in Containment Tree</strong> command.<br />        - Select the shape and press Alt+B.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2a3ba930-c9af-4a64-8b14-28f87fe613a7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 3</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">After selecting the <strong>Create Property Symbol and Display All Ports</strong> command, and</span> </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">dragging (long drag) the Block element to <em>Control and Air Distribution Group</em> Internal Block diagram pane, it</span> </span>creates a new Part Property typed by a <em>Air Distribution</em> Block and displays all ports on it.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="drag_block.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br /><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="593bf531-1a65-4783-ac36-35a678036e74"><ac:parameter ac:name="">drag_property</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can display a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part Property" /></ac:link> shape by dragging a Part Property element from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Browser]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to the <span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Internal Block Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Internal Block diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span> pane. If this Part Property has <span class="confluence-link">ports</span> defined, <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">you can display them by using the long drag, and selecting an appropriate option from the opened menu (see Example 4).</span></span><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The following procedures describe how to display Part Property shape and all its <span class="confluence-link">ports</span> by dragging the Part Property element.</span></p><p>To display a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part Property" /></ac:link> shape by dragging a Part Property element</p><hr /><ul><li>Drag a single or a multiple Part Property element from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Browser]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> onto the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Drag a single or a multiple Part Property element from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Browser]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> onto the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and hold it down <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">until the Tooltip appears (long drag)</span>. <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the<strong> Create Part Property Symbol</strong> command. </span><br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The Part Property shape is displayed on the diagram pane</span>.<br /><br /></li></ul><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="21aea531-8fce-41e3-a048-c759c72d59e2">To display a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part Property" /></ac:link> with all of its ports by dragging a Part Property element</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><hr /><ul><li>Drag a single or a multiple Part Property element from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Browser]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> onto the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and hold it down <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">until the Tooltip appears (long drag)</span>.<span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drop it, and, from the opened menu, select the<strong> Create Part Property Symbol and Display All Ports</strong> command.</span><br /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The Part Property shape is displayed with all its ports on that shape.</span><br /></span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9bbc855a-c19c-4afe-8c7c-56e981ef78a8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Example 4</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">After selecting the </span><strong>Create Part Property Symbol and Display All Ports</strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> command, and dragging (long drag) the Part Property element to</span><em> <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><em>Control and Air Distribution Group</em></span></span></em><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"> Internal Block diagram, it displays Part Property shape and all ports on it.</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="drag_part_property.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">After completing the steps outlined above, the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Part Property ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">shape and/or all its ports are displayed on the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Internal Block Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Internal Block diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">.</span></p><p><br /></p><h3>Displaying properties of a port</h3><p>If you display the properties of a port, all properties of its type will be displayed inside the port shape and Part Property compartments will be enabled for that port.</p><p>To enable Part Property compartments on the port shape</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a port shape whose type has properties.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>O<span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">n the diagram toolbar, click </span><ac:image ac:title="Display" ac:alt="Display"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Display.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, point to the <strong>Display</strong> command group.</li></ul></li><li>Select the required properties to display.</li></ol><p>The properties are displayed inside the port shape. The Part Property compartments are enabled for the port.</p><p>The example below shows the <em>p1</em> port both before and after displaying its type properties. The properties displayed on the port shape are the <em>Pressure Status</em> Flow Property and the <em>suspensionSensor</em> Part Property, which are the properties of the selected port type <em>iSuspension</em> Block. After the display of properties has been selected, compartments are automatically enabled for the <em>p1</em> port.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="part_compartments_on_port_shape.png" /></ac:image></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="adf3e2dd-0481-460b-829d-e4ef82cb43dd"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1096461592</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><span><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Applying Stereotypes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span><span> </span></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Nesting parts that are association ends" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Mapping ports on a Part type change" /></ac:link><span> </span></li><li><span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Proxy Port" /></ac:link></span></li><li><span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Full Port" /></ac:link></span></li><li><span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Flow Port" /></ac:link></span><span> </span></li><li><p class="with-breadcrumbs"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Displaying elements" /></ac:link><span> </span><span> </span></p></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Internal Block Diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Autowire Parts" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Autowiring]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Connecting parts through interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Connecting Parts Through Interface]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Enforce Ports Compatibility mode" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Hiding direction arrow on port shape" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Hiding direction arrow on Port shape]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Property path notation" /></ac:link></span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The models used in the figures of this page are:</p><ul><li>The <strong>Vehicle Climate Control Unit</strong> sample model. <span>To open this sample d</span>ownload <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Vehicle Climate Control Unit.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="_sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.<span class="confluence-link"><em> <br /></em></span></li><li><span class="confluence-link">The <strong>Vehicle parts under ports</strong> sample model. <span><span><span><span><span><span><span><span><span><span><span><span><span><span><span><span>To open this sample d</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span>ownload <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Vehicle parts under ports.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="_sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.</span></li></ul><p><strong><br /></strong></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189147259 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00122: Displaying Rake icon on symbol

- page_id: `189147259`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189147259/Displaying+Rake+icon+on+symbol
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2015-07-28T06:29:30.520+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can use rake icon for faster navigation in the model. The rake icon on the symbol indicates that the appropriate element has an internal structure. Double-click the element to open the internal diagram. The rake icon is shown by default on the symbol.

To hide rake icon

1. Right-click the symbol.
2. Select Symbol Properties from shortcut menu or press Alt+Enter.
3. Set the Show Rake Icon property value to false.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>You can use rake icon for faster navigation in the model. The rake icon on the symbol indicates that the appropriate element has an internal structure. Double-click the element to open the internal diagram. The rake icon is shown by default on the symbol.</p><p>To hide rake icon</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the symbol.</li><li>Select <strong>Symbol Properties</strong> from shortcut menu or press Alt+Enter.</li><li>Set the <strong>Show Rake Icon</strong> property value to false.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:alt="Rake icon on symbol" ac:title="Rake icon on symbol"><ri:attachment ri:filename="rake_icon.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying Rake icon on symbol" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189147646 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00123: Distributed Property

- page_id: `189147646`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189147646/Distributed+Property
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-06-01T10:13:56.180+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Glossary of SysML concepts > Element descriptions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Distributed Property is a property of a Block or a Value Type, used to apply a probability distribution to the values of the property. Specific distributions can be defined by applying a subclass of the *DistributedProperty* stereotype to the property.

#### PANEL: Related diagrams

Related diagrams

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>A Distributed Property is a property of a Block or a Value Type, used to apply a probability distribution to the values of the property. Specific distributions can be defined by applying a subclass of the <em>DistributedProperty</em> stereotype to the property.</p><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ad1d2f29-7ddc-4427-be11-2022073a6b8a"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related diagrams</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Internal Block Diagram" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p> </ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189147647 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00125: Domain

- page_id: `189147647`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189147647/Domain
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-03-21T13:44:27.770+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Glossary of SysML concepts > Element descriptions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Visibility representation

299228950

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Visibility representation

299228953

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Visibility representation

299228951

A Domain block represents an entity, a concept, a location, or a person from the real-world domain. A domain block is part of the system knowledge.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Visibility representation

Visibility representation

In SysML, properties and operations of the Block are public. Visibility representation literals, like +, #, ~, -, are not displayed in the Containment tree or in the element symbol on a diagram.

299228949

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Block Definition Diagram' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189146592 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00126: Edit Item Flow Realization Between Activities dialog

- page_id: `189146592`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189146592/Edit+Item+Flow+Realization+Between+Activities+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T08:02:25.260+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Item Flow management
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Edit Item Flow Realization Between Activities** dialog allows you to review Activities for a selected Item Flow.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the **Edit Item Flow Realization Between Activities** dialog

- In the Specification window of the Item Flow, select From Activity or To Activity property value cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] .
- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Item Flow Manager dialog' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']**Item Flow Manager** dialog , select the From Activity or To Activity cell and click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] .

The buttons of the dialog are described in the following table.

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Remove From Table | Click to delete the selected Activity pair. |
| Show Full Paths | Toggle either to show or hide the full element paths. |

To remove the realization between listed Activity pairs

1. Select an Activity pair.
2. Click the Remove From Table button.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189147999 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00128: Electrical Circuit

- page_id: `189147999`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189147999/Electrical+Circuit
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T14:46:22.757+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > Exporting to External Simulation Models > Modelica export > Simulating exported Modelica models
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page describes how to simulate *ElectricCircuit* example model in Dymola. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Electric Circuit sample' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']Learn more about *ElectricCircuit* sample model >>

You can find the *ElectricCircuit* sample model in:

- the modeling tool: Welcome window > Samples > Simulink and Modelica Transformation > ElectricCircuit.
- the Installation directory: <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML\Simulink and Modelica Transformation\*ElectricCircuit.*

To simulate the Circuit Modelica model

1. Export the Circuit Block to Modelica file. How to >>
2. Make sure the Dymola tool is installed. How to >>
3. Start the Dymola.
4. Click File > Open > Load... and select saved *Circuit.mo* file from your file directory. A*Circuit*model is displayed in the **Package Browser**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. In the **Graphics**tab toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to check model for syntax and semantic errors. The result panel displays whether the model is ready for simulation. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
6. Setup the simulation run for the model: 
 a. Select the******Simulation**tab and in its toolbar click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. b. In the**Simulation Setup**dialog, specifythe**Start time******value to**0**and the**Stop time******value to**60**seconds(or any other reasonable amount of time; for this model, 60 seconds is enough). c. Click**OK**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. In the**Simulation** tab toolbar, click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. When the simulation is completed, the output is displayed in the panel at the bottom and the *Circuit*variables are displayed in the **Variable Browser**.
8. To see input and output of the Circuit model, select variables in the Variable Browser as follows: Circuit > _Circuit > c, i, rc, ri, s > iThru , vDrop . T he current ( iThru ) and voltage ( vDrop ) variable across each of the circuit’s 5 components are displayed on the **Plot**window: the capacitor ( c ), the inductor ( i ), the voltage source ( v ), and the resistors ( rc, ri ). In the example below, only iThru , vDrop of s variable are displayed. [ATTACHMENT filename='plot_diagram_s_variable.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

rgb(62,63,64);">), the voltage source (</span><strong style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">v</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">), and the resistors (</span><strong style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">rc, ri</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">). In the example below, only </span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span><strong style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">iThru</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">, </span><strong style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">vDrop </strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">of</span><strong style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">s variable are displayed. </span><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="plot_diagram_s_variable.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Electrical Circuit" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p />
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189147926 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00129: Electrical Circuit example to Simulink and Simscape model

- page_id: `189147926`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189147926/Electrical+Circuit+example+to+Simulink+and+Simscape+model
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T14:46:21.032+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > Exporting to External Simulation Models > Simulink export > Simulating exported Simulink models
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This page describes how to simulate *ElectricCircuit* example model in Simulink. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Electric Circuit sample' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']Learn more about *ElectricCircuit* sample model >>

You can find the *ElectricCircuit* sample model in:

- the modeling tool: Welcome window > Samples > Simulink and Modelica Transformation > ElectricCircuit.
- the Installation directory: <modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML\Simulink and Modelica Transformation\*ElectricCircuit.*

To simulate the *Electrical Circuit*model

1. Export the Circuit Block to Simulink file. How to >>
2. In the Simulink Export Options dialog select the following options:
  - **Format**: XML (.slx)
  - **S-Function or Simscape**: Simscape
  - Simscape port libraries : Create new port types
  - Composite Signals : Bus Creators/Selectors
3. Make sure the MATLAB tool is installed.
4. Double-click the MATLAB icon to start it.
5. In the file directory select the location of your exported ElectricalCircuit model Simulink files and choose the +CircuitLibrary folder. The simscape files (.ssc) are displayed in the Current Folder panel. [ATTACHMENT filename='file_directory_simulink_files_circuit.png']
6. In the Command Window type ssc_build (or sscbuild depending on the Matlab version) and press Enter . This generates a folder sscprj in the +CircuitLibrary directory as well as a CircuitLibrary_lib.slx file in the directory with the original Circuit.slx file. [ATTACHMENT filename='generating_simulink_library.png']
7. Go back to the file directory, select the ElectricalCircuit package with exported Simulink files and in the Current Folder select the Circuit.slx . [ATTACHMENT filename='current_folder_circuit_slx.png']
8. Double-click on Circuit.slx to open the Simulink/Simscape model. Rearrange the blocks to get a better picture of the blocks and links. [ATTACHMENT filename='circuit_model_simscape.png']
9. On the **SIMULATION** tab, click the arrow on the **PREPARE** area and select the **Model Settings**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
10. In the **Configuration Parameters** dialog, on the left side of the dialog: 
a. Select a **Solver**property group****and specify the following on the right side of the dialog: 
****-****In the the **Simulation time** group, change **Start time**to **0.0**, **Stop time**to **60**(or any other reasonable number of seconds). 
 - In the **Solver selection** group, select**Type****Variable-step** or any other desirable solver that is suitable. 
 - Click the arrow of the **Solver details** group and change the **Max step size** to **0.01**. b. Select Simscape property group and specify the following on the right side of the dialog: - In the Data Logging group, change Log simulation data to All . - Make sure Limit data points is unchecked. [ATTACHMENT filename='configuration_parameters_dialog.png']
11. Press Apply > OK .
12. In the Circuit Simulink window, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
13. Once the model has compiled, o n the main toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='simscape_results_button.png'] to open a Simscape Results Explorer : Circuit window where you can expand the Circuit blocks to see how their properties simulated through time. For the circuit components c (capacitor), i (inductor), rc (resistor in series with the capacitor), ri (resistor in series with the inductor), and s (source), the properties iThru (current) and vDrop (voltage) can be selected to see the flow of charge (current) and its potential to flow (voltage) through each component. To see the simulation’s specific data points, select the mouse pointer on the plot. [ATTACHMENT filename='simscape_results_explorer_circuit.png']
14. To change the default value or initial value parameters of the model, double-click the blocks in the Circuit Simulink model. A Block Parameters dialog opens where yoy can change parameters. Repeat steps from 5 to 12 to run the simulation with (new) block parameters. [ATTACHMENT filename='rc_block_prameters.png']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189147945 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00130: Electrical Circuit example to Simulink and Simscape model R2016a

- page_id: `189147945`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189147945/Electrical+Circuit+example+to+Simulink+and+Simscape+model+R2016a
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-02-11T09:22:30.024+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > Exporting to External Simulation Models > Simulink export > Simulating exported Simulink models > Electrical Circuit example to Simulink and Simscape model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To generate a Simulink model directly from the modeling tool

1. Right-click the *Block* **TestCaseCircuit::Circuit** and select **Tools**> **Export to Simulink.** Please see Generating a simulation file for more information.
2. Set the options listed below and click OK :
  - Format : XML (.sdl)
  - S-Function or Simscape : Simscape
  - Simscape port libraries : Create new port types
3. Launch Matlab with Simulink and Simscape extensions.
4. In Matlab’s Current Directory navigation bar, search for the file directory where the Simulink/Simscape files (as well as the SysML files) are saved, and set it as the current directory. The Current Folder panel should display the generated files. [ATTACHMENT filename='Change_Directory_in_Matlab.png']
5. In the Current Folder panel, double-click on the folder named +CircuitLibrary (this sets the current directory to this folder). Type ssc_build (or sscbuild depending on the Matlab version) in the Command Window and press Enter . This will generate a folder sscprj in the +CircuitLibrary directory as well as a CircuitLibrary_lib.slx file in the directory with the original Circuit.slx file. Next, once again, search for and go back to the file directory where the Simulink/Simscape files (as well as the SysML files) are saved, and then set it as the current directory. [ATTACHMENT filename='Using_the_Ssc_Build_Command.png']
6. In the Current Folder panel, double-click on Circuit.slx to open the Simulink/Simscape model. Rearrange the blocks to get a better picture of the blocks and links. [ATTACHMENT filename='Circuit Simulink_Simscape_Diagram.png']
7. Go to Simulation > Model Configuration Settings . This allows to select the types of solver and the runtime of the simulation. Select a Start Time of 0 , and a Stop Time of 60 (or any other reasonable number of seconds). Change the Max step size to 0.01 . Under Solver Options , keep the solver Type to Variable-step (or any other desirable solver that is suitable). Press Apply . [ATTACHMENT filename='Simulink_Simscape_Model_Configuration_Settings_for_Simulation.png']
8. In Model Configuration Settings , select Simscape in the Commonly Used Parameters panel. Change Log simulation data to All . Make sure Limit data points is unchecked. Press Apply . Press OK . [IMAGE alt='' src='']
9. In the Circuit Simulink window, go to Simulation > Run .
10. Once the model has compiled, go to Matlab’s Command Window , type ssc_explore(simlog) (make sure that either the version of Matlab has the function ssc_explore, or a file ssc_explore.m is in the same directory as Circuit.slx in the Current Folder panel) and press Enter. A Simscape data logging explorer : Circuit window will open up. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
11. In the**Simscape data logging explorer**, the **Circuit** blocks can be expanded to see how their properties simulated through time. For the circuit components**c** (capacitor),**i** (inductor), **rc** (resistor in series with the capacitor),**ri** (resistor in series with the inductor), and **s** (source), the properties**iThru** (current) and **vDrop** (voltage) can be selected to see the flow of charge (current) and its potential to flow (voltage) through each component. To see the simulation’s specific data points, the **Data cursor** icon needs to be selected and points on the plot need to be chosen. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
12. To change the default value or initial value parameters of the model, double-click the blocks in the Circuit Simulink model. A window will pop out for block parameters that can be changed. Run through Steps 4 through 8 to run the simulation with (new) block parameters again. [ATTACHMENT filename='Changing_Block_Parameters.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Change the<strong> Max step size</strong> to <strong>0.01</strong>. Press <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><br /><strong><ac:image ac:width="700"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Simulink_Simscape_,Model_Configuration_Settings_for_Simscape.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Electrical Circuit example to Simulink and Simscape model R2016a" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong><br /><strong><br /></strong></li><li>In the <strong>Circuit</strong> Simulink window, go to<strong> Simulation</strong> &gt;  <strong>Run</strong>.</li><li>Once the model has compiled, go to Matlab’s <strong>Command Window</strong>, type<strong> ssc_explore(simlog)</strong> (make sure that either the version of Matlab has the function <strong>ssc_explore,</strong> or a file <strong>ssc_explore.m</strong> is in the same directory as <strong>Circuit.slx</strong> in the <strong>Current Folder</strong> panel) and press Enter. A <strong>Simscape data logging explorer</strong>: <strong>Circuit</strong> window will open up.<br /><br /><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sscexplore(simlog)_Command_and_the_Simscape_Data_Logging_Explorer.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Electrical Circuit example to Simulink and Simscape model R2016a" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li><p>In the<strong> Simscape data logging explorer</strong>, the <strong>Circuit</strong> blocks can be expanded to see how their properties simulated through time. For the circuit components<strong> c</strong> (capacitor),<strong> i</strong> (inductor), <strong>rc</strong> (resistor in series with the capacitor),<strong> ri</strong> (resistor in series with the inductor), and <strong>s</strong> (source), the properties<strong> iThru</strong> (current) and <strong>vDrop</strong> (voltage) can be selected to see the flow of charge (current) and its potential to flow (voltage) through each component. To see the simulation’s specific data points, the <strong>Data cursor</strong> icon needs to be selected and points on the plot need to be chosen.</p><p><br /><ac:image ac:width="750"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Using_the_Simscape_Data_Logging_Explorer.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Electrical Circuit example to Simulink and Simscape model R2016a" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li><li>To change the default value or initial value parameters of the model, double-click the blocks in the<strong> Circuit Simulink</strong> model.
