<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189146532 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00132: Enable Patterns-Based Verification option

- page_id: `189146532`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189146532/Enable+Patterns-Based+Verification+option
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-02-21T11:38:26.521+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Requirements management > Requirements verification
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can display instances in either Instance or Requirement tables to view the data and its verification results. In the tables' toolbar, via the **Options**button, you can choose to turn on or off the **Enable Patterns-Based Verification**option, which determines which type of verification is presented in the tables:

- Enable Patterns-Based Verification is ON : This mode presents the verification based on instance slot values . It ignores the tag values of the «VerificationStatus» stereotype.
  - Presents the verification performed by comparing the instance slot value with the requirement boundary.
  - The margin is calculated based on the comparison, and the table cells are colored (green for pass, red for fail) based on the comparison results.
  - If the simulation is done with requirements refined by constraints, the requirement boundary is unknown (Refined) and the margin cannot be calculated; thus the verification cannot be performed and the table cells are not colored.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
**Enable Patterns-Based Verification** is ON, the **Instance table**presents the verification performed based on the instance slot values with the table cells colored accordingly. «VerificationStatus» stereotype tag values are ignored. 
The *spring.coils* property column is not colored because it satisfies a requirement whose bounds are refined by a constraint, so verification cannot be done.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
**Enable Patterns-Based Verification** is ON, the **Requirement table**presents the verification performed based on the instance slot values with the table cells colored accordingly. «VerificationStatus» stereotype tag values are ignored. 
The *Spring Coils* requirement row is not colored because this requirement (its boundaries) is refined by a constraint, so verification cannot be done.

- **Enable Patterns-Based Verification** is **OFF**: This mode presents ****the **verification based on** **tag values**of the «VerificationStatus» stereotype. It **ignores**the **instance slot values**.
  - Presents the verification performed by comparing the 'margin' tag value with the requirement boundary.
  - The margin calculated during the simulation is stored in the 'margin' tag of the «VerificationStatus» stereotype. If the 'margin' tag value is empty, as is the case when the verification is done with requirements refined by constraints, the verification is presented based on the 'status' tag value (pass/fail). Based on this, the table cells displaying the slot values are colored (green for pass, red for fail).

- To color the Instance table based on the verification results without enabling the Enable Patterns-Based Verification option, make sure to display the Legend ( Legend button in the Table toolbar > Apply Legends > Verification Status ).
- Disabling the Enable Patterns-Based Verification option is only useful when working with instances (in either Instance or Requirement tables), as disabling the option considers the tag values of the «VerificationStatus» stereotype instead of the slot values.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
**Enable Patterns-Based Verification**is OFF, the **Instance table**presents the verification performed based on the tag values of the «VerificationStatus» stereotype with the table cells colored accordingly. The instance slot values are ignored. 
*spring.deflectionDistance*presents the verification based on the comparison of the 'margin' tag value with the requirement bounds. The *spring.coils*property satisfies a requirement whose bounds are refined by a constraint; thus it has no margin and its verification is presented based on the 'pass' tag value of the «VerificationStatus» stereotype.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
**Enable Patterns-Based Verification** **is OFF, the **Requirement table**presents the verification performed based on the tag values of the «VerificationStatus» stereotype with the table cells colored accordingly. The instance slot values are ignored. 
*Spring Deflection Distance* presents the verification based on the comparison of the 'margin' tag value with the requirement bounds. The *Spring Coils* property satisfies a requirement whose bounds are refined by a constraint; thus it has no margin and its verification is presented based on the 'pass' tag value of the «VerificationStatus» stereotype.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can display instances in either Instance or Requirement tables to view the data and its verification results. In the tables' toolbar, via the <strong>Options </strong>button, you can choose to turn on or off the <strong>Enable Patterns-Based Verification </strong>option, which determines which type of verification is presented in the tables:</p><ul><li><strong>Enable Patterns-Based Verification </strong>is<strong> ON</strong>: This mode presents the<strong> verification based on instance slot values</strong>. It <strong>ignores</strong> the <strong>tag values </strong>of the «VerificationStatus» stereotype.<ul><li>Presents the verification performed by comparing the instance slot value with the requirement boundary.</li><li>The margin is calculated based on the comparison, and the table cells are colored (green for pass, red for fail) based on the comparison results.</li><li>If the simulation is done with requirements refined by constraints, the requirement boundary is unknown (Refined) and the margin cannot be calculated; thus the verification cannot be performed and the table cells are not colored.</li></ul></li></ul><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-bottom: 0.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="instance_table_Patterns-Based_Verification_ON.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Enable Patterns-Based Verification option" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <br /><strong>Enable Patterns-Based Verification</strong> is ON, the <strong>Instance table </strong>presents the verification performed based on the instance slot values with the table cells colored accordingly. «VerificationStatus» stereotype tag values are ignored.<br />The <em>spring.coils</em> property column is not colored because it satisfies a requirement whose bounds are refined by a constraint, so verification cannot be done. <br /><br /></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-bottom: 0.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="requirement_table_Patterns-Based_Verification_ON.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Enable Patterns-Based Verification option" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <br /><strong>Enable Patterns-Based Verification</strong> is ON, the <strong>Requirement table </strong>presents the verification performed based on the instance slot values with the table cells colored accordingly. «VerificationStatus» stereotype tag values are ignored. <br />The <em>Spring Coils</em> requirement row is not colored because this requirement (its boundaries) is refined by a constraint, so verification cannot be done.<br /><br /></h6><ul><li><p><strong>Enable Patterns-Based Verification</strong> is <strong>OFF</strong>: This mode presents <strong> </strong>the <strong>verification based on</strong> <strong>tag values </strong>of the «VerificationStatus» stereotype. It <strong>ignores </strong>the <strong>instance slot values</strong>.</p><ul><li>Presents the verification performed by comparing the 'margin' tag value with the requirement boundary.</li><li>The margin calculated during the simulation is stored in the 'margin' tag of the «VerificationStatus» stereotype. If the 'margin' tag value is empty, as is the case when the verification is done with requirements refined by constraints, the verification is presented based on the 'status' tag value (pass/fail). Based on this, the table cells displaying the slot values are colored (green for pass, red for fail).</li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cfbcf4ed-906c-447e-883f-24282749bc35"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>To color the Instance table based on the verification results without enabling the <strong>Enable Patterns-Based Verification</strong> option, make sure to display the Legend (<strong>Legend </strong>button in the Table toolbar &gt; <strong>Apply Legends </strong>&gt; <strong>Verification Status</strong>).</li><li><span>Disabling the </span> <strong>Enable Patterns-Based Verification</strong> <span> option is only useful when working with instances (in either Instance or Requirement tables), as disabling the option considers the tag values of the «VerificationStatus» stereotype instead of the slot values.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-bottom: 0.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="instance_table_Patterns-Based_Verification_OFF.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Enable Patterns-Based Verification option" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <br /><strong>Enable Patterns-Based Verification </strong>is OFF, the <strong>Instance table </strong>presents the verification performed based on the tag values of the «VerificationStatus» stereotype with the table cells colored accordingly. The instance slot values are ignored. <br /><em>spring.deflectionDistance </em>presents the verification based on the comparison of the 'margin' tag value with the requirement bounds. The <em>spring.coils </em>property satisfies a requirement whose bounds are refined by a constraint; thus it has no margin and its verification is presented based on the 'pass' tag value of the «VerificationStatus» stereotype. <br /><br /></h6><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-bottom: 0.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="requirement_table_Patterns-Based_Verification_OFF.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Enable Patterns-Based Verification option" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> <br /><strong>Enable Patterns-Based Verification</strong> <em> </em>is OFF, the <strong>Requirement table </strong>presents the verification performed based on the tag values of the «VerificationStatus» stereotype with the table cells colored accordingly. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189146691 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=2 -->
````
## PAGE 00133: Enabling Part compartments on Port shape

- page_id: `189146691`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189146691/Enabling+Part+compartments+on+Port+shape
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T14:46:01.169+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Modeling structure with Blocks > Defining interfaces using ports > Creating ports
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

291194966

291194968

291194967

You can display a Port shape as a Part and display compartments of the Port type in it. This means you can represent the inner structure or properties of the Block type in a Port shape.

To enable Part compartments on the Port shape

1. On the diagram pane, right-click the selected Port.
2. In the open shortcut menu, select Enable Part Compartment . The Port is represented as a Part symbol in the diagram, and the Compartments smart manipulator activates on the Port. [ATTACHMENT filename='enabling_part_compartments.png']
3. Click the Compartments smart manipulator to open the list of properties and select properties you want to display in the Port shape.

To restore the usual representation of the Port, you must delete the Port from the diagram and add it again.

291194965

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space='MD2024xR2']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1af980ce-2a58-430e-8760-2189589de088"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">291194966</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e0a85032-b207-42df-833c-f90810504b5b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">291194968</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0a70b42b-f335-4d0a-b085-e46f57433565"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">291194967</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can display a Port shape as a Part and display compartments of the Port type in it. This means you can represent the inner structure or properties of the Block type in a Port shape.</p><p><br /></p><p>To enable Part compartments on the Port shape</p><hr /><ol><li>On the diagram pane, right-click the selected Port.</li><li>In the open shortcut menu, select <strong>Enable Part Compartment</strong>. The Port is represented as a Part symbol in the diagram, and the <em>Compartments </em>smart manipulator activates on the Port.<br /><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="192"><ri:attachment ri:filename="enabling_part_compartments.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Click the <em>Compartments </em>smart manipulator to open the list of properties and select properties you want to display in the Port shape.<br /><br /></li></ol><p>To restore the usual representation of the Port, you must delete the Port from the diagram and add it again.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="43936a9b-b0d8-4000-8f8c-657c1226fdcd"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">291194965</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Compartments" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189147447 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00134: Encapsulation of the Block

- page_id: `189147447`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189147447/Encapsulation+of+the+Block
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-02-11T09:49:12.016+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Validation > SysML validation rules > Validation rules of Blocks
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

Block[A]****

**Description**

This validation rule checks If **isEncapsulated**property value of a Block is set to *true*, then the Block is treated as a black box: a Part Property typed by this black box can only be connected via its ports or directly to its outer boundary.****

**Severity**

warning****

**Constrained Element**

Connector****

**Solvers**

To fix this, connect nested part that are typed by encapsulated Block via ports or outer boundary.

**Example**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### The Brake Block is encapsulated and nested Part Properties typed by it are connected via Proxy Ports.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Abbreviation</strong></p><p>Block[A]<strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>This validation rule checks If <strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="69f79cf1-05de-4c44-beb5-f1982924d439">isEncapsulated</ac:inline-comment-marker> </strong>property value of a Block is set to <em>true</em>, then the Block is treated as a black box: a Part Property typed by this black box can only be connected via its ports or directly to its outer boundary.<strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Severity</strong></p><p>warning<strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Constrained Element</strong></p><p>Connector<strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong>Solvers</strong></p><p>To fix this, connect nested part that are typed by encapsulated Block via ports or outer boundary.</p><p><strong>Example</strong></p><p><strong><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="isEncapsulated.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Encapsulation of the Block" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></strong></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The Brake Block is encapsulated and nested Part Properties typed by it are connected via Proxy Ports.</h6><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189146754 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00135: Enforce Ports Compatibility mode

- page_id: `189146754`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189146754/Enforce+Ports+Compatibility+mode
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2018-01-06T09:11:09.128+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Modeling structure with Blocks > Connecting Blocks in SysML Internal Block Diagram
- labels: ['compatible-connections', 'enforce-ports-compatibility']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Enforce Ports Compatibility** mode restricts connections and persists clearly visible only compatible ports ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Port' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Full Port' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']) in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Parameter' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Parametric Diagram' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']. Non compatible elements become transparent.

To use **Enforce Ports Compatibility** mode

1. Switch to Enforce Ports Compatibility mode.
2. Create the connection between ports ( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Port' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Full Port' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] ) or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Parameter' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] . All ports and Constraint Parameters that are compatible to connect persist clearly visible. [ATTACHMENT filename='enforce_ports_compatibility_mode.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Enforce Ports Compatibility</strong> mode restricts connections and persists clearly visible only compatible ports (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Flow Port" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Full Port" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Proxy Port" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ac:link>) in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Internal Block Diagram" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Constraint Parameter" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Parameters]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Parametric Diagram" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ac:link>. Non compatible elements become transparent.</p><p>To use <strong>Enforce Ports Compatibility</strong> mode</p><hr /><ol><li>Switch to<strong> Enforce Ports Compatibility</strong> mode.</li><li>Create the connection between ports (<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Flow Port" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Full Port" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Proxy Port" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ac:link>) or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Constraint Parameter" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Constraint Parameters]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br />All ports and <span class="confluence-link">Constraint Parameters</span> that are compatible to connect persist clearly visible.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Enforce Ports Compatibility mode" ac:alt="Enforce Ports Compatibility mode"><ri:attachment ri:filename="enforce_ports_compatibility_mode.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Enforce Ports Compatibility mode" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Glossary of SysML concepts > Element descriptions

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An Environmental Effect is an influence on the system from the environment without communicating with it directly.

#### TIP: Example

Example

- Temperature or Humidity.

#### PANEL: Related diagrams

Related diagrams

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Use Case Diagram' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189147206 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00138: Exporting tables to Excel or CSV files

- page_id: `189147206`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189147206/Exporting+tables+to+Excel+or+CSV+files
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T14:46:09.006+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Generating report documents
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can export any kind of tables to Excel or CSV format by:

43

##### Using the Export button

You can export the table to an *.html, *.csv, or *.xlsx file.

Before exporting a table, you can:

- Customize the template.html according to your needs. - The style of *.html tables is specified in template.html which can be found in <MagicDraw installation directory>\data\table.- The file stores cascading style sheets (CSS) that define the appearance of an exported table.
- Prepare a table by showing only particular data.

To export a table

1. On the Publish toolbar, click the Export button.
2. In the opened dialog, do the following:
  1. Browse for a location to save the table.
  2. Type the exported table name.
  3. Select the exported table format.
3. Click Save when you are done. The table is exported to the location you have specified.

Importing requirements from files that were exported using the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] **Export** option creates new elements instead of importing the existing ones. To avoid that, make sure to use [IMAGE alt='' src='']**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sync with Excel or CSV files' space='MD2024xR2']** instead.

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Basic tasks in tables' space='MD2024xR2']Learn more about basic tasks in tables >>

##### Using the Write To File command

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing Excel and CSV file' space='MD2024xR2']true

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sync with Excel or CSV files' space='MD2024xR2']Learn more how to sync your table with Excel or CSV files >>

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="confluence-link">You can export any kind of tables to Excel or CSV format by:</p><p class="confluence-link"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="88edaf6d-b897-40ac-a3fa-f32255a62594"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h3>Using the Export button</h3><p>You can export the table to an *.html, *.csv, or *.xlsx file.</p><p>Before exporting a table, you can:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Customize the template.html according to your needs.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5979e3d-c17e-4279-a6b9-0bcbab7e1e00"><ac:rich-text-body><p> - The style of *.html tables is specified in template.html which can be found in &lt;MagicDraw installation directory&gt;\data\table.</p><p> - The file stores cascading style sheets (CSS) that define the appearance of an exported table.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Prepare a table by showing only particular data.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To export a table</p><hr /><ol><li>On the Publish toolbar, click the <strong>Export</strong> button.</li><li>In the opened dialog, do the following:<ol><li>Browse for a location to save the table.</li><li>Type the exported table name.</li><li>Select the exported table format.</li></ol></li><li>Click <strong>Save</strong> when you are done.<br />The table is exported to the location you have specified.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ed6a97d-01a2-46ee-a52b-242aa94398cc"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Importing requirements from files that were exported using the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="gt_export.png" /></ac:image> <strong>Export</strong> <span>option creates new elements instead of importing the existing ones</span>. To avoid that, make sure to use <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="20"><ri:attachment ri:filename="excel_csv_sync.png" /></ac:image><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Sync with Excel or CSV files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Excel/CSV Sync]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> instead. </span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3808c95b-dc87-428c-a711-82f04123ad58"><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Basic tasks in tables" /><ac:link-body>Learn more about <span class="current">basic tasks in tables</span> &gt;&gt;</ac:link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Using the Write To File command</h3><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt-include" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae7c6671-efd4-4780-8209-360a194d7d0e"><ac:parameter ac:name=""><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Managing Excel and CSV file" /></ac:link></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="nopanel">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="de13f188-7435-4167-b908-940647b3a212"><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Sync with Excel or CSV files" /><ac:link-body>Learn more how to <span class="current">sync your table with Excel or CSV files &gt;&gt;</span></ac:link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189147821 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00139: Exporting to External Simulation Models

- page_id: `189147821`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189147821/Exporting+to+External+Simulation+Models
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-09-30T09:28:43.587+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

367932760

367932781

367932770

##### Introduction

Systems engineers coordinate the work of multiple other engineering disciplines (mechanical, material, electrical, control, and so on), requiring information to flow between systems engineers and those in other disciplines. Systems engineering information intentionally does not cover all disciplines, but must integrate with them to enable systems engineers to communicate with other engineers. Using discipline-specific tools separately from system modeling tools typically leads to redundancy, inconsistency, and less efficient engineering processes.

Graphical interfaces presented by physical interaction and signal flow simulators express concepts similar to the Systems Modeling Language (SysML), an extension of the Unified Modeling Language (UML). Both languages show system components, how components are connected together, and how physical substances and information flow between components. SysML and these simulators both have underlying textual languages to record models in computer-processable file formats. Simulators translate models specified through graphical interfaces into file-based formats, which are then transformed into equations for solution by numerical analysis. SysML-based tools use their filed-based formats to perform other kinds of analysis and verification, checking completeness of designs against requirements.

When SysML tools and physical interaction and signal flow simulators are used separately, simulation engineers must re-specify their systems in each tool they are using, including information that is also available in SysML models.

##### **OMG SysPhS standard implementation**

A new [OMG SysPhS specification](https://www.omg.org/spec/SysPhS/About-SysPhS/) (SysML Extension for Physical Interaction and Signal Flow Simulation) defines SysML elements mapping into Modelica and Simulink (incl. Stateflow, Simscape) models.

It is implemented as one-way transformation of selected SysML blocks and supports both black-box or/and full implementation which includes parametrics, state machines, internal structures with ports, interfaces and connectors.

Pure non-annotated SysML models can be exported, using SysPhS profile or libraries is not required for basic cases as blackbox structures.

Exported models and diagrams are ready to be opened in Simulink or Modelica tools for further implementation refinements, debugging, simulation and code generation.

367932758

**Related pages**

****

Systems engineering information intentionally does not cover all disciplines, but must integrate with them to enable systems engineers to communicate with other engineers. Using discipline-specific tools separately from system modeling tools typically leads to redundancy, inconsistency, and less efficient engineering processes.</p><p class="Default">Graphical interfaces presented by physical interaction and signal flow simulators express concepts similar to the Systems Modeling Language (SysML), an extension of the Unified Modeling Language (UML). Both languages show system components, how components are connected together, and how physical substances and information flow between components. SysML and these simulators both have underlying textual languages to record models in computer-processable file formats. Simulators translate models specified through graphical interfaces into file-based formats, which are then transformed into equations for solution by numerical analysis. SysML-based tools use their filed-based formats to perform other kinds of analysis and verification, checking completeness of designs against requirements.</p><p class="Default">When SysML tools and physical interaction and signal flow simulators are used separately, simulation engineers must re-specify their systems in each tool they are using, including information that is also available in SysML models.</p><h3><strong>OMG SysPhS standard implementation</strong></h3><p>A new <a href="https://www.omg.org/spec/SysPhS/About-SysPhS/">OMG SysPhS specification</a> (SysML Extension for Physical Interaction and Signal Flow Simulation) defines SysML elements mapping into Modelica and Simulink (incl. Stateflow, Simscape) models.</p><p>It is implemented as one-way transformation of selected SysML blocks and supports both black-box or/and full implementation which includes parametrics, state machines, internal structures with ports, interfaces and connectors.</p><p>Pure non-annotated SysML models can be exported, using SysPhS profile or libraries is not required for basic cases as blackbox structures.</p><p>Exported models and diagrams are ready to be opened in Simulink or Modelica tools for further implementation refinements, debugging, simulation and code generation.</p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fff9483a-4e54-4d53-bdc4-016b56ea6ec7"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">367932758</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="44bf06a2-d87c-48d6-bfae-ce39d0dbe51f" /><strong><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00140: Expose

- page_id: `189147651`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189147651/Expose
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2016-04-08T11:27:24.271+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Glossary of SysML concepts > Element descriptions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Expose relationship relates a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='View' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] to one or more model elements. Each model element is an access point to initiate the query. The View and the model elements related to the View are passed to the constructor when it is invoked. The method describes how the exposed elements are navigated to extract the desired information.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related diagram

Related diagram

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Views and Viewpoints Diagram' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189148022 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00142: Extending models with Comments

- page_id: `189148022`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189148022/Extending+models+with+Comments
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-02-20T10:52:32.121+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > Exporting to External Simulation Models > Modelica export > Advanced Modelica features
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can extend the model using a Comment element with «ModelicaExtension» stereotype. The Modelica Extension comment can be owned by the element it is attached to or have a different owner. The Modelica Extension content is exported to the Modelica file together with the model. The Modelica code is generated depending on the owner of the Modelica Extension comment.

There are several cases of the model extension with Modelica Extension comments and how the content of the comments is exported:

- When the comment is attached to a property which is owned by the Block. In the generated code, the Block is added as a model and the properties and content from the Modelica Extension comments are added in the same order as it was in the model. [ATTACHMENT filename='modelica_extension_to_block.png']
- When the comment is attached to a Block. In the generated code, the content from the comment, attached to the Block is added first, and the content from the comments, attached to the Block's properties, is added afterwards. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- When the comment is attached to an Instance. In this case, the Modelica code is generated the same way as for Block. You can attach the Modelica Extension comment to the nested instances. However, only first two levels of the nested instances are supported.The inserted comment must be plain text.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

The Modelica Extension content is exported to the Modelica file together with the model. The Modelica code is generated depending on the owner of the Modelica Extension comment.</p><p><br />There are several cases of the model extension with Modelica Extension comments and how the content of the comments is exported:</p><ul><li>When the comment is attached to a property which is owned by the Block. In the generated code, the Block is added as a model and the properties and content from the Modelica Extension comments are added in the same order as it was in the model. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="modelica_extension_to_block.png" /></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">When the comment is attached to a Block. In the generated code, the content from the comment, attached to the Block is added first, and the content from the comments, attached to the Block's properties, is added afterwards.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="modelica_extension_to_block_and_properties.png" /></ac:image></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">When the comment is attached to an Instance. In this case, the Modelica code is generated the same way as for Block.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d85a7051-8a6a-45d2-ad8e-026620a7b6b5"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>You can attach the Modelica Extension comment to the nested instances. However, only first two levels <span>of the nested instances are supported</span>.</li><li>The inserted comment must be plain text.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /><br /></p>
````

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Visibility representation

299748365

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Visibility representation

299748368

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Visibility representation

299748366

An External block is a block that represents an actor. It facilitates a more detailed modeling of actors like ports or internal structures.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Visibility representation

Visibility representation

In SysML, properties and operations of the Block are public. Visibility representation literals, like +, #, ~, -, are not displayed in the Containment tree or in the element symbol on a diagram.

299748364

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189146870 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00145: Extract Structure Wizard

- page_id: `189146870`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189146870/Extract+Structure+Wizard
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T14:46:04.457+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Modeling structure with Blocks
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1349885821

1349885823

1349896321

**On this page**

33

1349885822

##### Introduction

The extract structure feature allows you to select a portion of an existing system structure and transform it into another reusable [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space=''] (or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Subsystem' space='']) which may then be used as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space=''] in many other structures. You can also play a 'move' or a 'decompose' role when a structure becomes too complex and requires to be decomposed into several smaller reusable parts. Recursive decomposition of structure and behavior is an important aspect of the iterative development process. This feature is particularly useful for the automotive, aerospace, and defense communities for modeling complex systems-of-systems and building reusable components.

You can extract your structure by using the **Extract Structure Wizard**. It allows you to extract selected [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space=''] of an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space=''] to a separate diagram. Using this wizard, you can:

- Create and specify the target diagram that you want to create as the result of the extraction. The selected part is extracted to the newly created diagram.
- Select the ports that you want to create in a new diagram. Ports are listed for each intersected connector from the source diagram.
- Specify a referencing element (in this case, a property) that is created in the source Internal Block Diagram and represents the elements moved to the target diagram during the extraction.

##### The **Extract Structure Wizard** areas

The **Extract Structure Wizard** consists of the following areas:

- Steps area: displays all steps of the wizard and shows the current step you are working on.
- Options area: allows you to select and specify required options.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Using the Extract Structure Wizard

****

extracting_structureTo extract structure

1. In the SysML Internal Block Diagram or a structure compartment, select Part Properties that you want to extract.
2. Right-click selected parts and from the shortcut menu select Refactor > Extract .
3. Follow the steps of the Extract Structure Wizard . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Steps of the Extract Structure Wizard' space='']How to work in each step of the **Extract Structure Wizard** >>
4. Click Finish when you are done. Selected Part Properties are decomposed: another reusable Block (or Subsystem) and Internal Block Diagram are created (see the figure below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The selected Part Properties in the Climate Control IBD are extracted. As a result the new *climate System* Block and the SysML Internal Block Diagram are created.

1349885820

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Steps of the Extract Structure Wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Refactoring' space='MD2024xR2']
- Refactoring model elements
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Decomposing Blocks' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Internal Block Diagram' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='']

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the **climate control system** sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:

- Download *[ATTACHMENT filename='climate control system.mdzip'].*
- Find in modeling tool *<modeling tool installation directory>\samples\SysML\climate control system.mdzip.*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="abf3cbfd-b4ce-4f9b-8112-22e2ee6fe77e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1349885821</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d372ca16-39ff-4b82-ba82-fcc0ee8422f6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1349885823</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3bfcb9be-b0b0-4a9e-8719-9d04019f775e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1349896321</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1faaa24-4422-4ea5-964b-38c1f62defdc"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5ffde26b-5176-44fe-a836-51bc4f978e38"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1349885822</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3>Introduction</h3><p>The extract structure feature allows you to select a portion of an existing system structure and transform it into another reusable <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Block" /></ac:link> (or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Subsystem" /></ac:link>) which may then be used as <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[parts]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> in many other structures. You can also play a 'move' or a 'decompose' role when a structure becomes too complex and requires to be decomposed into several smaller reusable parts. Recursive decomposition of structure and behavior is an important aspect of the iterative development process. This feature is particularly useful for the automotive, aerospace, and defense communities for modeling complex systems-of-systems and building reusable components.</p><p>You can extract your structure by using the <strong>Extract Structure Wizard</strong>. It allows you to extract selected <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Part Properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of an <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Internal Block Diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Internal Block Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to a separate diagram. Using this wizard, you can:</p><ul><li>Create and specify the target diagram that you want to create as the result of the extraction. The selected part is extracted to the newly created diagram.</li><li>Select the ports that you want to create in a new diagram. Ports are listed for each intersected connector from the source diagram.</li><li>Specify a referencing element (in this case, a property) that is created in the source  Internal Block Diagram and represents the elements moved to the target diagram during the extraction.</li></ul><h3>The <strong>Extract Structure Wizard</strong> areas</h3><p>The <strong>Extract Structure Wizard</strong> consists of the following areas:</p><ul><li><em>Steps area:</em> displays all steps of the wizard and shows the current step you are working on.</li><li><em>Options area:</em> allows you to <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">select and specify required options.</span></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="extract_structure_wizard_areas.png" /></ac:image></p><h3>Using the Extract Structure Wizard</h3><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="920aba4c-545d-4436-a9e1-efaa609cb3d6"><ac:parameter ac:name="">extracting_structure</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To extract structure</p><hr /><ol><li>In the SysML Internal Block Diagram or a structure compartment, select Part Properties that you want to extract.</li><li>Right-click selected parts and from the shortcut menu select <strong>Refactor</strong> &gt; <strong>Extract</strong>.</li><li>Follow the steps of the <strong>Extract Structure Wizard</strong>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Steps of the Extract Structure Wizard" /><ac:link-body>How to work in each step of the <strong>Extract Structure Wizard</strong> &gt;&gt;</ac:link-body></ac:link></li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong> when you are done.<br />Selected Part Properties are decomposed: another reusable Block (or Subsystem) and Internal Block Diagram are created (see the figure below).</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Extract.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The selected Part Properties in the Climate Control IBD are extracted. As a result the new <em>climate System</em> Block and the SysML Internal Block Diagram are created.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="11876183-fd66-4387-a1f6-b3a093a0bbd2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1349885820</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_equal"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Steps of the Extract Structure Wizard" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024xR2" ri:content-title="Refactoring" /></ac:link></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/Refactoring+model+elements">Refactoring model elements</a></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Decomposing Blocks" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Internal Block Diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Block" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Part Property" /></ac:link></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The model used in the figures of this page is the <strong>climate control system</strong> sample model. To open this sample do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Download<span class="confluence-link"><em> <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="climate control system.mdzip"><ri:page ri:content-title="_sample models" /></ri:attachment></ac:link>.</em></span></li><li><span class="confluence-link">Find in modeling tool <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\samples\SysML\climate control system.mdzip.</em></span></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189147657 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00147: Flow Port

- page_id: `189147657`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189147657/Flow+Port
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2017-10-18T12:43:30.468+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Glossary of SysML concepts > Element descriptions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

313960321

313960324

313960322

Flow Port is deprecated in accordance with SysML specification and is defined for backward compatibility. The functionality of non-atomic Flow Ports is supported with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] typed by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interface Block' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] owning [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Flow Property' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']. Flow Properties are not deprecated. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining interfaces using ports' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']>]]>

A Flow Port is a port that specifies the input and output items that can flow between a Block and its environment. Flow Ports are interactions points through which data, material, or energy “can” enter or leave the owning Block. The specification of what can flow is achieved by typing the Flow Port with a specification of things that flow. This can include typing an atomic Flow Port with a single type (Block, Value Type, or Signal) representing the items that flow in or out, or typing a non-atomic Flow Port with a Flow Specification which lists multiple items that can flow. In general, Flow Ports are intended to be used for asynchronous, broadcast, or send-and-forget interactions. Note that only non-atomic Flow Ports can be conjugated. Once conjugated, all the directions of the typing Flow Specification's items are negated.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

313960320

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Hiding direction arrow on port shape' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']

Flow Properties are not deprecated. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Defining interfaces using ports" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn how to define interfaces >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">A Flow Port is a port that specifies the input and output items that can flow between a Block and its environment. Flow Ports are interactions points through which data, material, or energy “can” enter or leave the owning Block. The specification of what can flow is achieved by typing the Flow Port with a specification of things that flow. This can include typing an atomic Flow Port with a single type (Block, Value Type, or Signal) representing the items that flow in or out, or typing a non-atomic Flow Port with a Flow Specification which lists multiple items that can flow. In general, Flow Ports are intended to be used for asynchronous, broadcast, or send-and-forget interactions. Note that only non-atomic Flow Ports can be conjugated. Once conjugated, all the directions of the typing Flow Specification's items are negated.</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="flow_ports.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="Flow Port" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0d4281b6-3afd-4c77-82c3-e83a5a8f1808"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">313960320</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Hiding direction arrow on port shape" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ac:link></li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Internal Block Diagram" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ac:link></span></li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

A Flow Property signifies a single flow element to/from a block with a direction prefix (in, out, inout). Flow properties are listed in a compartment labeled *flow properties*.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189147659 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00149: Flow Specification

- page_id: `189147659`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189147659/Flow+Specification
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2016-03-15T10:42:07.863+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Glossary of SysML concepts > Element descriptions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Flow Specification specifies inputs and outputs as a set of flow properties that list can be displayed in a **flow properties** compartment. A flow specification is used to type Flow Ports, in order to specify items which can flow via the ports.

Flow Specification is deprecated in accordance with SysML specification and is defined for backward compatibility.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### PANEL: Related diagrams

Related diagrams

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Block Definition Diagram' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189147773 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00150: FMU import

- page_id: `189147773`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189147773/FMU+import
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T14:46:20.077+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > Importing External Simulation Models
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1171031961

INLINE

1171031973

INLINE

1171031960

INLINE

**On this page**

**42**

1171031962

INLINE

##### **What is FMI?**

FMI, also known as a Functional Mock-up Interface, is a tool independent open-source standard that supports both model exchange and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Simulation of SysML models' space='CST190SP3'] of dynamic models in a standardized format. The modeling tool supports FMI versions 1.0 and 2.0.

##### What is FMU?

A component that implements FMI is called FMU (Functional Mock-up Unit). It stores a simulation model that adheres to the FMI standard. To be more specific, the FMU file contains an XML description file and implementation in a binary form.

##### FMU import

The **FMU Import Options** dialog enables users to customize the FMU file import into the model. For instance, you can specify whether parameters from the FMU file should be imported as ports or value properties. Additionally, you can change the name of the Block (the FMU model name is used by default) and automatically attach the file to the project upon import.

The **FMU Import Options** dialog has the following options:

| Option name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Direction | The direction of the port: In or Out. Inputs and Outputs are selected to be imported as ports by default. |
| Name | The name of the property/parameter. |
| Type | The type of the property, for example, value property. |
| Description | The textual description of the property. |
| As Port | Click to select whether properties should be represented as Flow or Proxy ports. Select None to import the selected properties as value properties. |
| Interface Block | Click to select a compatible interface Block or create a new one. <NEW> is selected by default.The Interface Block column is active only if Proxy Port is selected in the As Port column. |
| Redefine | Click to redefine inherited value or part properties. |
|  | Click to select all listed properties at once. |
|  | Click to deselect all listed properties at once. |
|  | If the checkbox next to Attach file to the project is selected, the imported FMU file is automatically attached to the model. |
|  | The name of the Block created in the model upon import. By default, the name of the FMU model is used, but you can set the one you like. |

To import the FMU file into the model

1. In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click File > Import From > FMU File . [ATTACHMENT filename='fmu_file_import.png']
2. Select the FMU file on your file system and click Open . [ATTACHMENT filename='open_selected_fmu_file.png']
3. In the **FMU Import Options** dialog, specify which properties from the FMU file should be imported into your model as values and ports. Click **OK**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Dragging and dropping FMU on diagram

Alternatively, you can import the FMU model by dragging and dropping the selected FMU file directly onto the Block Definition (BDD) and/or Internal Block (IBD) diagrams.

To import the FMU file into the model using BDD/IBD

1. Locate the FMU file on your file system.
2. Do either:
  - Drag and drop the file from your file system onto the BDD diagram. A Block with the applied «FMU» stereotype is created in the model after customizing FMU import via the FMU Import Options dialog. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_fmu_block_bdd.png']
  - Drag and drop the file from your file system onto the IBD diagram. A Part property is created in the model after customizing FMU import via the FMU Import Options dialog. [ATTACHMENT filename='creating_fmu_block_ibd.png']

###### Dragging and dropping FMU on existing Block

You can drag and drop the FMU file directly onto the Block as implementation as well. Once the FMU file is dropped, the**FMU Import Options** dialog that allows redefining value and part properties of the Block opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Redefining properties using FMU Import Options dialog

In this case, a new Block is created as a subtype of the existing Block together with the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generalization' space='MD190SP3'] relationship.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Dropping FMU on an existing Block

**In/Out properties** in the **FMU Import Options** dialog are by default imported into the model as ports of a Flow type.

###### Updating existing FMU Block

An existing FMU Block can be updated by dragging and dropping a FMU file directly on it. This is useful in those cases when there is a need to quickly import properties that were, initially, left out.

To update a FMU Block

1. Locate the FMU file on your file system.
2. Drag and drop it on the shape of your FMU Block.
3. In the opened FMU Import Options dialog, select the properties to update the FMU Block with.
4. Click OK .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Dragging and dropping FMU on the existing FMU Block

Alternatively, you can update the FMU Block directly from the shortcut menu.

To update a FMU Block from the shortcut menu

1. Right-click the Simulink Block.
2. Select Tools > Update from FMU file from the shortcut menu. [ATTACHMENT filename='updating_from_fmu.png']
3. In the opened FMU Import Options dialog, select the properties to update the FMU Block with. [ATTACHMENT filename='updating_fmu.png']
4. Click OK .

Already imported properties are grayed out and cannot be edited in the **FMU Import Options**dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If the **Attach file to the project** is checked in the **FMU Import Options dialog**, a previous version of the attached file is replaced with a new one upon import.

##### Co-Simulation

The FMU file can be simulated using [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation' space='CST2024xR2'] (CST). To learn more, please click [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Simulation of SysML models' space='CST2024xR2'].

2129431801

INLINE

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Simulation of SysML models' space='CST190SP3']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3494ea3a-2c83-4f27-8088-930d32310e46"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1171031961</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b3fa975e-002f-4bf4-9667-4b6f17fe0812"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1171031973</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9939e2f6-82f2-4363-a7c2-4ea6bda7670a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1171031960</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c9baf3ea-904c-49ea-ac2d-6cbc3b6ad485"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">2</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ff3b367-29a4-4bba-99be-945fa6fea563"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1171031962</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h3><strong>What is FMI?</strong></h3><p>FMI, also known as a Functional Mock-up Interface, is a tool independent open-source standard that supports both model exchange and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST190SP3" ri:content-title="Simulation of SysML models" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[co-simulation]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of dynamic models in a standardized format. The modeling tool supports FMI versions 1.0 and 2.0. </p><h3>What is FMU?</h3><p>A component that implements FMI is called FMU (Functional Mock-up Unit). It stores a simulation model that adheres to the FMI standard. To be more specific, the FMU file contains an XML description file and implementation in a binary form. </p><h3>FMU import</h3><p>The <strong>FMU Import Options</strong> dialog enables users to customize the FMU file import into the model. For instance, you can specify whether parameters from the FMU file should be imported as ports or value properties. Additionally, you can change the name of the Block (the FMU model name is used by default) and automatically attach the file to the project upon import. </p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>The <strong>FMU Import Options</strong> dialog has the following options:</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 68.2727%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 29.2712%;" /><col style="width: 70.6837%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Option name</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Direction</strong></td><td>The direction of the port: <em>In</em> or <em>Out</em>. <em>Inputs</em> and <em>Outputs </em>are selected to be imported as ports by default.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Name</strong></td><td>The name of the property/parameter.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Type</strong></td><td>The type of the property, for example, value property.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Description</strong></td><td>The textual description of the property.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>As Port</strong></td><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click to select whether properties should be represented as Flow or Proxy ports. Select None to import the selected properties as value properties.</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Interface Block</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click to select a compatible interface Block or create a new one. <span>&lt;NEW&gt; is selected by default.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01905682-2985-4a08-b900-bef8b85c3585"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Interface Block column is active only if <strong>Proxy Port</strong> is selected in the <strong>As Port</strong> column.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Redefine</strong></td><td colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click to redefine inherited value or part properties.</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_all.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="FMU import" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1">Click to select all listed properties at once.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="24"><ri:attachment ri:filename="select_none.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="FMU import" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1">Click to deselect all listed properties at once.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="25"><ri:attachment ri:filename="attach_file_to_the_project.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="FMU import" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1">If the checkbox next to <strong>Attach file to the project</strong> is selected, the imported FMU file is automatically attached to the model.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="block_name.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="FMU import" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1">The name of the Block created in the model upon import. By default, the name of the FMU model is used, but you can set the one you like.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>To import the FMU file into the model</p><hr /><ol><li>In the top-left corner of the modeling tool, click <strong>File</strong> &gt;<strong> Import From </strong>&gt; <strong>FMU File</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="fmu_file_import.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="FMU import" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Select the FMU file on your file system and click <strong>Open</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="open_selected_fmu_file.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="FMU import" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><p>In the <strong>FMU Import Options</strong> dialog, specify which properties from the FMU file should be imported into your model as values and ports. Click <strong>OK</strong>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specifying_fmu_properties.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="FMU import" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><h4>Dragging and dropping FMU on diagram</h4><p>Alternatively, you can import the FMU model by dragging and dropping the selected FMU file directly onto the Block Definition (BDD) and/or Internal Block (IBD) diagrams.</p><p><br /></p><p>To import the FMU file into the model using BDD/IBD</p><hr /><ol><li>Locate the <strong>FMU file</strong> on your file system. </li><li>Do either:<br /><ul><li>Drag and drop the file from your file system onto the BDD diagram. A Block with the applied «FMU»  stereotype is created in the model after customizing FMU import via the<strong> FMU Import Options</strong> dialog.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_fmu_block_bdd.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="FMU import" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Drag and drop the file from your file system onto the IBD diagram. A Part property is created in the model after customizing FMU import via the<strong> FMU Import Options</strong> dialog.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="creating_fmu_block_ibd.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="FMU import" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></li></ol><h4>Dragging and dropping FMU on existing Block</h4><p>You can drag and drop the FMU file directly onto the Block as implementation as well. Once the FMU file is dropped, the<strong> FMU Import Options</strong> dialog that allows redefining value and part properties of the Block opens.</p><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(94,108,132);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="redefining_fmu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="FMU import" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(94,108,132);">Redefining properties using FMU Import Options dialog</span></h6><p>In this case, a new Block is created as a subtype of the existing Block together with the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD190SP3" ri:content-title="Generalization" /></ac:link> relationship. </p><p><br /></p><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="fmu_implementation.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="FMU import" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Dropping FMU on an existing Block</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4836eded-870c-4976-bba3-1c26597c5727"><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>In/Out properties</strong> in the <strong>FMU Import Options</strong> dialog are by default imported into the model as ports of a Flow type.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Updating existing FMU Block</h4><p>An existing FMU Block can be updated by dragging and dropping a FMU file directly on it. This is useful in those cases when there is a need to quickly import properties that were, initially, left out. </p><p>To update a FMU Block</p><hr /><ol><li>Locate the FMU file on your file system.</li><li>Drag and drop it on the shape of your FMU Block.</li><li>In the opened <strong>FMU Import Options</strong> dialog, select the properties to update the FMU Block with.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="updating_existing_fmu_block.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="FMU import" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Dragging and dropping FMU on the existing FMU Block</h6><p>Alternatively, you can update the FMU Block directly from the shortcut menu. </p><p><br /></p><p>To update a FMU Block from the shortcut menu</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Simulink Block. </li><li>Select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt;<strong> Update from FMU</strong> <strong>file </strong>from the shortcut menu.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="updating_from_fmu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="FMU import" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>In the opened <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">FMU Import Options</strong> dialog, select the properties to update the FMU Block with.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="updating_fmu.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="FMU import" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b627fb5d-7372-4afc-9f42-465555b849ba"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Already imported properties are grayed out and cannot be edited in the <strong>FMU Import Options</strong><span> dialog.</span></p><p><span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="greyed_out_properties.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="FMU import" ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ac48cca1-eeec-4efb-8aab-1a26cb98b185"><ac:rich-text-body><p> If the <strong>Attach file to the project</strong> is checked in the <strong>FMU Import Options dialog</strong>, a previous version of the attached file is replaced with a new one upon import.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Co-Simulation</h3><p>The FMU file can be simulated using <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024xR2" ri:content-title="Cameo Simulation Toolkit Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Cameo Simulation Toolkit]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> (CST). To learn more, please click <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST2024xR2" ri:content-title="Simulation of SysML models" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[here]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. <br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e40a5784-0013-4dcb-85f2-6937ad35bf76"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2129431801</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CST190SP3" ri:content-title="Simulation of SysML models" /></ac:link><strong><br class="_mce_tagged_br" /></strong></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00151: Full Port

- page_id: `189147662`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`

- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Glossary of SysML concepts > Element descriptions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

314864701

314864703

314864702

A Full Port is a port which is considered as a separated element of owning Blocks. It may have internal parts or behaviors that support interactions with owning Blocks.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

314864700

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189147031 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00153: Functional analysis

- page_id: `189147031`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189147031/Functional+analysis
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-01-29T10:28:59.901+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Functional analysis is necessary for a user to understand in detail how a system works or should behave. Functional analysis is a continuation of Use Case scenario refinements, using diagrams describing the behavior of a system, e.g., SysML Activity, SysML Sequences, or SysML State Machine diagrams. A new SysML Activity diagram should be created for every function of a Use Case allocated to a particular block or part. Although there are two or even more Swimlane partitions, you should choose only those functions that are nested under the partition representing the Block which captures your system.

The following chapters describe each feature's functionality.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Functional analysis is necessary for a user to understand in detail how a system works or should behave. <span>Functional analysis is a continuation of Use Case scenario refinements, using diagrams describing the behavior of a system, e.g.,  <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e9ec4ca5-0256-4bda-ae4e-c09b1643a9c2">SysML Activity, SysML Sequences, or SysML State Machine diagrams</ac:inline-comment-marker>. A new SysML Activity diagram should be created for every function of a Use Case allocated to a particular block or part. Alt<span>hough there are two or even more <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6012bc6c-658a-4ec4-9287-2455da85980f">Swimlane</ac:inline-comment-marker> partitions, you should choose only those functions that are nested under the partition representing the Block which captures <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="89bf5843-7dd2-4454-bd1b-d6039794885f">your system</ac:inline-comment-marker></span><span>. </span></span></p><p><span>The following chapters describe each feature's functionality.</span></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="22934b67-10a8-40e3-b5c9-7c155401a4ec" /></p>
````

## PAGE 00155: FunctionalRequirement satisfied by an invalid element

- page_id: `189147504`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189147504/FunctionalRequirement+satisfied+by+an+invalid+element
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2021-01-13T12:09:06.855+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Validation > SysML validation rules > Validation rules of Non-normative Extensions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Abbreviation**

functionalRequirement[1]****

**Description**

This validation rulechecks if the Functional Requirement is satisfied by an Operation, Action, State or Behavior. ****

**Severity**

warning****

**Constrained Element**

Functional Requirement****

**Solvers**

- **Remove invalid Satisfy relationship(s)** - Deletes not valid Satisfy relationship from the model. Manual SolverReconnect existing or create new satisfy relationship from Operation, Action, State or Behavior to Functional Requirement.

**Example**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The*Break Functional Requirements*Functional Requirementis satisfied by a *Break***Activity.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189148053 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00158: Generating simulation files

- page_id: `189148053`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189148053/Generating+simulation+files
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2020-02-10T09:19:44.651+01:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > Exporting to External Simulation Models > Sample models > Creating a simple model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To generate a simulation file, right-click the *Block* corresponding the system being simulated (see the image below). The shortcut menu displays the menu item **Tools******that contains**Export to Modelica** and **Export to Simulink**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To export to Modelica and Simulink

1. Depending on the selected option (either Export to Modelica or Export to Simulink ), do one of the following:
  - Specify options such as output directory, how to export unannotated SysML properties, and whether diagram layout annotations should be generated on export. [ATTACHMENT filename='modelica_export_options_.png']
  - Specify options such as format, output directory, S-Function or Simscape, Simscape port libraries, and whether to apply the <<SimulinkBlock>> stereotype. [ATTACHMENT filename='simulink_export_new_dialog (1).png']
2. Click OK .

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189147204 space=SYSMLP2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00159: Generating Word document report

- page_id: `189147204`
- space_key: `SYSMLP2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/SYSMLP2024xR2/pages/189147204/Generating+Word+document+report
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-04T14:46:08.893+02:00`
- ancestors: SysML Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Generating report documents
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To create a .docx format report

1. In the Tools menu, click Report Wizard .
2. In the Select Template area, select SysML, and choose the report type. Click Next .
3. In the Select Report Data area, you can then select a predefined report data for the selected template (default = Built-in). Click Next .
4. In the Select Element Scope area use:
  - The Add button to add an element selected in the element tree to the Selected objects area.
  - The Add All button to add all elements directly owned by the element selected in the element tree to the Selected objects area.
  - The Add Recursively button in to add all elements listed under the element selected in the element tree to the Selected objects area.
  - The Remove button in to remove the selected element from the Selected objects area.
  - The Remove All button in to remove all selected elements from the Selected objects area.
5. Click Next .
6. In the Output Options area, define the appropriate options.
7. Click Generate . Your report is generated and automatically opens. See the example: [ATTACHMENT filename='coverage_analysis.docx'] .

#### INFO: Report customization

Report customization

If you want to customize your report, use the **[Report Wizard](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MD2024xR2/MagicDraw+Report+wizard+overview)**.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
